commits.subject: fix(export): cold-audit follow-up — partial-weight tolerance, fast tests, vendor split
commits.body:
Addresses every finding from the `cold` branch audit (8 items) plus the two
pre-existing concerns disclosed in that pass.

Findings 1–3 — fontEmbed correctness + observability:
- Promise.all → Promise.allSettled for woff2 fetches inside fetchInlinedCSS.
  One transient 503 on a single weight no longer drops the entire font;
  surviving weights inline as data URLs, the failed weight URL stays
  verbatim so the browser can retry at SVG-rasterize time.
- New onWarning callback on getEmbeddedFontCSS — invoked once per dropped
  weight or per failed font with a human-readable message. exportHelpers
  bridges to debugLog('export', 'font-embed-warning', ...).
- Removed the dead getFontEmbedCSSWithLogging wrapper. Its outer try/catch
  was catching impossible errors; per CLAUDE.md "Don't add error handling
  for scenarios that can't happen". Inlined call site uses the new callback.
- 3 new tests cover partial-weight failure, per-font onWarning, and the
  optional-callback path.

Finding 4 — test latency:
- Abort-timeout test now uses jest.useFakeTimers({ doNotFake: ['queueMicrotask'] })
  + advanceTimersByTime(8001). Test suite total: 8.47s → 1.09s.

Findings 5–7 — CLAUDE.md doc currency:
- Architecture utils/ block now lists fontEmbed.js + pwaCleanup.js with
  one-line descriptions matching the surrounding style.
- New AI Note "Font embedding for export" explains why we roll our own CSS
  pre-fetch instead of html-to-image's exported getFontEmbedCSS (which has
  the same internal getCSSRules SecurityError issue), why Promise.allSettled,
  and lists every companion change. Future sessions reading the file as the
  canonical map will find the rationale before re-walking the path.
- Layout link comments shrunk to one-liners pointing to the AI Note. The
  three near-identical 4-line comments in MobileLayout/DesktopLayout/
  ReaderMode are now consistent one-liners.

Finding 8 — vite.config doc gaps:
- runtimeCaching gets a top-level comment explaining why we keep SW caches
  for fonts despite the browser HTTP cache (offline support + PWA standalone
  context). Per-cache `maxEntries` numbers now have inline rationale
  (10 covers typical CSS pick rate; 30 covers ≈3 weights × N families).

Pre-existing concern — bundle size:
- manualChunks: vendor-react (140KB), vendor-pdf (429KB),
  vendor-export (113KB), vendor-text (65KB), index (349KB).
- Total bytes unchanged. App chunk dropped 1099KB → 349KB so app-only
  updates re-download ~1/4 of the previous bytes; vendor chunks stay cached
  for as long as their package.json version is unchanged.
- React.lazy(ExportButtons) explicitly rejected in the code comment —
  ExportButtons is always visible in the desktop sidebar, Suspense fallback
  on every cold load would be a real UX regression.

Pre-existing concern — runtime cache rationale:
- Documented inline in vite.config.js next to the runtimeCaching block.

TODO.md: pwaCleanup.js sunset entry added (target ~Oct 2026 once SW cache
rename has propagated to all installs).

Test count CLAUDE.md: 150 → 153.

commits.subject: chore: fresh-eyes branch polish after cold audit
commits.body:
Six small findings from the cold-review pass, none blocking:

- CLAUDE.md: the Architecture line for lib/auth.ts now mentions the
  temporary sancio bypass so a fresh reader sees it before hitting AI Notes.
- lib/cors.ts: brief inline comment explaining why X-Dev-User-Id /
  X-Dev-User-Email sit in the preflight allowlist and pointing at the
  removal path.
- api/__tests__/auth.test.ts: save and restore process.env around each
  test instead of leaving SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_ANON_KEY set after the
  suite runs. Vitest isolates workers so the risk was low, but hardening.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: expanded the removal-path step 3 run-on sentence
  into sub-bullets so flip-day operator can tick each off.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: dropped point-in-time facts that silently rot
  ("19 commits ahead," "95 tests passing") in favour of durable wording.
- src/App.tsx: typed the fetch-catch err as unknown and narrowed via
  `instanceof Error` before reading .name. Promise.catch's signature types
  the param as any so strict mode didn't flag the implicit any, but the
  explicit unknown + narrowing reads clearer and matches the pattern the
  rest of the repo uses for untrusted values.

Flagged but intentionally not fixed (documented in the cold pass):
- Preflight advertises dev headers to all allowed origins, not just
  sun-sea-o. Per-origin preflight logic adds code for zero functional
  benefit — no opt-in means no bypass regardless of what the preflight
  advertises.
- lib/auth.ts accepts any non-empty string as x-dev-user-id without UUID
  validation. Code is slated for deletion; downstream DB UUID parse
  failures are self-limiting for a dev tool.

commits.subject: chore(verify): JSDOM inline-script suite + DebugPill Rules-of-Hooks fix
commits.body:
Cold audit + genuine attempt at automating the live-browser gaps
I'd been flagging as unreachable from sandbox.

## DebugPill Rules-of-Hooks fix

Found during fresh-eyes read: the `if (typeof document ===
'undefined') return null` guard ran BEFORE any hook call. SSR and
CSR are different component instances so React doesn't actually
blow up on this, but it's a non-idiomatic pattern a linter will
flag. Moved the hooks above the early return; document-unavailable
branch now runs after all hook calls have occurred.

## `scripts/verify-inline-scripts.mjs` — automated verification

New JSDOM-based suite that runs the two critical inline scripts
(theme flash-prevention + pre-React debug pill) against a real DOM
with mocked localStorage + matchMedia, asserts their documented
side-effects on `<html>`, and validates the static DOM structure
of the built HTML.

Uses JSDOM's `runScripts: 'dangerously'` mode to execute inline
scripts naturally during DOM construction — same path a real
browser takes. 23 assertions across 8 scenarios:

1. No saved theme + prefers-dark → synthwave + `.dark` + data-theme
2. saved dark + combo=luxe → luxury + `.dark` + data-theme
3. saved light + combo=retro → cupcake + no `.dark`
4. invalid combo → falls back to retro/dark = synthwave
5. `__debugPreMountQueue` + `__debugPushError` wired up
6. `__debugPushError` queues + renders fallback DOM pill with
   HH:MM:SS.mmm-formatted timestamps
7. `<div id="debug-root">` present in built HTML
8. manifest + favicon.ico + apple-touch-icon + SVG icon links
   present in <head>

All 23 assertions pass. Run via `npm run verify:inline-scripts`.
Prerequisite: `npm run build:web` to produce the dist/ that the
suite reads from.

## Build verification

`npm run build:web` succeeds end-to-end against sandbox (required
seeding `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` / `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
from the `UTIL_` env vars per CLAUDE.md). Emits:
 - 13 route HTMLs, each with the theme flash script (with the
   stringified COMBO_THEME_MAP intact), pre-React debug pill
   script, #debug-root div, and all required link tags.
 - 4-icon manifest.json with distinct purpose values (3 any + 1
   maskable at 1024×1024).
 - public/favicon.ico, icon-1024-maskable.png, icon-512.png all
   bundled.
 - @custom-variant dark + color-scheme CSS rules present in the
   compiled CSS.

## JSDOM, not Playwright

Tried Playwright first — Chromium binary download is blocked by
sandbox network restrictions. JSDOM gets us 23 of the 22 listed
live-browser scenarios that are actually automatable without a
real paint/layout engine. The remaining scenarios (no-flash on
hard reload, mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, iOS pagehide/
pageshow, HMR single-attach, prod visibility live-load) genuinely
need either a real browser or a physical iOS device — still
tracked in docs/TODO.md § High Priority.

## Deps

 +jsdom ^29.0.2 (devDependency; ~12 MB) — enables verify suite
 -playwright (removed; binary download blocked anyway; JSDOM
  sufficient for these assertions)
 (+@types/react-dom ^19.2.3 from the prior debug-system commit —
  was needed for createRoot typings)

## Unused-locals scope check

Ran `tsc --noEmit --noUnusedLocals --noUnusedParameters`: 16
remaining unused-locals repo-wide, ZERO in files I touched on
this branch. Pre-branch baseline was 31 — this branch's refactor
cleaned up 15 of them as a side-effect. Not adjusting synctone's
tsconfig to enable these strict flags (out of scope).

Tags: verification, testing, debug, jsdom
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch

commits.subject: a11y(motion): boot-loader spinner respects prefers-reduced-motion
commits.body:
index.html hosts the pre-React loading spinner in an inline <style>
block that renders before Vite injects src/index.css. The app's
reduced-motion rules live in index.css, so the spinner kept spinning
through the boot window (~0.2-2s, longer on slow connections) even
when the user had reduced-motion enabled at the OS level.

Fix:
- Added `boot-spinner` class to the animating <div>.
- Extended the inline <style> block with a scoped reduced-motion rule:
  `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .boot-spinner{animation:
  none !important} }`.
- Left an explanatory comment so a future edit doesn't wonder why the
  keyframes live in the HTML rather than the CSS bundle.

Post-mount, index.css's global reduced-motion rules take over for all
DaisyUI + custom animations (already verified compliant in the motion
audit).

No test lock on the boot-loader markup; iconCacheBustHtml's literal
replacements target <link rel="icon|apple-touch-icon"> tags, not the
<style> block, so the plugin ran cleanly through build.

Tests 155/155, tsc clean, eslint clean, npm run build clean.

commits.subject: fix(copy): remove accounting jargon and clarify ambiguous labels
commits.body:
High-severity
1. "Import Actuals" → "Import transactions".
   "Actuals" is bookkeeping jargon non-technical users don't recognise.
   Fixed in ImportStepUpload.vue h1 and WorkspaceDetailView.vue import
   button title. The route name `import-actuals` is internal (URL path
   is /workspace/:id/import — clean) so left unchanged.
   Also caught by verification: CashflowGraph chart legend showed
   "Actuals" as the series name — changed to "Spending" (+ updated
   the colour-match switch and the screen-reader summary string).

Medium-severity
2. "Continue to classify" → "Review & import".
   Next step title is "Review transactions" — old copy told users they
   were going somewhere else ("classify"). New copy matches the wizard
   step labels + states the final action in the button.
3. Import save-error clarified.
   "Couldn't save imported data. Please try again." →
   "Couldn't save your transactions. Your review is still loaded —
    please try again." The added clause tells the user their work
   isn't lost on retry, which the old message left ambiguous.
4. "Mark all unmatched as once-off" → "Mark remaining as one-time".
   "Unmatched" leaked an implementation detail (didn't match a
   pattern). "Remaining" + "one-time" matches the existing user
   vocabulary in the classification badges.
5. Pattern-matching dev-speak swept from ImportStepReview progress UI.
   "Preparing tag suggestions and pattern matching..." →
   "Preparing tag suggestions and recurring detection…"
   Progress bar label "Pattern matching" → "Recurring detection".
   Error states updated consistently ("Recurring detection unavailable",
   "Smart detection unavailable — you can still review manually").

Dropped finding: agent flagged ErrorBoundary's "Something went wrong"
as inconsistent with the "Couldn't…" voice. Verified — the full
copy is actually fine: title + reassurance ("Your data is safe") +
clear next action. Title is conventional for an error boundary.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: "Spending" replacing "Actuals" in the chart
legend should be confirmed visually — worth checking the colour
mapping and tooltip still render correctly.

commits.subject: fix(export): tighten font-embed approach — tests, timeout, observability, cleanup
commits.body:
Follow-up to 8312157 addressing the seven self-review findings (`approach`):

1. Tests for fontEmbed (9 cases, 1 file): empty/unknown IDs, happy-path
   inlining, per-URL cache, inflight dedup, partial-failure tolerance,
   non-200 handling, relative URL resolution, abort timeout. Mocks fetch +
   FileReader; mocks the .ts fonts config (Jest can't parse TS in this repo).

2. AbortController timeout on every fontEmbed fetch (8s). A hung gstatic.com
   response can no longer freeze the export indefinitely — it falls back to
   system fonts after the timeout.

3. Resolve relative url(...) refs against the CSS document URL via new URL().
   Google Fonts uses absolute https today, but a future format change or
   non-Google CDN with relative refs would have silently broken inlining.

4. exportHelpers logs fontEmbed failures via debugLog instead of swallowing
   them silently. Silent degradation in the field is what produced the
   original CORS noise — surfacing it preserves observability.

5. Removed the dead noise filter from debugLog. With fontEmbedCSS provided,
   html-to-image short-circuits before getCSSRules runs (verified in
   embed-webfonts.js:188-192), so the four filtered patterns can't fire from
   the export path. Defensive-suppressing patterns we no longer trigger
   means a future genuine regression would be hidden.

6. New utils/pwaCleanup.js fires caches.delete for the old `google-fonts-cache`
   and `gstatic-fonts-cache` names on app load. Idempotent. Reclaims storage
   from existing PWA installs that were holding ~1y of opaque entries before
   the *-v2 rename.

7. Inlined the comment justifications on the four <link> tags
   (index.html + MobileLayout/DesktopLayout/ReaderMode). The previous
   "see index.html note" cross-references would rot if the index.html
   comment moved.

Test count CLAUDE.md/TODO.md: 141 → 150.

commits.subject: a11y(mobile): apply 44x44 touch-target fixes + codify -sm pairing convention
commits.body:
All findings from the mobile trigger's WCAG 2.5.5 audit — every one is
a compact DaisyUI variant (`btn-sm` / `input-sm`) that was rendering
below 44px touch-target without the matching `min-h-[44px]` override.

Size fixes (6):

- NudgeExpiryConfig nudge-days number input (HIGH) — `w-24` config
  input, primary interaction surface on the per-module nudge config.
- ModuleSummaryEditor text inputs (HIGH) — three summary fields
  ("what it does" / "when it matters" / "if not followed") rendered
  via the shared `SummaryField` component; adding min-h there fixes
  all three.
- ToneSelector custom-tone input (HIGH) — the `input-sm flex-1` users
  type custom tones/intents into. The field is used in every note +
  proposal composition flow.
- ToneSelector Add button (MEDIUM) — `btn btn-sm` next to the custom
  input. Now `min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]` matching the NotificationBell
  + BurgerMenu trigger pattern.
- ContractUploadPreview section rename input (MEDIUM) — the inline
  rename that appears when you click a section title during upload
  preview. Less frequently used but critical when active.
- AppLayout breadcrumb links (MEDIUM) — 4px short at `min-h-[40px]`,
  bumped to `min-h-[44px]`. Mobile breadcrumb taps happen all the
  time during navigation.

Convention (1):

- CLAUDE.md "User Experience (Non-Negotiable)" — added two bullets
  codifying the touch-target rule + the DaisyUI compact-variant
  pairing convention. Also added a companion bullet on iOS
  auto-zoom prevention (text-base / 16px) since the audit verified
  Input/TextArea already enforce this and it's worth documenting so
  future ad-hoc inputs don't regress. Future mobile audits should
  have nothing to flag beyond new code that breaks the rule.

Deferred (1):

- `checkbox-sm` elements surrounded by flex-row text labels — the
  audit called these "acceptable via adjacency" pending real-device
  testing. Leaving until that testing disputes it; the convention
  codified today applies to checkboxes too if it ever does.

Verified untouched by the fixes: viewport meta, safe-area insets,
tap-delay, gesture handlers (none), prefers-reduced-motion, native
`<dialog>` + keyboard behaviour, PWA manifest, primary trigger
buttons — all still compliant.

Tests: 155/155 still passing — none of the touched tests lock the
min-h-[44px] class string specifically, so behaviour checks continue
unchanged. tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

commits.subject: fix(forms): 9 forms UX + a11y fixes from frontend.forms sweep
commits.body:
## Forms audit findings

High-severity
1. Cash-on-hand input had no accessible label.
   WorkspaceDashboard.vue had a <label> as a sibling of the <input>
   with no for/id binding — screen readers heard an unlabeled number
   input. Added id/for association plus aria-label derived from the
   workspace currency.
2. Unsaved-changes warning on TransactionEditModal close.
   Backdrop click, Escape, X, and Cancel-edit all silently discarded
   edits. Added dirty-state tracking (compares local fields against
   the original transaction) and a ConfirmDialog that fires before
   tear-down when dirty. Two discard targets: 'close' (exit modal)
   and 'view' (revert to read-only without closing).

Medium-severity
3. Period selector converted to WAI-ARIA radiogroup.
   WorkspaceForm.vue Monthly/Custom-dates toggle was plain buttons
   with no arrow-key support and no group semantics. Now role=radiogroup
   + role=radio + aria-checked + roving tabindex; Left/Right/Up/Down
   keys select and move focus to the newly selected option.
4. inputmode="decimal" on all currency number inputs.
   Triggers the iOS numeric-with-decimal keypad instead of the
   telephony keypad. Applied to WorkspaceDashboard cash-on-hand and
   TransactionEditModal amount.
5. Date-format dropdown label now associated via for/id.
   ImportStepUpload.vue had a bare text label sibling to the <select>.
6. Search input label in ImportStepReview.
   Was placeholder-only — not a label per WCAG. Added aria-label and
   changed input type to "search" for the native clear button.

Low-severity
7. File input accessible label in ImportStepUpload.
   Was `class="hidden"` (also removes from tab order) with no aria-label.
   Switched to `sr-only` so keyboard users can Tab to it, added an
   explicit aria-label, and gave the drop-zone label a focus-within
   ring so focus is visible.
8. Tag-input combobox in ImportStepReview now has aria-controls and
   aria-activedescendant — matches the wiring TransactionEditModal got
   in the previous a11y pass. Each list/option gets a stable id per
   transaction index.
9. Cash-on-hand step fixed 100 → 0.01.
   Old step forced values to multiples of 100 currency units; typing
   more granular amounts got coerced.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: The radiogroup behaviour, unsaved-changes
confirm, and iOS inputmode need manual verification. Focus-change
on arrow-key selection is the subtle one — tab into "Monthly",
press Right, focus should jump to "Custom dates" with it selected.

Tags: forms, a11y, aria, radiogroup, ios, inputmode, unsaved-changes

commits.subject: refactor(debug): wrap-pass hardening — prod visibility, URL redaction,
commits.body:
subscribe-during-replay, mount race, ARIA, docs

All 8 items from the DEBUG_SYSTEM wrap-pass landed. The biggest was
the prod false-positive: the React pill was gated `if (!isDev) return`,
which meant the pre-React inline pill's 20-second "React failed to
mount" escalation fired for every production user. Fixed by mounting
the React pill in prod too but rendering nothing when no errors or
warnings exist.

## Fix 1 — Prod mount-timeout false positive

`utils/debugRootMount.ts`: removed the `if (!isDev) return` guard.
The React pill now mounts in prod too, so `__debugMounted` flips
correctly, so the inline pill's 20s escalation doesn't misfire.

`components/debug/DebugPill.tsx`: added a visibility gate — in prod
the component returns null unless `entries` contains at least one
error or warning. Production users see nothing on a clean page;
when an error or warning is captured (via the inline pill's
`__debugPushError` bridge, `window.error`, `unhandledrejection`, or
intercepted `console.error`/`console.warn`), the pill fades in
with the error count + tap-to-expand.

`debugLog()` itself still no-ops in prod for ordinary `.debugLog()`
call-site tracing, so the prod log buffer only contains
error/warning captures — nothing gets logged speculatively.

Doc comment in debugRootMount.ts explains the tradeoff: ~10 KB of
inactive prod UI in exchange for actual error visibility to end
users hitting an issue.

## Fix 2 — URL fragment redaction

`redactUrl()` in `utils/debugLog.ts` now strips both `?query` AND
`#fragment`. OAuth implicit flow hashes, client-side session state,
and anything else after `#` is replaced with `#[redacted]` before
the URL lands in a `debugGenerateReport()` copy.

## Fix 3 — Subscribe-during-replay

`subscribeDebugLog()`:
 - Snapshots `entries` with `.slice()` before iterating so a
   concurrent `debugLog()` push doesn't inject entries into the
   "replay" phase of the contract (they'll arrive through the
   normal notify path instead).
 - Checks `listeners.has(listener)` each iteration so a listener
   that unsubscribes itself from inside a replayed call (by
   invoking its returned unsubscribe fn synchronously) stops
   receiving further replay deliveries.

## Fix 4 — Mount race guard

`utils/debugRootMount.ts`: new `window.__debugMountAttempted` flag
set synchronously BEFORE `createRoot()`. Covers the tiny race
window between `createRoot(...).render(...)` returning and
DebugPill's `useEffect` flipping `__debugMounted` — two concurrent
importers (HMR re-exec + transitive sibling import, etc.) would
previously race on `__debugMounted` and could attempt a second
`createRoot` on the same DOM element.

Declared on Window in `types/rn-web-style.d.ts` with doc
distinguishing it from `__debugMounted`.

## Fix 5 — Documentation

New `### Debug System` subsection in `CLAUDE.md` § Core Concepts
documenting:
 - The inline pre-React pill (what it covers, ~1.5 KB inline script,
   ships in both dev + prod)
 - The React DebugPill (separate root, pure DOM, prod visibility
   gate)
 - The debugLog store (API, capture surfaces, prod no-op, design
   choice to NOT filter React dev-mode warnings)
 - Why THREE cooperating surfaces: one per failure mode (bundle
   never loads / main-tree crashes / everything works).

`docs/TODO.md`: Live-browser verification entry expanded with 9
specific DEBUG_SYSTEM confirmations (throw-in-render test,
console-capture latency, `<div id="debug-root">` presence,
pre-React inline pill fallback, HMR single-attach, subscribe-
during-replay, mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, prod visibility
gate, URL redaction).

Removed one stale entry from the Low Priority section ("clean up
`as any` casts") — that was done in commit d314346.

## Fix 6 — React warning capture is intentional

Added a design-note comment in the `console.error` interception in
`utils/debugLog.ts` explaining that React dev-mode warnings are
deliberately captured unfiltered. Filtering would suppress
legitimate library errors formatted the same way; the right fix
for noisy warnings is to address the underlying issue.

## Fix 7 — Residual `PWADebugBanner` text sweep

Updated `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts` doc-comment and
`utils/debugLog.ts` doc-comment that referenced the old name by
file. `docs/SESSION_NOTES.md` retains a historical reference as
narrative (session snapshots describe past state; the end-of-
session rewrite will naturally replace it).

## Fix 8 — ARIA roles on DebugPill

- `role="tablist"` on tab container, `aria-label="Debug tabs"`
- `role="tab"` on each tab button with `aria-selected`,
  `aria-controls`, and stable `id`
- `role="tabpanel"` on panel body with `aria-labelledby` pointing
  at the active tab's id
- `role="log"` + `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic={false}` on
  the Log tab body specifically, so new entries get polite-
  announced without interrupting other screen-reader flow
- `role="dialog"` + `aria-label="Debug pill"` on the expanded
  panel wrapper

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors. All 8 fixes land without introducing
new casts or directives. Fix 7 grep confirms zero source-file
references to `PWADebugBanner` remain; the only hits are in
session-narrative history docs.

Live-browser verification of all items expanded in
`docs/TODO.md § High Priority` (9 concrete DevTools steps including
`throw new Error()` in render, console-capture latency check,
mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, production visibility gate,
URL redaction roundtrip).

Tags: debug, accessibility, production, docs, fleet-alignment

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(export): pre-embed Google Fonts so html-to-image stops spamming CORS errors
commits.body:
Mobile PWA debug report showed every export firing 5+ console errors:
  Error inlining remote css file SecurityError: ... cssRules ...
  Error loading remote stylesheet TypeError: Failed to fetch
  Error while reading CSS rules from https://fonts.googleapis.com/...

Root cause: html-to-image's getCSSRules walks document.styleSheets and
calls sheet.cssRules on each — cross-origin sheets without crossorigin
throw SecurityError. The fallback fetch() then fails because the SW had
cached opaque (status 0) responses under no-cors and they can't satisfy
the new CORS-mode request. Export "succeeded" but with system fonts.

Fix:
- New `utils/fontEmbed.js` fetches active fonts' CSS over CORS, inlines
  every woff2 url(...) as a data URL, caches per font URL.
- `captureAsBlob` / `captureForPdf` now accept fontIds and pass the
  resulting CSS to toCanvas as `fontEmbedCSS`. With that option set,
  html-to-image skips its broken stylesheet walker entirely.
- ExportButtons threads activeFontIds (title + body, useMemo) through
  all four call sites and useCallback deps.

Defense in depth (so other CSSOM consumers also work):
- crossorigin="anonymous" on every Google Fonts <link> tag (index.html
  + MobileLayout / DesktopLayout / ReaderMode).
- SW caches bumped: google-fonts-cache → -v2, gstatic-fonts-cache → -v2,
  cacheableResponse.statuses tightened from [0,200] → [200] so opaque
  can never be cached. Old caches (with stale opaque entries that would
  fail CORS) are abandoned by the rename.
- debugLog.js console interceptor filters the four known html-to-image
  noise messages so future stragglers don't pollute debug reports.
  Original console.error output is preserved.

Why pre-fetch instead of skipFonts:true: SVG <foreignObject> rasterized
via Image element loses parent document context — document.fonts isn't
consulted, so the result would render with system fonts.

Tags: export, pwa, fonts, html-to-image, debug, service-worker
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-export-fidelity
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QRw1MjzQ87B6weXuqwvz19

commits.subject: fix(motion): ApexCharts reduced-motion + bounded kebab pulse
commits.body:
High-severity
1. ApexCharts now respects prefers-reduced-motion.
   The CSS reduced-motion clamp in index.css covers Tailwind/DaisyUI
   animations, but ApexCharts drives SVG animations imperatively via
   its own RAF loop — line draw-in, tooltip fades, and legend
   transitions still played for motion-sensitive users. CashflowGraph
   reads the media query into a reactive ref (with a change listener
   so mid-session OS toggles apply), then passes it to the chart
   options: `chart.animations.enabled: !reducedMotion.value`.
   Added reducedMotion to the chart :key so the instance re-inits
   on preference change — ApexCharts reads animations config at init
   time, not reactively.

Medium-severity
2. Kebab-menu hint pulse now bounded.
   The UX pass removed the 6s auto-dismiss to make the hint persist
   until tapped, but animate-pulse runs forever by default. WCAG 2.2.2
   requires a stop mechanism for motion lasting >5s. Replaced with
   a new `animate-hint-pulse` class (5 iterations ≈ 10s) defined in
   index.css; the surrounding `ring-2 ring-primary/30` remains
   visible after the animation ends, so the affordance is preserved
   without continuous motion.

Dropped finding: the agent flagged HelpDrawer.vue setTimeout leak,
but that file already clears the timer in onUnmounted — verified
at HelpDrawer.vue:50-52. False positive.

Non-motion findings (chart re-render cost, dashboard computed chain,
BottomSheet per-frame reactivity) deferred to the `perf` trigger.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: The ApexCharts fix needs testing with a real
prefers-reduced-motion setting. On macOS: System Settings → Accessibility
→ Display → Reduce motion. The chart line should appear instantly on
load instead of drawing in.

Tags: motion, a11y, reduced-motion, chart, apexcharts, wcag
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish
Semver: patch

commits.subject: ux: apply all 9 audit findings — affordances, lock-state visibility, copy polish
commits.body:
Applies the ux-trigger findings from this session's frontend sweep. No
HIGH issues remain; MEDIUM + LOW items landed as copy + small JSX
additions. Nothing changes behaviour — only what the user sees, when,
and why.

HIGH (2)
- ModuleEditor save button: added `title="No unsaved changes"` so the
  disabled state explains itself on hover (screen-reader users still
  rely on the button label — title is supplementary, not primary).
- ContractUploadPreview Create button: dynamic title covering the two
  disabled reasons ("Split oversized sections first..." vs "Include at
  least one section..."). The post-flex warning text still renders
  below; this adds the inline explanation next to the button instead
  of just after it.

MEDIUM (3)
- ModuleEditorPage: when `isSigned`, render an `alert alert-info`
  ABOVE the editor explaining that both signatures invalidate on
  save. ModuleEditor's textarea always renders; without the banner
  the disabled state was silent.
- AgreementDetailPage partial-upload toast: extended copy to name
  the recovery path — "...failed — use Retry failed in the progress
  panel". The retry button was already shown; users just didn't know
  to look for it.
- AppLayout "Check for updates" aria-label: adds "— this may take a
  moment" during the checking state so screen-reader users get
  context for the disabled + relabelled button.

LOW (4)
- HandshakeStatus no-counterparty state: swapped neutral
  `bg-base-200 border-base-300` styling for `alert alert-info` with
  a user-plus icon. Matches the other three handshake states (all
  use semantic alert variants) instead of blending with the page
  background in dark mode.
- ReadyToSignToggle: renamed "Undo ready status" →
  "Clear ready status". "Undo" implies reversing a recent action;
  this is a state toggle. Title copy updated to match.
- ProjectDetailPage delete confirm: expanded the message from
  generic "all its agreements" to explicit "all agreements, modules,
  notes, and proposals". Names the full blast radius without
  promising a recovery window that doesn't exist.
- SequenceSignOff pending state: expanded the one-line "approve to
  complete" into a sentence naming consequences — PDF locked with
  verification code, no further reordering/edits/additions without
  reopening the whole agreement. Users learn what they're about to
  irreversibly trigger.

Tests: 155/155 still passing (none of the touched components have
tests that lock the specific copy strings affected). tsc -b clean,
eslint clean.

  flippable kill switch; backend chose single-gate; trade-off is
  consistent with this project's "one environment" policy (bypass is
  always on anyway) but means the removal path is code-change-only
  rather than env-var-flippable. Future-me or future-owner can read
  this and know why it's like this.

- Updated the removal-path steps in BOTH entries (top and the
  "Re-enable authentication" one below): backend removal is now
  "delete the allowDevBypass block from lib/auth.ts + strip the option
  from 25 sancio call sites" rather than "remove env var, optional
  code-delete later."

- Kept the coordinated-flip requirement intact — frontend and backend

No code change. Docs only.

commits.subject: fix(mobile): 6 mobile UX fixes from frontend.mobile sweep
commits.body:
High-severity
1. Restore error alert no longer sits behind iOS keyboard.
   AppLayout.vue fixed div was `bottom-20` (80px). On iOS with the
   on-screen keyboard or home indicator, the alert fell into the
   obscured zone. Now `bottom: calc(1.5rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom))`.

Medium-severity
2. Modal-box scrollability made explicit.
   DaisyUI's `.modal-box` doesn't guarantee overflow-y:auto. On small
   phones, TransactionEditModal in edit mode with many tags + ML
   suggestions could exceed 90vh and trap the user. Added
   `max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto` to TransactionEditModal,
   InstallInstructionsModal, TutorialModal, and ConfirmDialog for
   consistency.
3. Body scroll locked while any dialog is open.
   Centralized in useDialogA11y — first push to the dialog stack saves
   current inline styles and sets body/html overflow:hidden plus
   overscroll-behavior:contain; last pop restores them. Eliminates
   ghost-scroll under modals, prevents iOS rubber-band exposing page
   edges, and stops pull-to-refresh firing under overlays.
4. Pull-to-refresh gated behind dialog stack.
   usePullToRefresh imports isDialogOpen() from useDialogA11y and
   bails at touchstart if any dialog is up. A downward drag inside
   a modal or sheet no longer triggers a page refresh instead of
   scrolling the sheet contents.
5. ErrorAlert dismiss button now a 40×40 touch target.
   Was a bare 16px icon with no padding — impossible to tap
   reliably on mobile. Wrapped the X in the project's standard
   40×40 flex container (matches other close buttons).

Low-severity
6. Tag removal X buttons bumped 20×20 → 28×28.
   TransactionEditModal tag chips had 20px close buttons — below
   the 44px recommendation and inconsistent with other close
   buttons. 28×28 keeps chip compactness while improving tappability.
   Focus-visible ring added.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: body-scroll-lock and P2R gating need manual
verification on a real mobile device. Open TransactionEditModal,
try to pull down on the backdrop — should not scroll the page or
trigger refresh. Open BottomSheet on workspace detail, same test.
Dismiss alert on iOS with keyboard open — alert should stay visible.

commits.subject: feat(auth): drop SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS env var gate, opt-in arg alone controls bypass
commits.body:
The env gate was added as defense-in-depth plus a kill-switch. In practice:
- The kill-switch requires a redeploy anyway (Vercel env change is not instant
  without one), so it has no speed advantage over a code change.
- The defense-in-depth only helps if the wrong call site gets `allowDevBypass:
  true` merged — unlikely given review, and the mistake is still caught by the
  "non-sancio routes unaffected" test.
- The activation friction is real: every fresh Vercel deploy / preview env /
  project clone needs a manual env var set, and a single misconfigured env
  produces silent 401s on every sancio call from sun-sea-o.

This project is pre-first-release with a plan to delete the bypass code
entirely before real auth ships. The env gate buys little for that timeline.
Removing it so the deployed sun-sea-o works end-to-end from the moment the
backend deploy lands.

Changes:
- lib/auth.ts: drop env check, update JSDoc to describe single-gate design
  and the now-simplified removal procedure.
- api/__tests__/auth.test.ts: drop two env-gate tests (env unset, non-"true"
  env value) and update titles on remaining 6 tests.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: drop the Vercel env var activation entry entirely.
  Replace with a follow-up action for sun-sea-o's USER_ACTIONS.md (which
  currently references the env var as "the second gate" and is now stale).
  Simplify the removal path — no env var to remove, just the code.

The 25 sancio call sites still pass `{ allowDevBypass: true }` — no change
needed there.

commits.subject: refactor(pwa): harden visibility-based update checks + granular errors
commits.body:
Wrap-pass fixes to the PWA update-check work. All 7 items flagged
in the reflective pass addressed.

## Fix 1 — Initial-state gate at setup

`initUpdateDetection()` now checks `document.visibilityState` before
starting the hourly interval. If a user opens the app in a background
tab (middle-click, window-swap), we skip interval start entirely;
the first `visible` transition later fires an immediate check and
kicks off the cadence. Previous behavior ran the interval anyway
until the first `hidden` event — wasteful in the background-tab case.

## Fix 2 — iOS Safari PWA standalone (pagehide / pageshow)

`visibilitychange` covers desktop tab-switch and window-minimize but
iOS PWA standalone mode transitions through `pagehide` → `pageshow`
on app-switch (swipe-up). Previously we silently kept polling while
the installed iPhone PWA was in the background. Now:

- `pagehide` → stop interval (symmetric to visibilitychange→hidden)
- `pageshow` → immediate check (throttled) + restart interval
  (symmetric to visibilitychange→visible)

`pageshow.persisted` (bfcache restore) intentionally runs the same
path: the bundle may have been updated server-side since the page
was cached, so we re-check on every restore.

## Fix 3 — docs/TODO.md live-browser verification expanded

The existing "Live-browser theme verification" High Priority entry
absorbed the 7 PWA-visibility confirm bullets from this task. Now
one entry covers both theme + PWA visibility verification, with
full DevTools step-by-step instructions. Drops once verified
end-to-end on both desktop (Chrome) and mobile (Safari iOS
installed-PWA).

## Fix 4 — HMR flag ONE-WAY documentation

Added explicit doc-comment to `window.__synctonePwaVisAttached` in
types/rn-web-style.d.ts: once set to `true`, it stays set for the
session. Nothing clears it, which is correct today because the
listeners live at module scope and share the page lifetime. The
note spells out the cleanup expectation if a future feature flag
ever tears down PWA update detection.

## Fix 5 — CheckForUpdatesResult forward-compat doc

Expanded the `CheckForUpdatesResult` doc comment with a usage
example showing the full discriminated switch. Notes explicitly
that no synctone UI consumer currently uses the distinction, but
the typed return is ready when a future "Check for updates" button
needs it.

## Fix 6 — Granular typed errors (allSettled)

Split `'error'` into `'error-sw' | 'error-version' | 'error'`:
- `'error-sw'` — SW update threw, version.json succeeded. Likely
  offline or SW crashed.
- `'error-version'` — version.json fetch threw, SW update succeeded.
  Likely server-side deploy-in-progress or CDN hiccup.
- `'error'` — both failed. Generic catch-all for when there's no
  useful distinction.

Implementation switched from `Promise.all` (fails on first rejection,
loses the other outcome) to `Promise.allSettled`. Either side's
failure no longer masks the other side's outcome.

## Fix 7 — Rapid-cycle throttle

New `VISIBILITY_THROTTLE_MS = 30 * 1000` constant + module-level
`_lastPeriodicCheckAt` timestamp. New `runPeriodicUpdateCheckThrottled()`
helper: if the last check was less than 30s ago, log
`pwa:visibility-throttled` and skip. A user rapidly cycling tabs
(5× in 2s) now fires exactly ONE check, not five. The interval
restart is not throttled — only the immediate check is — so the
hourly cadence proceeds normally.

Chosen 30s specifically: well below the hourly interval (so a real
return-from-long-absence still fires the immediate check) but high
enough to suppress realistic tab-juggling.

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors. All 7 items landed without widening
the hack surface (zero new `as any` / `@ts-expect-error` introduced).

Live-browser verification of the 7 scenarios (background tab, rapid
cycling, iOS pagehide/pageshow, HMR single-listener, native no-crash,
typed-error dispatch) requires `npm run web` locally — tracked in
the expanded docs/TODO.md entry.

Tags: pwa, service-worker, ios-safari, hygiene, fleet-alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
commits.title: harden visibility-based update checks + granular errors

commits.subject: fix(a11y): 10 accessibility fixes from frontend.a11y sweep
commits.body:
Highest-impact item is #1 — a regression I introduced in the prior
ux commit when routing transaction delete through ConfirmDialog.

High-severity
1. Dialog stack in useDialogA11y.
   Two open modals (ConfirmDialog inside TransactionEditModal) each
   registered their own document keydown listener. A single Escape
   fired both handlers, so pressing Escape on the inner confirm also
   closed the outer edit modal — losing the entire edit session.
   Refactored to a module-level stack with one shared keydown
   listener; only the topmost dialog handles Escape/Tab. Focus trap
   also yanks focus back if it leaks out of the top dialog.
2. Validation errors announced to screen readers.
   TransactionEditModal now tags the failing input with aria-invalid,
   points aria-describedby at the error, wraps the error in role="alert"
   for auto-announcement, and focuses the first failing input on save.
   Previously a screen-reader user got no feedback when Save failed.

Medium-severity
3. prefers-reduced-motion global clamp in index.css.
   Tailwind animate-*, DaisyUI spinners, and ApexCharts transitions all
   respect the OS-level preference now (vestibular / motion-sensitive
   users). 0.01ms animation/transition duration with !important.
4. Text contrast bumped from /40 → /60 on real content.
   EmptyState description, TransactionTable empty-state messages,
   TransactionEditModal "No tags" italic, CashflowGraph no-data copy.
   40% opacity failed WCAG AA 4.5:1 on both themes for body text.
5. Desktop transaction rows get role="button" + aria-label.
   Was tabindex="0" only — AT users heard a table cell with no cue
   that Enter opens the edit modal. Mobile cards gained matching
   aria-label for parity.
6. Chart text alternative for screen-reader users.
   CashflowGraph wraps the chart in role="img" with a computed
   aria-label summarising period, cumulative direction, forecast
   endpoint, and runway depletion date. ApexCharts SVG is now
   aria-hidden so AT users hear the summary instead of traversing
   the DOM.

Low-severity
7. Tutorial step dots — 32×32 touch target.
   Was 8×8 px — far under the 44px mobile minimum. Visible dot stays
   8px for design continuity, wrapped in a flex-centred 32×32 button.
   Close button bumped to 40×40 to match TransactionEditModal.
8. Pagination contrast and labels.
   ImportStepReview and TransactionTable pagination: disabled opacity
   bumped 30% → 50% (keeps WCAG 3:1 for non-text UI), aria-label on
   Previous/Next clarifies page context, aria-live="polite" on the
   page indicator so the current page announces on change.
9. Combobox aria-controls + aria-activedescendant.
   Tag-input listbox gains an id; input points at it via aria-controls,
   and aria-activedescendant tracks the highlighted option id. Explicit
   combobox wiring that screen readers don't need to infer.
10. aria-busy on loading containers.
    "Suggesting tags..." and "Parsing…" indicators gain role="status"
    + aria-live="polite" + aria-busy so AT users hear the progress
    state, not silence.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: these changes affect keyboard, screen reader,
and contrast behaviour that can't be asserted from tests alone. The
modal-stack fix in particular should be exercised manually —
TransactionEditModal open, click Delete transaction, press Escape,
verify only ConfirmDialog closes. Then verify Escape on the edit
modal still closes it.

Tags: a11y, dialog, forms, focus, aria, contrast, motion
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(cssColor): three bugs branch findings — opaque-black sentinel, alpha-zero probe, probe side-effect
commits.body:
Bugs-branch trigger pass against the branch diff found three theoretical
issues worth hardening. None observed in production yet, all cheap to
preempt.

1. **SENTINEL_RE over-matched opaque black.** The regex marked the
   alpha component optional, so `rgb(0, 0, 0)` (opaque black) would
   have matched the sentinel had any browser ever serialized opaque
   #000000 as a 3-component form. Per spec, opaque colors serialize to
   #RRGGBB — but a non-conforming browser or one deviating during CSS
   Color 4 transition could emit `rgb(0, 0, 0)`, and opaque black
   would then be mis-classified as rejected → caller gets null → falls
   back to hex. On graphiki today the dark-theme `base-content` is
   near-white, so the real impact is zero, but the over-match is a
   latent bug. Fix: require four zero components in the regex.

2. **Integrity probe didn't validate alpha.** The probe checked R/G/B
   channels of the #ff0000 and #0000ff readbacks but not alpha. A
   hypothetical hardened environment that returned [255, 0, 0, 0] and
   [0, 0, 255, 0] would pass the probe (RGB distinct, in ballpark) but
   every subsequent rasterization would return alpha=0, so every
   Cytoscape element would render fully transparent with no warning.
   Fix: add `red[3] === 255 && blue[3] === 255` to the probe's success
   condition.

3. **Probe left blue pixel in canvas.** The probe ends with
   `fillStyle = '#0000ff'` and pixel (0,0) painted blue. Stage 2's
   clearRect + fillRect overwrites this before reading, so no bug
   today. But a future optimization that removes the clearRect call
   ("we're about to fillRect anyway") would silently leak the probe's
   blue pixel into the first normalization result. Fix: call
   `clearRect` in a `finally` block at the end of the probe so the
   canvas is left in a clean state regardless of probe outcome.

Tests:
- New test: opaque black round-trips through rasterization even when
  the mock fillStyle getter returns `rgb(0, 0, 0)` — catches SENTINEL_RE
  regression.
- New test: probe with zeroed-alpha getImageData returns null instead
  of trusting the canvas — catches the alpha-blind probe.

All 347 tests pass. Lint clean, typecheck clean.

Also: removed `Confirm DaisyUI pastel token → oklch value mapping` from
docs/TODO.md. Investigation revealed the tokens were in the dark theme
(not pastel — report was from dark mode), confirming the mapping to
--color-warning / info / success / secondary / accent is correct.

Tags: bug, cssColor, sentinel, integrity-probe, bugs-trigger
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: alpha-debug-system
Semver: patch

commits.subject: harden(cssColor): fingerprint-protection probe, CSS Color 5 catch-all, non-tautological tripwire
commits.body:
Risk-pass follow-up to the cssColor rasterization fix (af48633 +
9991eda). Addresses forward-looking issues that the green test suite
couldn't catch because the existing mock-canvas tests encoded the
same assumptions the implementation depended on.

Code — cssColor.ts:
- **Canvas integrity probe.** On first real use, paint known-distinct
  colors (#ff0000 then #0000ff) and read each back via getImageData.
  If reads collapse to the same RGBA (Firefox
  `privacy.resistFingerprinting`, Tor Browser, some hardened
  anti-fingerprinting extensions) or the probe throws,
  `canvasIntegrityOK` flips to false and every subsequent
  `normalizeColor` returns null — callers fall back to their hex
  literal (visible + correct) instead of a silently-wrong rasterized
  value. Probe cached once per canvas lifetime; reset by
  `clearCSSColorCache`. Gated on `fillRect`/`getImageData` existing so
  test mocks without rasterization support are not mis-classified.
- **Harden SENTINEL_RE.** Previous regex only accepted
  `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)` (comma-separated, any whitespace). New regex
  also accepts `rgb(0 0 0 / 0)` and `rgba(0 0 0 / 0)` (CSS Color 4
  slash-alpha syntax). Prevents a browser that emits the new form
  from misclassifying the sentinel check and flagging every assignment
  as rejected.
- **Extend rejection guards.** Split the previous
  `MODERN_SYNTAX_RE` into two layers: (a) a hit-list of known-rejected
  functions (`oklch`/`oklab`/`color`/`color-mix`/`lab`/`lch`/`hwb`/
  `light-dark`/`contrast-color`) and (b) an `UNKNOWN_FN_RE` catch-all
  with `KNOWN_SAFE_FN_RE` whitelist (`rgb`/`rgba`/`hsl`/`hsla`). Any
  future CSS Color 5+ function we haven't enumerated is rejected by
  the catch-all rather than silently leaking to Cytoscape.

Tests:
- **Upgrade `cytoscapeStyles.safety.test.ts` to a raster-aware mock.**
  The previous mock scripted `fillStyle` to downsample oklch to hex —
  same tautological pattern documented in AI_MISTAKES. The upgraded
  mock preserves raw values on `fillStyle` (simulates modern Chromium
  CSS Color 4 serialization) and returns RGBA bytes from `getImageData`
  derived from the expected-output hex. The tripwire now actually
  exercises the production rasterization path instead of a rigged
  fallback. Also expanded `UNKNOWN_FN_RE` coverage in the stylesheet
  walk assertion.
- **New cssColor tests:**
  - TRIPWIRE for `color-mix()` + UNKNOWN_FN catch-all via selective
    getImageData failure (probe passes on sRGB colors, fails on CSS
    Color 4 — simulates an extension that whitelists legacy syntax).
  - Positive + negative tests for the integrity probe (collapsed
    uniform readbacks return null; cooperative canvas returns hex).
  - Renamed the pre-existing "normalizeColor" describe block to
    "normalizeColor (fallback path — no getImageData)" so a filtered
    run surfaces both the fallback block and the modern-canvas block.
- Helper `installModernCanvas` auto-seeds probe colors in both the
  serialization and raster rule maps so individual tests don't have
  to remember.
- Replaced the older "tolerates rasterization failure when fillStyle
  already downsampled to hex" test with "returns null when
  getImageData throws during the integrity probe" — the new semantic
  (fail-closed when canvas can't rasterize) is strictly safer than
  the old (trust legacy fillStyle serialization on a misbehaving
  canvas).

Other:
- GraphCanvas.tsx — updated the `document.fonts.ready` comment block
  to clarify the re-apply's primary purpose (DaisyUI stylesheet not
  yet painted on Cytoscape init) is a lifecycle issue distinct from
  normalization. Rasterization is deterministic per-input; the
  re-apply still matters because CSS vars are initially unset.
- CLAUDE.md + docs/CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md — reflected the new
  probe, the regex split, and the hardened sentinel coverage.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md — first entry added: merge
  `claude/review-claude-md-wuSD2` to `main` so Vercel deploys the
  fix. Production still serves the pre-fix build; re-test on Chrome
  147 Android after merge.

Tests: 342 -> 345 (3 new in cssColor). Lint clean, typecheck clean.

Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing

commits.subject: feat(pwa): visibility-based update checks + typed checkForUpdates result
commits.body:
Completes the PWA_SYSTEM pattern's "Update Check Scheduling" surface
for synctone's custom-sw.js setup (synctone doesn't use
vite-plugin-pwa; hand-wired equivalents in hooks/usePWAUpdate.ts).

## Part A — Visibility-based update checks

Previously: `initUpdateDetection()` created an hourly `setInterval`
whose ID was discarded. A tab left open in a background window kept
firing `_registration.update()` + `checkVersionJson()` every hour —
wasteful and (for users with auto-sleeping laptops) led to phantom
errors in the debug log.

Now: interval handle stored in module-level `_updateCheckInterval`
so a `visibilitychange` listener can pause/resume it.
- Tab hidden → `stopUpdateCheckInterval()` clears the interval.
- Tab visible → immediate `runPeriodicUpdateCheck()` (tab may have
  been backgrounded through a deploy) then restart the interval at
  the same hourly cadence via `startUpdateCheckInterval()`.

HMR double-attach guarded behind `window.__synctonePwaVisAttached`
— Metro module re-exec on hot-reload would otherwise fire the
handler twice per visibility change. Flag declared on Window in
`types/rn-web-style.d.ts` (not `window as any` — that pattern was
already cleaned up in d314346).

Listener lives at module scope because the SW registration itself
lives at module scope; component mounts/unmounts don't control
this lifecycle. Native platforms skip the whole path via the
existing `Platform.OS === 'web'` guard at the top of
`initUpdateDetection()`.

## Part B — Typed `checkForUpdates()` return

Was: `Promise<void>` — callers could only call-and-ignore.

Now: `Promise<'no-sw' | 'done' | 'error'>` — exported as the
`CheckForUpdatesResult` type so UI callers can write a typed
switch-statement without re-deriving the string set.

- `'no-sw'` — SW API unavailable (non-web, browser without SW,
  or registration not yet initialized). Fast-path return.
- `'done'` — Check completed; `hasUpdate` will reflect any
  newly-detected update on the next listener tick.
- `'error'` — Check threw; logged via `logger.error` + `debugLog`.

Dual-mechanism check preserved (SW + version.json in parallel) —
the task spec's simpler `reg.update()`-only version would regress
bundle-only updates. Existing `_registration` null check gives the
typed `'no-sw'` signal early.

Existing callers (pull-to-refresh in `home.tsx` and
`DesktopLayout.tsx`) continue fire-and-forget — neither is an
explicit "Check for updates" button where toast surfacing would
help the user. Typed return is forward-compat for when that
button lands.

## Docs

CLAUDE.md § PWA System updated with two new subsections:
- "Update Check Scheduling" — visibility-based interval management.
- "Manual Update Check — Typed Result" — the three return values,
  what each means for UI feedback.

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit`: 0 errors (unchanged — synctone's been strict-clean
since d314346).

Behavioral verifications that need a real browser (tracked in
docs/TODO.md § High Priority for the next live-web smoke test):
1. Background tab for several minutes → DevTools Network tab shows
   no SW update requests firing at the hourly boundary.
2. Foreground tab → immediate check fires within a few ms.
3. HMR reload in dev → `__synctonePwaVisAttached` prevents a
   second listener attach (verify with `getEventListeners(document)`
   in DevTools console).
4. iOS/Android Expo build doesn't reach the visibilitychange code
   path (Platform.OS guard).
5. Manual check returns the correct enum value.

Debt: paid

commits.subject: feat(auth): sancio-scoped dev auth bypass for sun-sea-o AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED
commits.body:
Closes the backend half of the sun-sea-o dev auth bypass. When the frontend
ships with AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED=true it sends X-Dev-User-Id + X-Dev-User-Email
instead of Authorization: Bearer, and every sancio call 401s until the
backend also accepts those headers.

Design:
- Two-gate: opt-in arg `{ allowDevBypass: true }` AND env `SANCIO_AUTH_BYPASS=true`.
  Either alone has no effect, so a forgotten opt-in committed to main cannot
  become a production backdoor without the env var, and an accidentally-set
  env var cannot affect non-sancio routes.
- Call-site opt-in instead of URL-prefix check: scoping is explicit in code
  review, doesn't couple auth to routing, and fails closed for any new sancio
  route added without the opt-in.
- X-Dev-User-Email is ignored server-side — UUID is the identity.
- All 25 sancio call sites (across 10 route files) pass allowDevBypass:true.
  Non-sancio call sites (api/forms/*, api/submissions/*) unchanged — still
  require JWT regardless of env state.

Tests cover the full matrix: env+opt-in+header → bypass; env+opt-in+no header
→ JWT fallthrough; env+no opt-in+header → 401 (non-sancio case); no env+opt-in
+header → 401 (env gate); non-"true" env values → 401; empty header → 401;
valid JWT passthrough unchanged; invalid JWT still 401 even with bypass env on.

USER_ACTIONS.md updated with the Vercel env var step and the full removal
path for first real release (both sides must flip together).

commits.subject: chore(cssColor+docs): wrap-pass polish — tighter comments, canvas sizing, doc sync
commits.body:
Follow-up to af48633 addressing items surfaced by the wrap reflective
pass. No behavior changes for any happy path; one minor allocation
reduction and several doc corrections / additions.

Code:
- Resize shared canvas to 1x1 at creation (default 300x150 wastes a
  ~180KB backing buffer for a single-pixel workload). Rationale
  documented inline so future readers don't re-expand it.
- Tighten cssColor.ts decision-doc header (38 -> 28 lines). Kept the
  valuable "alternatives considered" block; trimmed redundant prose.
- Soften "modern Chromium preserves CSS Color 4 syntaxes on fillStyle"
  from stated fact to "some modern Chromium builds (per CSS Color 4)
  preserve..." in both the code comment and the CLAUDE.md convention.
  The hypothesis fits the Chrome 147 Android debug report exactly, but
  wasn't independently verified against a spec commit or Chromium
  source, so the wording now matches the evidence we actually have.
- Clarify the `transparent` escape-hatch comment and explicitly list
  the known-limitation zero-alpha forms (hsla(0,0%,0%,0),
  #00000000, rgb(0 0 0 / 0)) so a future reader doesn't re-derive the
  reasoning from the test assertion alone.

Tests:
- Annotate the existing `normalizeColor` describe block as exercising
  the rasterization-unavailable fallback path. Without this note, a
  contributor might assume passing the original tests is sufficient
  proof of correctness on modern browsers — it isn't; that's the
  `modern canvas fillStyle` block's job.

Docs:
- docs/CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md — remove the stale claim that
  fillStyle serialization guarantees downsample to #rrggbb/rgba(...).
  Describe the two-stage normalization (sentinel rejection + raster
  normalize) that actually ships. Update test count 18 -> 26 and
  explain why the suite now has two describe blocks.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md — new entry "Mock-canvas tests shielded a real
  browser regression for 3 days." Captures the tautological-mock
  anti-pattern: the mock encoded the same assumption the impl
  depended on (fillStyle downsamples), so green tests proved internal
  consistency only. Prevention: add tests whose mock simulates the
  alternative browser behavior; add TRIPWIRE tests asserting negative
  invariants (output never starts with `oklch(`/etc.); treat
  production debug reports as authoritative over green local tests.
- docs/TODO.md — two new backlog items: verify the rasterization fix
  on Safari + Firefox (production report was Chrome 147 Android
  only), and confirm the inferred DaisyUI pastel token -> oklch value
  mapping used in the report.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md — rewritten with current-session summary
  (CLAUDE.md alignment + cssColor fix + wrap-pass polish), per
  CLAUDE.md convention that SESSION_NOTES is rewritten at session end.

All 342 tests still pass. Lint clean, typecheck clean.

commits.subject: refactor(types): replace all hack-trigger patterns with proper augmentations
commits.body:
Honest self-review after the user asked "were these proper fixes?" of
the previous 14-TS-error cleanup. Four of those fixes were
cast-based (`as unknown as X`, `as any`) or minimal-directive-removal
rather than root-cause type fixes. This commit upgrades all of them
to proper module augmentations, and extends the same treatment to
every other `@ts-expect-error` / `as any` / `as unknown as` in the
runtime source tree.

## New ambient declarations — `types/rn-web-style.d.ts`

Module augmentations for:
- `ViewStyle` / `TextStyle` — `backdropFilter`, `WebkitBackdropFilter`,
  `WebkitOverflowScrolling`. react-native-web silently forwards these
  to CSS; RN's type declarations don't cover them. Native RN ignores
  them at runtime (no-op), so marking them on the shared type matches
  both-platform reality.
- `Navigator` — `brave`, `setAppBadge`, `clearAppBadge`, `standalone`.
  Browser-specific APIs not in standard lib.dom.d.ts.
- `Window` — `__pwaInstallPromptEvent`, `__pwaPromptReceived`,
  `__deferredInstallPrompt`, `__pwaPromptListenerAdded`. Globals set
  by the flash/prompt inline scripts in `app/+html.tsx` and
  `app/_layout.tsx`.
- `BeforeInstallPromptEvent` — Chrome-specific install event, moved
  from a local declaration in `usePWAInstall.ts` to global so every
  consumer agrees on the shape.

Scope discipline on the augmentation file: only properties that are
(a) genuinely used in synctone, (b) documented in MDN/caniuse, and
(c) silently no-op on native. No speculative API surface.

## Call-site cleanups (downstream of the augmentations)

- `components/ComposeBar.tsx`: `getGlassStyle` return type narrowed
  from the lazy `Record<string, unknown>` to proper `ViewStyle`. The
  stale `@ts-expect-error` on the <View style={...}> site was already
  removed in the previous commit.
- `app/(app)/chat/[id].tsx`, `components/ChatPanel.tsx`,
  `app/(app)/home.tsx`: three `@ts-expect-error` directives on
  backdropFilter / WebkitOverflowScrolling style props — all removed,
  styles now type-check natively against the augmented ViewStyle.
- `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts`: local `BeforeInstallPromptEvent`
  interface deleted (moved to the ambient file). Two
  `as unknown as Record<...>` / `as X | undefined` casts on window
  global access — gone; `window.__pwaInstallPromptEvent` is now
  typed directly.
- `app/_layout.tsx`: four `window as Window & { __x?: ... }` inline
  intersection types replaced with direct property access. Listener
  callback parameter narrowed via one targeted `as BeforeInstallPromptEvent`
  with a comment explaining why DOM's `Event`-only listener typing
  is too conservative.
- `components/PWADebugBanner.tsx`: five `(window as any)` /
  `(navigator as any)` casts on debug diagnostics — gone; every
  property now resolves against the augmented globals.
- `services/notifications.ts`: five `(navigator as any).setAppBadge` /
  `.clearAppBadge` calls replaced with optional-chained
  `navigator.setAppBadge?.(...)`. Runtime feature-detection
  (`'setAppBadge' in navigator`) retained as belt-and-braces.
- `components/MessageBubble.tsx`: three `@ts-expect-error` directives
  on View ref bindings (refs typed as `HTMLDivElement` because the
  file uses DOM APIs like `.style` mutation and `getBoundingClientRect`
  on the underlying element — this is correct for web, where
  react-native-web renders View as div and forwards the ref). Replaced
  with a documented `domRef<T>` helper that narrows an `HTMLElement`-
  typed ref to `React.Ref<View>`. One contained cross-type cast in a
  named helper with rationale beats three `@ts-expect-error`
  directives scattered at binding sites.

## Verification

`./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` → **0 errors**.

`grep -rn "@ts-expect-error\|as any\b\|as unknown as"` across
`app/ components/ hooks/ lib/ services/ contexts/ stores/ utils/`:
- Zero `@ts-expect-error`
- Zero `as any`
- One `as unknown as` — inside the documented `domRef` helper
  body, which is the single narrow cast bridging
  react-native-web's View-as-div runtime reality to RN's type
  surface. Centralized, named, documented.

No behavior changes — all edits are type-annotation tightening
plus the mechanical call-site cleanup that follows once the
underlying types are correct.

commits.subject: fix(ux): nine targeted UX improvements from frontend.ux sweep
commits.body:
Findings from the `ux` trigger against the whole codebase. Each item
closes a discoverability, feedback, or destructive-action gap a real
user would hit. No architectural changes; all targeted component edits.

High-severity
1. Empty state on dashboard when no transactions imported.
   WorkspaceDashboard.vue now renders an EmptyState with a primary
   "Import transactions" CTA when transactions.length === 0, rather
   than a blank graph + zero metrics + empty table. Suppressed while
   loading to avoid a flash.
2. Transaction delete promoted to ConfirmDialog (was inline panel).
   TransactionEditModal.vue now uses ConfirmDialog for delete, matching
   the pattern already used for workspace delete. Same alertdialog
   semantics, same button weight — destructive actions now consistent.

Medium-severity
3. CSV parse UI no longer appears frozen on large files.
   ImportStepUpload.vue wraps the sync parseCSV call in
   requestAnimationFrame so the "Parsing…" spinner paints before the
   main thread is grabbed. Spinner visual upgraded from text-only
   "Parsing..." to a proper loading-spinner + reassuring copy.
4. ML retry affordance on dashboard (was only in import review).
   WorkspaceDashboard exposes modelError + retryModel through
   TransactionTable → TransactionEditModal. Modal shows a retry link
   when suggestionsError is set and no suggestions exist. Also fixes
   a latent bug: handleRequestSuggestions now awaits waitForModel()
   before calling suggestTags, so users opening a transaction before
   the model finishes loading actually get suggestions once it does.
5. Restore-from-backup loading state.
   useRestoreWorkspace exposes a restoring ref; ConfirmDialog gains
   a `busy` prop that disables both buttons and swaps the confirm
   label for a spinner + "Working…". Prevents the app-looks-frozen
   window during IDB replace transactions and blocks double-clicks.
6. Kebab-menu hint persists until tapped (was 6s auto-dismiss).
   WorkspaceDetailView.vue: pulse continues until user interacts with
   the button. Previously a user who glanced away during the 6s
   window would never see the hint and could miss Save as PDF /
   Export / Edit entirely. Removes kebabHintTimeout entirely.

Low-severity
7. Wizard error banner clears on navigation and retry.
   NewImportWizard.vue clears error at the top of handleUploadComplete
   (stale banner from prior upload no longer persists) and on the
   ImportStepReview back emit.
8. TransactionTable tablet breakpoint moved sm → md.
   Card layout now extends through tablets (< 768px); table kicks in
   only at laptop widths. Prevents awkward tag-column wrapping on
   640-768px screens.
9. Export toast tells the user where the file went.
   "Workspace exported" → "Workspace exported — check your downloads".

Type-check clean. 111/111 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

commits.subject: fix(cssColor): rasterize via getImageData to survive CSS Color 4 fillStyle preservation
commits.body:
Modern Chromium (Chrome 147 on Android, confirmed via production debug
report) implements CSS Color 4 serialization on canvas 2D fillStyle.
Setting `ctx.fillStyle = 'oklch(...)'` and reading `String(ctx.fillStyle)`
now round-trips the oklch value unchanged instead of downsampling to
legacy hex/rgba. The previous normalizer's central assumption — "the
HTML spec guarantees fillStyle serializes any valid CSS color to
#rrggbb / rgba(...) / #rrggbbaa" — is no longer true. Every DaisyUI
oklch token leaked through getCSSColor to Cytoscape, which rejected
them silently, breaking node:selected borders, dual-query A/B/bridge
colors, analysis highlights, and arc fallbacks.

The previous mock-canvas test suite could not catch this because it
replaced real browser behavior with explicit input→output rules. Tests
asserted "oklch → #eab308" because the mock was told to do that, not
because real canvases do it.

Fix:
- Replace fillStyle-serialization as the normalization primitive with
  rasterization: paint a 1x1 pixel via fillRect, read the 8-bit sRGB
  bytes via getImageData, serialize to #rrggbb (a=255) or rgba(...).
  Pixel readback is guaranteed sRGB 8-bit regardless of how fillStyle
  chooses to serialize, so CSS Color 4 preservation cannot leak.
- Keep sentinel-based rejection detection as stage 1 — fillStyle still
  ignores unparseable inputs, and catching that lets us return null so
  getCSSColor routes to its hex fallback.
- Add MODERN_SYNTAX_RE defense-in-depth: if getImageData throws
  (tainted canvas, CSP, privacy mode), any serialized value starting
  with oklch(/oklab(/color(/lab(/lch(/hwb( is treated as rejection
  rather than fed to Cytoscape.
- Update decision-doc header to reflect the real mechanism and add an
  "alternatives considered" entry rejecting fillStyle-downsample
  specifically so the assumption can't reappear silently.

Tests:
- New describe block "modern canvas fillStyle (CSS Color 4
  serialization)" with a raster-aware mock that simulates Chrome 147+
  behavior (fillStyle getter preserves oklch; getImageData returns the
  known sRGB tuple). Verifies normalizeColor returns hex, not oklch.
- New TRIPWIRE test asserts normalizeColor never returns a string
  starting with oklch(/oklab(/color(/lab(/lch(/hwb( for non-null
  outputs — catches any future regression at the unit-test layer
  instead of production.
- New test for rasterization failure + modern-syntax guard path.
- New test for rasterization failure + legacy hex path (older
  Chromium / WebKit) still caches successfully.
- All 21 existing tests pass unchanged — they exercise the fallback
  path where the mock ctx has no fillRect/getImageData methods, the
  try/catch catches the TypeError, and the serialized hex is returned.

Docs:
- Updated CLAUDE.md "Cytoscape color normalization" convention to
  describe the two-stage mechanism (sentinel rejection + raster
  normalize), note that modern Chromium preserves CSS Color 4 syntaxes
  on fillStyle, and reference the new TRIPWIRE test by name.

Tags: bug, cytoscape, cssColor, color-normalization, css-color-4, canvas
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(types): close all 14 pre-existing TS errors (strict-clean repo)
commits.body:
I'd been reporting "14 pre-existing errors, zero new" on every commit
this session and treating the baseline as someone else's problem.
That's the bit-rot pattern the "no skipping" directive rules out.
Closing all of them now so the repo is strict-TS-clean for the first
time.

## A. Supabase Edge Function (6 errors)

`supabase/functions/send-notification/index.ts` is Deno code, not
Node/Metro. Its `Deno` global and `https://deno.land/...` imports
can't resolve under the app's tsconfig. Fix: exclude
`supabase/functions/**/*` from `tsconfig.json`'s `include`/`exclude`
and add a minimal `supabase/functions/deno.json` so Deno-aware
editors still type-check it with the correct runtime. Edge
function behavior unchanged — Supabase CLI compiles it via Deno.

## B. `app/index.tsx` (1 error)

`JSX.Element` as a bare namespace no longer resolves under React 18+
strict types. Switched to `ReactElement` from 'react' (explicit import,
no global lookup).

## C. `app/(app)/chat/[id].tsx` (2 errors)

The `@ts-expect-error` on FlatList's `WebkitOverflowScrolling` style
was stale (RN types or React types now accept it implicitly via the
conditional-object pattern), so the directive itself errored (TS2578).
Replaced the directive + object literal with an explicit
`as unknown as ViewStyle` cast scoped to the web-only branch, with
a comment explaining why. `ViewStyle` imported as a type.

## D. `components/ComposeBar.tsx` (1 error)

Same stale-`@ts-expect-error` pattern. `getGlassStyle` returns
`Record<string, unknown>` which already accepts web-only CSS
properties without complaint. Removed the directive; added a
comment documenting why it isn't needed.

## E. Null-returning components (2 errors)

`MessageActionSheet` and `Toast` both declared `React.JSX.Element`
return types but had `if (!message) return null` / `if (!visible)
return null` paths. Widened both signatures to
`React.JSX.Element | null` — the actual contract.

## F. `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts` (1 error)

`navigator as Record<string, unknown>` — TS2352 because Navigator
and Record don't structurally overlap. Fixed with double-cast
`navigator as unknown as Record<string, unknown>` and a comment
explaining the explicit widening.

## G. `services/chat.ts` (1 error)

`sanitizeDisplayName(name?: string)` accepts `string | undefined`
but `updateChatDisplayName` passes `string | null`. Widened the
sanitizer to `name: string | null | undefined` — matches the caller
contract so TypeScript tracks the shape correctly. Body already
handled falsy inputs via `if (!name) return null`.

## Verification

`./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` → **0 errors** (was 14).
No behavior changes — only type annotations, config exclusions,
and a helper widening that affects zero runtime paths.

## docs/SESSION_NOTES.md

Known Issues section updated:
- Removed the "pre-existing TS errors untouched" caveat.
- New note stating repo is now strict-clean with a summary of
  what was addressed.

commits.subject: docs(claude-md): correct Z_INDEX_SCALE deviation rationale
commits.body:
Previous wording implied MobileNav z-40 vs BurgerMenu backdrop z-40
was a tie-break driven by DOM order — that's not the real mechanism.
The actual gate is the header's `z-50` stacking context (applied when
the menu opens), which contains the backdrop and reliably places it
above MobileNav regardless of DOM ordering. Also clarified:

- MobileNav z-40 is driven by a concrete constraint (stay above
  BottomSheet z-30 so tab-tap can close the sheet), not an arbitrary
  choice.
- Pattern-strict alignment would require a BottomSheet layout refactor
  (bottom-inset above MobileNav) with no behavioral improvement —
  intentionally not undertaken.
- Modal deviation rationale tightened: the debug pill's crash-survival
  purpose is preserved because modals are inside App.jsx and unmount
  with it, while the pill lives in #debug-root.

No code changes — documentation accuracy only.

commits.body:
Follow-up to commit e99afb7 (six-point align fix). Running `align` again
surfaced two residual minor drifts between this repo's CLAUDE.md and

1. Code Standards → Timer and Subscription Cleanup: kept the repo-local

commits.subject: refactor(theme): single source of truth + prefers-color-scheme + tightening
commits.body:
Wrap-pass cleanup for the THEME_DARK_MODE work. Addresses every item
flagged in the reflective pass after the previous commit landed —
nothing deferred.

## Single source of truth (drift elimination)

Previously the combo → (light theme name, dark theme name) mapping
lived in three places — `constants/themeCombos.ts`, the inline
resolver in `lib/themeColors.ts#resolveThemeName`, and the
flash-prevention inline script in `app/+html.tsx` — each with its
own hardcoded literal. Adding a new combo would silently leave the
flash script returning the default, producing a flash for users of
the new combo.

Fix: new `COMBO_THEME_MAP` + `COMBO_IDS` exports in
`constants/themeCombos.ts`, derived from `themeCombos` via
`Object.fromEntries`. `resolveThemeName` now imports the same
constant (the old "circular dependency" comment was stale — neither
file actually imports the other, verified by grep). `+html.tsx`
imports `COMBO_THEME_MAP`, `COMBO_IDS`, `DEFAULT_COMBO` and
`JSON.stringify`s them into the inline script body. TypeScript
now enforces the three consumers agree.

## prefers-color-scheme first-visit detection

When no `synctone_dark_mode` is saved, both the inline script and
the `useTheme` load handler now fall back to
`window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches` on web
(native still defaults to dark because `Appearance.getColorScheme()`
would race against first paint). New `getSystemPrefersDark()`
helper in `useTheme.ts` centralizes the Platform guard.

The flash-prevention inline script mirrors this fallback with a
direct `matchMedia` check — same semantic, pre-mount.

## Verified sync assumption

Read Uniwind source (`node_modules/uniwind/dist/module/core/config/
config.common.js`) to confirm `setTheme()` is synchronous: mutates
internal state, synchronously calls `onThemeChange` + listener
notifications (including the rnw.js layer that adds the theme name
to `<html>.classList`), returns `void`. `applyWebAttributes()`
running on the next line therefore cannot race against Uniwind.
Documented in the `applyTheme` doc comment with a pointer to the
source location so future readers don't have to re-discover this.

## Honest docs

- CLAUDE.md § Web theme application — three layers: now marks
  `data-theme` and `.dark` as "currently unused / kept for
  forward-compat" instead of implying they have active consumers.
  ~30 bytes of HTML payload for each; the honest tradeoff is
  documented.
- CLAUDE.md new "Single source of truth" paragraph points at
  `COMBO_THEME_MAP` as the one place to edit.
- CLAUDE.md new "First-visit default" paragraph documents the
  `prefers-color-scheme` behavior per platform.
- docs/TODO.md High Priority: new "Live-browser theme verification"
  entry with 6 concrete DevTools checks the sandbox can't run.
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md new "Theme verification (web)" section with
  step-by-step tables for flash prevention, runtime toggle,
  color-scheme, `dark:` utility, and single-source-of-truth drift
  check. Added "Theme" row to Regression Checklist.

## Smoke tests run

- `tsc --noEmit`: 14 pre-existing errors (same count as before this
  branch's code changes); zero in touched files.
- `tsc --noEmit --noUnusedLocals --noUnusedParameters` on touched
  files: zero new unused locals/imports.
- Node `new Function(body)` parse check on the inline flash
  script with stubbed interpolations: syntactically valid.

Live-browser verification still requires `npm run web` locally —
tracked in docs/TODO.md High Priority as an explicit follow-up
with detailed DevTools steps.
