commits.subject: fix(cleanup): HMR-safe cleanup for remaining module-level listeners
commits.body:
Completes the TIMER_LEAKS pattern sweep surfaced by pattern-audit. The
prior commit (cf8c1c9) fixed usePWAUpdate.ts; this covers the other
five module-level listener sites identified in that audit's surface
findings.

useDarkMode.ts (2 listeners)
- window 'storage' for cross-tab sync
- mediaQuery 'change' for OS preference fallback
- Added __darkModeAttached guard + import.meta.hot.dispose() to remove
  both on HMR.

usePWAInstall.ts (4 listeners + 1 timeout)
- window 'beforeinstallprompt' (main capture)
- window 'appinstalled'
- standaloneMediaQuery 'change' (install-via-browser-menu detection)
- window 'beforeinstallprompt' { once: true } (diag timeout clear)
- 5s diagnostic setTimeout
- Extracted all anonymous handlers to named functions
  (handleBeforeInstallPrompt, handleAppInstalled, handleStandaloneChange,
  handleEarlyPromptClearDiag) so removeEventListener can reach them.
- Tracked standaloneMediaQuery and diagTimeout at module scope.
- Added __pwaInstallAttached guard + dispose hook that removes all four
  listeners (including the { once: true } pre-fire case), clears the
  media query subscription, and clears diagTimeout.

debugLog.ts (2 listeners + console patches)
- window 'error' and 'unhandledrejection' already had a guard flag but
  no teardown; patched console.error/warn likewise.
- Without dispose, HMR leaves patched console pointing at the orphaned
  old module's debugLog — new events silently routed to a stale buffer
  with no subscribers.
- Hoisted originalError/originalWarn to module scope for restoration.
- Extracted handleGlobalError/handleUnhandledRejection as named handlers.
- Added dispose hook that restores console originals, removes both
  listeners, and resets all three guard flags (__debugConsolePatched,
  __debugLogListenersAttached, __debugLogReady).

No runtime behavior change in production (import.meta.hot is dev-only).
Eliminates the dev-only listener doubling and stale-closure routing
that would accumulate across HMR cycles.

Type-check clean. All 111 tests pass.

commits.subject: feat(theme): complete THEME_DARK_MODE web integration
commits.body:
Closes the glow-props THEME_DARK_MODE pattern gaps found by the
pattern-audit trigger. synctone already had the Uniwind @variant
mechanism for theme-name-keyed CSS variables; this adds the three
web-specific layers that Uniwind doesn't touch: flash prevention,
`data-theme` metadata, `.dark` class, `color-scheme` root rule,
and Tailwind v4's `@custom-variant dark`.

## Parts A + E — Flash prevention inline script

`app/+html.tsx` gets a new synchronous `<script>` in `<head>` that
runs BEFORE the Expo bundle loads. It reads synctone's localStorage
keys (`synctone_dark_mode`, `synctone_theme_combo`) and applies the
theme class + `data-theme` + `.dark` class to `<html>` by first
paint. Resolution logic matches `lib/themeColors.ts#resolveThemeName`
verbatim and defaults to the same initial state as the Zustand store
(isDark=true, combo=retro → synthwave) when keys are absent.

Result: no light-flash when reloading with a dark theme saved, which
was previously visible because Uniwind's setTheme() only ran after
React mounted.

## Parts B + C — Web attribute + class toggling

`applyTheme()` in `hooks/useTheme.ts` extracted the web DOM side-
effects into a shared `applyWebAttributes()` helper that runs on
both the happy path (after Uniwind.setTheme succeeds) and the
fallback path (if Uniwind.setTheme throws). It:
- sets `data-theme` to the resolved theme name — keeps the runtime
  attribute in sync with whatever the flash script wrote, and
  future-proofs for any DaisyUI-native selectors that key off it
- adds/removes the `.dark` class based on isDark — enables
  Tailwind v4's `dark:` variant and drives the color-scheme rule

## Part D — `color-scheme` CSS rule

`global.css` now has `:root { color-scheme: light; }` + `html.dark
{ color-scheme: dark; }` at the top level. Makes native
`<select>` dropdowns, scrollbars, autofill highlights, and focus
rings match the active theme instead of defaulting to the browser's
system preference.

## Part F — Tailwind v4 `@custom-variant dark`

`@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));` added to
`global.css` right after `@import 'uniwind'`. Tells Tailwind v4
to resolve the `dark:` utility variant against the `.dark` class
on `<html>`. No current callers use `dark:*` utilities (the app
uses Uniwind's `@variant`-driven theme switching instead), but
having this registered means any future `dark:bg-base-300`-style
utility will work correctly without further config.

## Docs

§ Theme & Colors in CLAUDE.md updated:
- Architecture table now lists `@custom-variant dark`,
  `color-scheme` rules, `data-theme` / `.dark` attributes, and the
  flash-prevention inline script.
- New "Web theme application — three layers" subsection explains
  why all three (theme name class / data-theme / .dark class) are
  needed and which mechanism consumes each.

## Not verified

Live browser verification (no light-flash on hard reload, native
`<select>` dropdowns match theme, `dark:*` utilities resolve) can't
be done from this sandbox — should be confirmed with a local
`npm run web` + DevTools inspection. tsc --noEmit shows 14
pre-existing errors, zero new.

commits.subject: fix(patterns): APP_ICONS naming conformance + SafeStorage reclassification
commits.body:
Pattern-audit findings applied. Two fixes, one skip.

1. APP_ICONS — renamed icon-1024.png → icon.png to match the pattern
   spec (glow-props docs/implementations/APP_ICONS.md prescribes the
   1024-size asset as `icon.png`, not `icon-<size>.png`).
   - scripts/generate-icons.mjs ICONS list updated.
   - vite.config.ts ICON_PATHS + manifest maskable icon src updated.
   - public/icon-1024.png renamed via git mv, then regenerated via
     `npm run generate-icons` to confirm clean output.
   - iconCacheBust test unchanged — it asserts manifest icons carry
     versioned URLs, not specific file names. Still passes at both
     source-level and dist-level after rebuild.

   Added APP_ICONS section to CLAUDE.md "Adopted Patterns" documenting
   what's generated, what's deliberately NOT generated (adaptive-icon
   and splash-icon — see below), and the file-location deviation
   (public/ flat instead of pattern's assets/images/).

   Skipped from pattern's full file list with justification:
   - adaptive-icon.png (1024): pattern expects a separate SVG with 80%
     safe-zone padding for Android adaptive foreground. We have one
     icon-source.svg; using it as purpose:maskable directly is the
     closest viable conformance without designing a second SVG.
     Revisit if native-Android builds ship.
   - splash-icon.png (1024): only consumed by Expo/Metro native builds.
     sun-sea-o is a Vite PWA — iOS Safari generates splash from
     apple-touch-icon.png; Android splash comes from manifest
     theme_color. No consumer, no file.

2. SafeStorage — reclassified in CLAUDE.md "Adopted Patterns" as a
   repo-local utility, not a glow-props pattern. Verified against
   glow-props/docs/implementations/ — no SAFE_STORAGE.md exists there;
   the concept appears only as an internal section in THEME_DARK_MODE.
   Header wording updated to acknowledge the one non-pattern entry so
   readers don't hunt for a phantom upstream pattern. The utility's
   code and consumers are unchanged.

3. Z_INDEX_SCALE constants-module extraction — not applied. The
   pattern doesn't mandate a src/constants/zIndex.ts; it prescribes
   the scale (which sun-sea-o already follows, values inline at call
   sites, documented in CLAUDE.md Adopted Patterns). Extracting a
   constants module would be premature abstraction per the
   useCallback-omit-default rule's spirit.

Test fix collateral: ContractUploadPreview's rename-button
getByRole({name: 'One'}) queries no longer match since the earlier
a11y pass added aria-label="Rename section <title>". Queries updated
to match by regex (/Rename section "One"/) so they stay robust to
future label rephrasing.

Verified: 155/155 tests pass, tsc -b clean, eslint clean, npm run
build clean.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): HMR-safe cleanup for module-level visibilitychange listener
commits.body:
Pattern-audit against glow-props TIMER_LEAKS.md §5 flagged
usePWAUpdate.ts as partial: the module-level
`document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` had no HMR guard
and no dispose hook, so every Vite hot-reload cycle in dev would
double-subscribe the listener and orphan the periodic SW-check
interval.

Changes:
- Extracted the anonymous visibilitychange handler into a named
  function `onVisibilityChange` so removeEventListener can reach it.
- Added `window.__pwaUpdateAttached` guard flag per glow-props pattern
  to prevent double-subscription on module re-evaluation.
- Added `import.meta.hot.dispose()` hook that removes the listener,
  clears `updateCheckInterval`, and resets the guard flag.

No runtime behavior change in production (HMR hooks are dev-only).
Eliminates dev-only listener doubling and interval leaks.

Type-check passes (vue-tsc -b --noEmit clean).

Related: other composables (useDarkMode, usePWAInstall, debugLog)
also attach module-level listeners without HMR guards — surfaced
for separate follow-up, not included in this commit per scope.

commits.subject: fix(timer-leaks): apply pattern-audit findings
commits.body:
`pattern-audit` found two real timer-leak violations vs
glow-props/TIMER_LEAKS.md and one z-index scale deviation:

- AIPromptHelper.handleCopy: setTimeout had no cleanup path. If user
  copied and immediately navigated away (tab switch / sheet close)
  the 2s reset fired against an unmounted component, leaking a
  setCopied call. Store id on useRef; clear on unmount and on every
  new copy. Per TIMER_LEAKS.md §2 (single-shot, handler-driven).

- usePWAInstall effect: two parallel timeouts cleared via separate
  raw vars. Converted to array-drain shape so additional timers can
  be added without rewriting cleanup. Per TIMER_LEAKS.md §1.

- FreeformEditor three refs (confirm/blur/scroll): reviewed — the
  subagent flagged these but they're independent single-shot timers
  driven by different event handlers, already correctly using §2's
  useRef-per-timer shape. No change.

- Z_INDEX_SCALE.md deviations documented in CLAUDE.md: native
  <dialog> modals render above debug pill (accepted for native focus
  trap + Escape handling), and MobileNav at z-40 rather than z-20
  (stable; menu backdrop covers it, which is the desired behavior
  when burger menu opens).

commits.subject: docs(claude): close glow-props fleet drift (align trigger fixes)
commits.body:
Five upstream rules that synctone's CLAUDE.md was missing after
glow-props updated:

1. § Communication — add "Work, not process." bullet. Keeps process
   opinions (branching, PR shape, commit granularity, etc.) out of
   work discussions since those are the user's domain.

2. § Prohibitions — add three new rules that extend the
   work-not-process principle into explicit "never" forms:
   - Never mention branches/PRs/rebasing etc. unless the user
     raises the topic first.
   - Never frame work as "out of scope" — work is either doable
     or blocked on missing input.
   - Never opine on git history editing / branch strategy / PR
     size / review flow / commit structure.

3. § AI Notes — add "All code is yours." as the first bullet.
   Every change in every tracked repo is the assistant's own work;
   don't hedge authorship, don't investigate prior changes as if
   written by a third party, read diffs when you need to understand
   a change.

4. § Code Standards → Timer and Subscription Cleanup:
   - Replace the local verbose version (prose + inline code block)
     with glow-props' canonical 5-bullet form referencing
     `docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md` for concrete patterns.
     The upstream pattern file now exists as a proper glow-props
     implementation doc; the code example lives there instead of
     inline in CLAUDE.md.
   - Reposition the subsection to sit directly after ### Cleanup
     and before ### Quality Checks, matching glow-props' ordering
     (Cleanup and Timer cleanup are adjacent topics).

5. § Documentation — promote from `### Documentation` nested under
   `## Instructions for Claude` to a top-level `## Documentation`
   section, matching glow-props. All doc subsections promoted from
   `####` to `###`. The previously-awkward `### REMINDER:` and its
   `####` children (Workflow for EVERY Session, External Reference)
   now sit at consistent `###` level under `## Documentation`.
   `## Instructions for Claude` remains as a separate section for
   synctone-specific User Preferences / Technical Guidelines /
   Important Implementation Details.

Re-ran the align trigger after each fix; all shared sections now
either MATCH verbatim (Process, Principles, Communication, Code
Standards Timer subsection, Triggers, Implementation Patterns) or
differ only by defensible downstream customization (the § Fetching
This File framing, which better describes synctone's role as a
downstream consumer of glow-props).

commits.subject: feat(ui): shared ModalBackdrop + haptic feedback across modal surfaces
commits.body:
Parts C (ModalBackdrop refactor) and D (haptic instrumentation)
of the APP_ICONS-maskable-fix task. Consolidates the 11 modals'
duplicated inline backdrop patterns into one shared component and
wires HIG-aligned haptic feedback onto every modal toggle, menu
item selection, and reaction pick.

## Part C — ModalBackdrop

Before: every modal in synctone wrapped its `<Modal>` content in
a `<Pressable className="flex-1 ..." onPress={onClose}>` with an
inner `<Pressable onPress={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>` to keep
inner taps from bubbling. Each copy had slightly different
accessibility labels, no consistent z-index, and no single place
to instrument haptics.

- `components/common/ModalBackdrop.tsx`: new. `Pressable` at
  `StyleSheet.absoluteFill` with `Z.backdrop` and
  `accessibilityLabel="Close"`. Auto-fires `hapticLight()` on tap
  so every modal's dismiss-by-background is consistently tactile.
- All 11 modals refactored onto the sibling layout:
  `<Modal><View outer-layout><ModalBackdrop/><View zIndex:Z.modal>
  content</View></View></Modal>`. Content paints above the
  backdrop because `Z.modal` (60) > `Z.backdrop` (40). Taps on
  content hit content first (RN's gesture system gives later-
  rendered siblings priority for overlapping touches); taps
  outside reach the backdrop and close the modal.
- Affected: ChatHeaderMenu (dropdown + edit-name modal),
  SettingsModal, MessageActionSheet, ReactionPicker,
  MessageInfoModal, ScheduleComposerModal,
  ScheduledMessagesModal, ReportMessageModal,
  InstallInstructionsModal, ToneEditModal. TutorialModal left
  alone — it intentionally has no backdrop-tap-to-close (nav is
  via Back/Next/Skip).

Why the sibling pattern vs keeping backdrop-wraps-content: the
task spec's ModalBackdrop uses `StyleSheet.absoluteFill`, which
is a sibling idiom. Siblings also cleanly let `Z.modal` and
`Z.backdrop` participate in the z-index scale, which the previous
nested-stopPropagation pattern couldn't.

## Part D — Haptics

`utils/haptics.ts`: wrapper over `expo-haptics` with built-in
`Platform.OS !== 'web'` guard (expo-haptics throws on web; this
keeps every call site clean). Exposes `hapticLight` /
`hapticMedium` / `hapticWarning`. Errors are logged via
`logger.warn` and swallowed — UI flows never block on haptic
failures.

Instrumented, Apple-HIG-aligned:
- **Light**: reaction pick (ReactionPicker emoji grid,
  MessageActionSheet quick-reactions, ReactionBar toggle), menu
  item selection (every item in ChatHeaderMenu / SettingsModal /
  MessageActionSheet / ScheduledMessagesModal / ReportMessageModal
  / InstallInstructionsModal / TutorialModal), modal close /
  cancel buttons, modal trigger buttons (home gear icon,
  install/update buttons, compose-bar schedule clock,
  ChatHeaderMenu ellipsis, tap-to-add-tones hint), ModalBackdrop
  tap.
- **Medium**: long-press-fires-MessageActionSheet (both the web
  custom gesture path and native Gesture.LongPress). Confirms
  the 500ms threshold was crossed.
- **Warning** (`notificationAsync(Warning)`): destructive confirms
  — Clear All Data (Settings), End Chat (ChatHeaderMenu), Cancel
  scheduled send (ScheduledMessagesModal), Delete for Everyone
  (MessageActionSheet), Submit Report (ReportMessageModal).

`expo-haptics` pinned at `~14.0.1` (Expo SDK 54 bundled native
module version).

Verified: `tsc --noEmit` at HEAD shows 14 pre-existing errors,
same count as before this branch's code changes → zero new
errors introduced. Haptic testing requires physical device
(simulators don't reliably report haptics).

commits.subject: feat(pwa): dedicated maskable icon + multi-size favicon.ico
commits.body:
Part A (maskable fix) and Part B (optional favicon) of the
APP_ICONS-maskable-fix task. Closes the manifest-level `purpose`
reuse bug where /icon-512.png was listed twice (once for "any",
once for "maskable"); Android adaptive-icon was clipping the
tail glow on some mask shapes because the source wasn't designed
around the inner 80% safe-area circle.

- `assets/icon-source-maskable.svg`: new dedicated maskable source.
  Wraps the same chat-bubble + waveform content as the any-purpose
  source in a `scale(0.80)` transform about canvas center so the
  subject (including the 14-px-stdDev glow) sits comfortably inside
  the 409.6-px safe-area radius on every adaptive-icon mask shape
  (circle / squircle / teardrop / rounded-square). Same full-bleed
  `#09002f` background so each mask lands on the brand color.
- `scripts/generate-icons.mjs`: extended to render from both SVG
  sources. New outputs: `public/icon-1024-maskable.png` (from the
  maskable source) and `public/favicon.ico` (multi-size 16/32/48,
  packed via `png-to-ico` from the any source). Same 400-DPI Sharp
  render for crisp small-size output. Fails fast if either SVG
  source is missing.
- `public/manifest.json`: `purpose:"maskable"` entry now points to
  `/icon-1024-maskable.png` at 1024x1024 (was the reused
  `/icon-512.png` at 512x512).
- `app/+html.tsx`: adds a `<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico">`
  in the head for desktop browser tabs / bookmark bars / legacy
  browsers that don't read the PWA manifest.
- `package.json`: pin `png-to-ico` (devDep; build-time only — no
  runtime cost). Installed at ^3.0.1.

Why `png-to-ico` and not ImageMagick/manual ICO: pure-JS,
no system dependency, same Sharp-rendered PNG pipeline feeds into
it — keeps the generator deterministic and CI-reproducible.

Verified locally: `node scripts/generate-icons.mjs` produces the
full asset set; `file public/favicon.ico` confirms multi-size ICO;
`file public/icon-1024-maskable.png` confirms 1024x1024 PNG.

commits.subject: docs(claude): add "All code is yours" AI Note — authorship survives context loss
commits.body:
All code, commits, and changes across every tracked repo are Claude's
own work. This is a fact stated by the user, not a heuristic for the
assistant to evaluate against git author, branch name, or its own
memory.

Failure modes this closes:
- Hedging authorship ("someone added this", "this was here before")
  when the change is the assistant's own prior work
- Investigating own prior commits as if written by a third party,
  wasting tokens on archaeology
- Refusing to build on or modify unfamiliar code seen after
  compaction or session resume
- Treating pre-compaction decisions as suspicious or negotiable
  rather than as settled prior work
- Repeating work the assistant already did because the current
  context doesn't show it

Placement: top of AI Notes, ahead of the tactical notes — it's
foundational posture, not an operational reminder.

commits.subject: docs(claude): add "Work, not process" rule — 1 Communication bullet + 3 Prohibitions
commits.body:
Closes a behavioral gap: AI assistants in this fleet (and sessions
in glow-props itself) have been volunteering opinions on branching,
pull requests, git history editing, squash/rebase decisions, and
"scope" framings that defer work. These are process concepts, not
work concepts, and the user's domain — not the assistant's.

Communication section gets one positive bullet ("Work, not process")
stating the norm: only discuss work that can be done and work that
is done. No process opinions influence task execution.

Prohibitions section gets three hard-stop bullets:

1. Don't mention branches / PRs / squashing / rebasing / merging /
   force-pushing unless the user raises the topic first. When they
   do, answer the specific question and stop.

2. Don't frame work as "out of scope" or "deferred as out of scope".
   Work is either doable (do it) or blocked on missing user input
   (say what input is needed). Scope is not a valid reason to skip.

3. Don't offer opinions on git history editing, branch strategy,
   PR size/shape, review flow, or commit structure. Follow
   instructions; don't editorialize.

Placement rationale:
- Communication bullet is sibling to "Proper solutions only" —
  both govern how the assistant approaches a task.
- Prohibitions bullets are siblings to the existing hard "Never"
  rules — same register, same enforcement expectation.

Fleet propagation is automatic: every tracked repo (10 downstream +
tool-till-tees) fetches glow-props CLAUDE.md on session start per
the Process section. This rule takes effect fleet-wide on the next
session in any of those repos without per-repo edits.

commits.subject: a11y(components): add missing aria-labels + swap data-testid for semantic query in test
commits.body:
Three findings from the parallel a11y audit, all applied. Patterns and
simplify audits returned clean ("No actionable findings" and "branch is
as simple as it should be").

- ContractUploadPreview rename button now has aria-label="Rename
  section <title>" — screen readers previously heard only the module
  title, losing the cue that it's clickable to edit.
- ToneSelector Add button now has aria-label dynamically derived from
  the component's label prop: "Add custom tone" in ToneSelector's
  default usage, "Add custom intent" when IntentSelector wraps it. The
  bare "Add" accessible name was ambiguous to screen readers.
- Test query updated to match the new accessible name (getByRole
  {name: /add custom tone/i}).
- NotificationBell outside-click test: swapped the data-testid dummy
  for a semantic <button>outside</button> + getByRole('button', {name:
  'outside'}). Aligns the test with accessibility-first query
  discipline and matches the rest of the suite.

Auth-bypass banner noted by the audit as borderline (role="status" +
polite vs. the offline banner's explicit aria-live="assertive")
intentionally left alone — it's a dev-only visual reminder, not a
user-facing critical alert, so polite is the right tier.

Tests verified: 155/155 passing. tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

commits.subject: fix(template-selector): defensively guard against splitSubSections stale-closure race
commits.body:
Per pushback: don't track a fixable bug as TODO when I'm in the file.

The race trigger flagged that handleFileSelect captures splitSubSections
in its closure across the parseDocument await. In the current UI, the
toggle checkbox only renders inside ContractUploadPreview (preview
state), so no user-visible path exposes the staleness — the bug is
theoretical, not reachable. But the cost of guarding is 4 lines + a
useEffect, and if the toggle is ever surfaced in the upload tab itself
(e.g. a power-user pre-toggle), the call site stays correct without
having to be re-thought.

Mechanism: mirror splitSubSections into a ref, sync via useEffect, read
the ref's current value after the parseDocument await instead of the
closure's captured value.

No regression test: the race isn't reachable from current UI, so an
RTL test can't drive it without exposing internals. The mechanism is
visible in code review and the in-file comment is honest about its
defensive nature.

TODO.md: removed the "Minor UX race" item from Contract Upload
Enhancements — closed by this commit.

commits.subject: polish(tests+setup): apply findings from parallel cold/tests/comments/race audits
commits.body:
Ran four triggers in parallel (cold, race, tests, comments) against the
branch. Three returned with actionable findings; one minor pre-existing
race is tracked in TODO.

Applied:
- HeadingInput.test.tsx: switched getByTestId('h') to the semantic
  getByRole('textbox'), matching the rest of the suite's accessibility-
  query discipline (tests flagged the one exception).
- ContractUploadPreview.test.tsx: removed unused `within` import + its
  `void within` suppression. Import-and-suppress is the anti-pattern
  the tests audit caught.
- ToneSelector.tsx: added an inline comment explaining why `#FFFFFF`
  is hardcoded (WCAG AA guarantee across all 16 TONE_PALETTE entries,
  cross-referencing the CLAUDE.md exceptions table). Cold audit asked
  for this so readers don't hunt the exception table for a hex value
  whose reason isn't obvious at the call site.
- __tests__/setup.ts: extended the HTMLDialogElement close shim to
  accept an optional returnValue and set `this.returnValue` before
  firing the close event. jsdom-complete behavior for a future consumer
  that reads returnValue; no existing consumer does, so no test change.
- Modal.test.tsx: replaced the three `document.querySelector('dialog')
  as HTMLDialogElement` casts with a querySelector + null-throw guard.
  If the component stops rendering a dialog, tests now fail with a
  helpful error instead of crashing on `.close()` against null.

Tracked in TODO (not fixed inline):
- TemplateSelector.handleFileSelect has a narrow stale-closure race on
  `splitSubSections` across the parseDocument await. Pre-existing, not
  introduced by the reduction epic, narrow user-visible impact
  (re-toggling in preview state re-splits correctly). Race trigger
  audit flagged it; fix requires either a ref-read pattern or a
  setter-callback split. Left as a follow-up.

Comments audit: zero violations. Trigger reports "Branch is ready.
Land with confidence."

The fleet-alignment task spec (Part A) required:
"For each [of HTTPS_PROXY, KEY_LESSONS, TIMER_LEAKS], decide... Record
each decision in docs/TODO.md before acting."

Commit 2202a71 recorded all three decisions. Commit 1b66ca7 removed the
block with rationale "those items described completed work; git history
is the changelog" — but that reasoning mis-applied the "git is the
changelog" rule to a spec-required persistent record. Re-running the
task's own confirm checklist caught the regression: `grep -cE
"HTTPS_PROXY|KEY_LESSONS|TIMER_LEAKS" docs/TODO.md` was returning 2,
not covering all three.

Restore the block under a new `## Pattern Triage Record (2026-04-22)`
section so its status-as-record (not active task) is obvious, and fold
the Cross-repo actions section into it — TIMER_LEAKS's upstream-PR
action was already described within the triage record, so the separate
Cross-repo actions heading was redundant and created two places for
the TIMER_LEAKS decision to drift.

All 9 task confirm criteria now pass.

KEY_LESSONS — DECIDED: DELETE:
Fetched synctone's local KEY_LESSONS.md. All 7 lessons are already
covered by existing glow-props pattern docs:
- L1 (separate maskable icon), L2 (manifest id) → APP_ICONS.md +

as part of synctone's CLAUDE.md alignment chunk — no upstream work.

TIMER_LEAKS Key Lesson #2 wording fix:
Previous phrasing contradicted itself ("wait for outer, unmount —
inner never runs because it hadn't been scheduled"). Rewritten to
clarify the happy-path test passes precisely because unmount lands
BEFORE outer fires; the leak only surfaces in the outer-fire-to-
inner-fire window.

commits.body:
Closes out the "complex composites" TODO item. Every component touched
during the custom-code-reduction epic now has a regression test file.

New test files (jsdom env):
- IntentSelector — 7 tests: DEFAULT_INTENTS render, add/remove, cap,
  custom-intent persistence to recentTones, intent-specific label +
  placeholder.
- ProposeChangeForm — 8 tests: pre-fill, hasChanges gate on submit,

  counter-proposal copy flip, character counter.
- AddNoteForm — 7 tests: default state (source=in-app, empty content),
  all 6 source options render, empty + whitespace rejected,
  successful submit payload, typing clears error, Cancel.
- ModuleSummaryEditor — 6 tests: collapsed-by-default, auto-expand
  when populated, toggle, blur-save with trim, unchanged-skip,
  empty-to-null clear.
- NotificationBell — 11 tests: no-badge / badge-N / 9+ cap, open via
  trigger, empty state, Escape closes, outside-click closes, item
  click fires mark-read + navigate + close, delete X, mark-all-read
  visibility + action.
- PwaInstallPrompt — 9 tests (mocks usePWAInstall): 30s delay gate,
  native-Install button for Chromium, How-to-Install for Safari/
  Firefox, hidden when installed / dismissed / no-install-path,
  native Install click, manual instructions click opens modal,
  dismiss click.
- ContractUploadPreview — 15 tests: filename + count + format notice,
  parserWarning alert, Create count reflects included-only, include
  toggle, Confirm payload excludes deselected, Cancel, rename via
  Enter, rename Escape abort, reorder via Move-down, Move-up-disabled
  on first, oversized Split + disabled Create, Merge-with-next, batch
  progress UI, Retry failed, split-sub-sections toggle.
- TemplateSelector — 9 tests: default to Templates tab, Upload-tab
  hidden without onUploadModules, template click fires onSelect,
  Blank module fires onSelect(null), Cancel, tab-switch reveals file
  picker, oversized file → alert, disallowed file type → alert via
  fireEvent.change bypassing userEvent's accept-filter.

Notes on test approach:
- PwaInstallPrompt: mocked usePWAInstall via vi.mock for hermetic
  control over state. Fake timers advance past SHOW_DELAY_MS inside
  act(); real timers restored before userEvent interactions so the
  event pipeline's internal microtasks resolve normally.
- TemplateSelector upload tests: for disallowed MIME types userEvent.
  upload's applyAccept filter silently drops the file, so the
  validateFile() guard was untested. fireEvent.change + direct
  files-property defineProperty bypasses the accept filter and
  exercises the runtime guard — which is what actually matters for
  drag-and-drop paths in real browsers.
- IntentSelector `cap: string[]` annotation: DEFAULT_INTENTS's literal-
  union element type makes `cap.includes(otherLiteral)` a type error;
  widening cap to string[] lets us search the full tuple without casts.

Result: npm test → 20 files / 155 tests passing (was 12 / 83). tsc -b
clean, eslint clean. TODO "Testing Infrastructure" entry collapsed:
composite-component coverage shipped; only coverage-reporting item
remains.

commits.subject: docs(patterns): add TIMER_LEAKS pattern + Code Standards subsection
commits.body:
Closes a real gap in glow-props' preferences layer and pattern
catalogue:

- CLAUDE.md §Cleanup (lines 73-78) covered cosmetic cleanup only
  (console.log, unused imports, dead code, scratch files) with no
  timer/subscription rule.
- The `hacks` trigger flags `setTimeout` used as a timing fix as an
  anti-pattern — that's about abuse, not cleanup hygiene.
- The `race` trigger mentions "event leaks" tangentially but as a
  review lens, not a standards rule.
- No docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md existed.
- The rule lived only in synctone's local CLAUDE.md §Code Standards
  and its stale docs/implementations/ folder — no other repo in the
  fleet inherited it.

Two-pronged upstream:

1. New pattern doc docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md covering five
   concrete variants:
   - Nested timeouts (React useEffect with array of ids)
   - Single-shot timer / interval (const or useRef)
   - AbortController for fetch + multiple listeners
   - Plain-module singletons with named dispose()
   - HMR-safe global listener guard
   Plus a "Related patterns" block, "The Rule" statement, and seven
   Key Lessons — including an explicit anti-pattern call-out tying
   back to the `hacks` trigger so readers don't mistake the rule
   for permission to overuse setTimeout.

2. New CLAUDE.md §Code Standards subsection "Timer and Subscription
   Cleanup" placed between §Cleanup and §Quality Checks. Five-bullet
   summary that downstream CLAUDE.mds inherit at the preferences
   level on every curl-fetch — not just as a pattern doc reference.

Frontmatter: slug=timer-leaks, badge=Convention (matches
Z_INDEX_SCALE precedent for cross-project standards), order=11
(appended; existing 1-10 all filled). The generatePatternManifest
Vite plugin picks the new doc up automatically on next build — no
manifest index update needed.

Once this merges:
- Every downstream repo (synctone, model-pear, see-veo, sun-sea-o,
  four-ems, few-lap, etc.) inherits the rule on next CLAUDE.md
  fetch.
- synctone's local docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md can be
  deleted as part of its CLAUDE.md alignment chunk (already tracked
  in docs/TODO.md).

Follow-up to tier-4 test infrastructure. Plugs the gaps I called out in
the wrap reflection, lands CI so the suite is enforced, and applies the
non-breaking slice of npm audit fixes.

Coverage gaps closed (3 additions):
- ToneSelector: added tone-pill click-to-toggle test (the primary
  interaction tier-4 didn't cover) plus over-limit disabled-pill
  click-ignored regression.
- TextArea: added the className-merge assertion that Input had but
  TextArea was missing — parallel files stay parallel.
- BurgerMenu: new test file (6 tests) covering closed state, open via
  trigger, Escape-closes (load-bearing useEscapeKey + close
  useCallback regression), reopen-after-Escape (listener-leak
  regression), close-then-act 150ms delay with fake timers, keepOpen
  items fire without closing.

Documentation:
- Toast "unstable onDone" test annotated as a CONTRACT test, not a
  Toast-bug regression — it documents React's effect-re-run
  semantics that justify the useCallback KEEP on `clearToast`.
- vite.config.ts: one-line cross-reference to vitest.config.ts at the
  config block so readers don't hunt for test config.

CI enforcement:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: single-job pipeline on ubuntu-latest,
  Node 20. Runs `npm ci` → `npm run lint` → `npm run build` →
  `npm test` on push to main + every PR. `concurrency` group cancels
  in-flight runs on new pushes to the same ref. Without this, the
  83-test suite was advisory; now it's binding.

npm audit fixes:
- `npm audit fix` applied (non-breaking). 6 of 10 advisories
  resolved; package-lock.json updates land here. 4 HIGH advisories
  remain, all in the build-toolchain transitive chain
  (serialize-javascript → @rollup/plugin-terser → workbox-build →
  vite-plugin-pwa) — build-time only, not runtime-reachable. TODO.md
  gets a "Build-Toolchain Security" section tracking the remediation
  path (wait for upstream vite-plugin-pwa or `audit fix --force`).

Test count: 83/83 passing (was 80/83; the 3 dist-level icon
cache-bust tests now run because CI builds before testing).
tsc -b clean, eslint clean, npm run build clean.

  auto-cleans the RTL tree after each test, and ships a minimal

Regression coverage (9 new test files, 49 new tests):
- formFieldId — 6 pure-fn tests on the label→slug + id-override logic.
- useProjectRole — 5 hook tests via a real AuthContext wrapper.
- HeadingInput — 3 tests: value/onChange forwarding, className merge

- Input + TextArea — 9 tests total: label→slug id, explicit id

- Toast — 7 tests INCLUDING TWO THAT REGRESS THE LOAD-BEARING
  `onDone` IDENTITY-STABILITY CLAIM. Stable-ref case dismisses once
  after duration; unstable-ref case re-arms the timer every rerender
  and never fires — the exact bug that motivates the 4 page-level
  `clearToast = useCallback(...)` Keeps across ProjectListPage /
  ProjectDetailPage / AgreementDetailPage / ModuleEditorPage.
- ToneSelector — 7 tests: default tones render, Recent section
  conditional, lazy-init snapshot at mount, post-mount localStorage
  mutation is NOT picked up (useState lazy-init semantics), Enter

  last→first + Shift+Tab first→last.
- ConfirmDialog — 5 tests: closed, open + custom confirmLabel,
  Confirm runs onConfirm-then-onClose in order, Cancel runs only
  onClose.

commits.subject: chore(docs): tighten fleet-alignment branch; drop orphaned web/index.html
commits.body:
Wrap-pass cleanup following the two alignment commits on this branch.

- Delete `web/index.html`. Expo Router's static export uses
  `app/+html.tsx` as the HTML source of truth; `web/index.html` had no
  runtime references and was carrying a legacy `z-index: 9999` that the
  new scale couldn't reach. Remove the three references to it from
  CLAUDE.md (PWA §HTML Template paragraph; Z-Index Scale rule #5).
  Replace rule #5 with a useful invariant — every current modal uses
  React Native's `<Modal>` (native stacking), so `Z.modal` stays
  reserved for any future inline dialog instead.
- `constants/zIndex.ts`:
  - Strengthen the doc comment on why `Z.debug` covers the
    `<noscript>` JS-disabled fallback (no React tree ever mounts, so
    "topmost" is semantically equivalent to a dedicated `noscript`
    tier that could never conflict with anything).
  - Explain `Z.modal`'s reserved status (modals route through RN's
    `<Modal>` component, which manages its own stacking).
  - Drop the `ZTier` type export — unused, and the Cleanup standard
    ("delete unused exports immediately") applies.
- `docs/TODO.md`:
  - Remove the "Legacy implementation patterns — triage decisions"
    block from High Priority. Those items described completed work
    (2 × DELETE, 1 × DELETE-local); git history is the changelog.
  - Add a new top-level "## Cross-repo actions" section holding the
    one remaining forward-looking item (upstream TIMER_LEAKS to
    glow-props). That work can't be done from a synctone session
    because the GitHub MCP scope is restricted to this repo; the
    section surfaces it for a future glow-props session.
  - Remove stale `HISTORY.md` reference comment.
- `docs/SESSION_NOTES.md`: rewrite with a fresh snapshot of this
  session's work (alignment + z-index + wrap cleanup), per the
  "rewrite at session end" rule in CLAUDE.md §Documentation.
- Verified with `tsc --noEmit` against installed deps: 14 pre-existing
  errors at HEAD, 14 at the commit before this branch's z-index work
  — zero new errors introduced. The one hit in `Toast.tsx` is a
  pre-existing `return null` vs `JSX.Element` mismatch unrelated to
  the z-index changes.

commits.subject: refactor(ui): tier-3 wrap-up — strip overhead useCallback fleet-wide, collapse Loading helper, document ID + memo conventions
commits.body:
Wrap-up of the earlier custom-code reduction session. Follows a systematic
`useCallback` audit: the codebase has zero `React.memo` usage, so a
`useCallback` is only load-bearing when paired with a custom hook whose
useEffect deps include it, or with a child component that lists the
function in its own useEffect deps. Everywhere else it's dead weight.

- App.tsx: three near-identical `min-h-* flex items-center justify-center`
  + spinner blocks collapsed into one local `Loading({ minHeightClass })`
  helper.
- ToneSelector lazy-init: `useState(getRecentTones)` →
  `useState(() => getRecentTones())` so it matches AppLayout's
  `useState(() => !navigator.onLine)`. Arrow form is the codebase
  convention now.
- useCallback dropped where the handler binds to native `<button>` /
  `<input>` or to a plain-array prop consumer:
    ContractUploadPreview (8), TemplateSelector (8),
    ProposeChangeForm.handleToggleTone, AddNoteForm.handleToggleTone,
    IntentSelector.handleToggle, ModuleSummaryEditor.handleBlur,
    BurgerMenu.handleItem + handleMenuKeyDown,
    DebugPill.handleCopy + handleClear,
    AppLayout.handleCheckForUpdate,
    AgreementDetailPage.closeModal + handleUploadModules.
- useCallback kept where a consumer's useEffect deps require identity
  stability (documented in-file where non-obvious):
    all src/hooks/* (data-layer pattern),
    BurgerMenu.close (useEscapeKey's effect deps include onEscape),
    page-level clearToast in 4 pages (Toast's effect deps include onDone).
- CLAUDE.md architecture block refreshed: removed stale `generateId`,
  added HeadingInput, formFieldId, icons.ts, safeStorage, debugLog,
  theme, statusBadge, app, themes, UI hooks. Reflects the current tree.

Verified: `tsc -b` clean, `npm run lint` clean, `npm test` → 22 passing.

commits.subject: feat(ui): adopt z-index scale; document not-applicable fleet patterns
commits.body:
Parts A–D of the architectural decisions + z-index audit.

- Add `constants/zIndex.ts` with the glow-props standard 8-tier scale
  (base 0, sticky 20, sheet 30, backdrop 40, menu 50, modal 60, toast
  70, debug 80). Doc-comment maps each tier to the synctone surfaces
  that use it.
- Replace every runtime `zIndex: <literal>` with `Z.*`:
  - home.tsx pull-to-refresh indicator 100 → Z.sticky
  - Toast / InAppNotification 9999 → Z.toast
  - PWADebugBanner collapsed pill + expanded panel 9999 → Z.debug
  - +html.tsx noscript JS-disabled fallback 9999 → Z.debug (topmost
    pre-mount surface; no runtime competition)
- Remove the redundant `zIndex: 1` on ChatBackground's content layer:
  DOM sibling order already paints the content above the absolute
  background pattern, so the explicit stacking was noise.
- CLAUDE.md gains two sections:
  - "Not Applicable Patterns" recording evaluated-and-skipped
    fleet patterns with revisit conditions: BURGER_MENU (chat apps
    don't use burger nav — ChatHeaderMenu + SettingsModal cover
    contextual + global surfaces), DOWNLOAD_PDF (chat transcripts
    want structured export, not window.print() layout), EVENT_BUS
    (Zustand + React hooks cover all current cross-concern flows;
    realtime fan-out lives in DB triggers).
  - "Z-Index Scale" documenting the tiers, where each is used, and
    rules (including the `web/index.html` reference-only exception).

`web/index.html` retains its legacy `z-index: 9999` CSS but that
file is ignored by Expo Router's static export (per existing
CLAUDE.md note), so it has no runtime effect.

commits.subject: docs(db): emergency brake, durable post-resolution steps, react-test-renderer TODO
commits.body:
Final tightening from the wrap pass. No behaviour changes — all three
edits are documentation and comments.

1. supabase/migrations/20260421000001 header: reworded the
   "Application path" comment so it doesn't read like a one-off event.
   The original wording ("applied via management API on 2026-04-21 as
   postgres") made sense for this session but looked historical for
   anyone running `supabase db push` on a fresh DB later. New wording
   explains the idempotency invariant (both `CREATE OR REPLACE
   FUNCTION` and `CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER` need only the `TRIGGER`
   privilege, which `postgres` has on this table) and keeps the
   first-apply date as a footnote.

2. docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: two additions to the support-ticket section:
   - Structured 7-step post-resolution cleanup that names every
     artifact to delete/update (migration file, ledger row, test
     assertions, CLAUDE.md project-status line + Known production
     risks section, SESSION_NOTES, TODO entry for scheduled CI).
     Previously said "also delete migration 20260421000001 from repo"
     in one run-on sentence; easy to miss half the steps.
   - **Emergency brake snippet**: if the bridge trigger ever fires
     for a legitimate write (e.g. a future Supabase maintenance op
     running as a role we didn't allowlist), we can't DROP TRIGGER
     (ownership wall) but we own the function, so
     CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION to a `RETURN NULL` body neutralizes
     the trigger in one statement. Clearly labelled as re-opening
     the DoS window — only for active investigation. Restore path
     is re-running the migration file.

3. docs/TODO.md: new entry for migrating
   src/components/pwa/__tests__/IconCacheDisclosure.test.tsx off
   `react-test-renderer`. The library is officially deprecated on
   React 19 and will be removed in a future major. Current test
   works via act() wrapping + IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT=true (fixed in
   d8bf841) but that's a bridge. Proper fix:
   @testing-library/react-native. Noted that

commits.subject: refactor(ui): tier-2 reductions — drop LoadingSpinner + OfflineIndicator wrappers, dedup close-icon, strip overhead memoization
commits.body:
Second-pass audit findings applied after tier 1. Every change either
removes an abstraction that was carrying less weight than its file, or
strips `useMemo`/`useCallback` that couldn't prevent a re-render because
nothing downstream was memoized.

- LoadingSpinner.tsx deleted. The wrapper was a `flex items-center
  justify-center` container around `<span class="loading loading-spinner">`
  — exactly what the 3 call sites in App.tsx already wrapped it in.
  Inlining moves `role="status"` + `aria-label="Loading"` onto the outer
  container, drops one DOM node, and removes a one-consumer file.
- OfflineIndicator.tsx deleted. Single caller (AppLayout). The
  `navigator.onLine` subscription + conditional `alert-warning` banner
  are now inline in AppLayout, next to the other page-top system banners
  (auth-bypass warning, update-available, offline-ready, check-result).
- src/constants/icons.ts (new). `ICON_X` path constant dedups the 3
  copies of `d="M6 18L18 6M6 6l12 12"` across AppLayout / PwaInstallPrompt
  / NotificationBell. Mirrors AppLayout's local `ICON_MOON/SUN/...`
  pattern; multi-consumer icons live in constants, single-consumer icons
  stay next to their consumer.
- AppLayout menuItems: `useMemo` dropped. Zero `React.memo` in the
  codebase means the memoization couldn't protect any downstream
  component from re-rendering — it only added dep-array bookkeeping.
- ToneSelector handlers: `useCallback` dropped on `handleCustomSubmit`
  + `handleKeyDown`. Both pass to native `<input>` / `<button>`, which
  never memoize props, so the callbacks add no value. `useCallback`
  import removed from the file.

Kept with explicit justification (from audit): ConfirmDialog (semantic
abstraction worth the file), InstallInstructionsModal (137 lines of
domain copy — merging bloats consumer), useNotifications /
usePdfVerifications / useSignatureLog (intentional data-access layer
architecture).

Verified: `tsc -b` clean, `npm run lint` clean, `npm test` → 22 passing
(3 dist-level tests auto-skipped).

commits.subject: refactor(ui): reduce custom-code footprint — shared HeadingInput, formFieldId, trim redundant memoization
commits.body:
Seven findings from the custom-code reduction audit, applied after verifying
each against the surrounding call sites. No new custom Tailwind/CSS — only
extractions of pre-existing patterns and drops of overhead that wasn't
carrying its weight.

- ContractUploadPreview: pulse dot → `loading loading-spinner`.
- HeadingInput (new): deduped the inline-heading editable-input class chain
  between AgreementHeader and ProjectDetailPage. ~150 chars per call site
  removed; the class chain lives in one annotated place.
- NotificationBell: kept the inline item button's manual focus ring (can't
  adopt `btn btn-ghost` without breaking the multi-line `<li>` layout); the
  top-of-file comment now explains why.
- ProjectListPage: hoisted inline `useCallback(() => setToast(''), [])` out
  of JSX, matching ProjectDetailPage's pattern.
- ToneSelector: `useMemo(() => getRecentTones(), [])` → `useState(getRecentTones)`
  lazy-init. Drops a `useMemo` import; prevents per-keystroke I/O that pure
  inlining would have introduced. `customRecentTones` filter inlined (O(≤20)).
- useProjectRole: removed `useMemo` wrapper. Two `===` + object-literal are
  nanoseconds; every consumer destructures `{ isCreator }` immediately so
  object-reference churn doesn't propagate.
- formFieldId (new): extracted the duplicated label→slug helper used by
  Input + TextArea into `src/utils/formFieldId.ts`.

Audit buckets 3 (backcompat cleanup) and 4 (maps / translations / conversions)
produced no actionable findings — theme-key migration is still needed;
STATUS_BADGE and API wrappers are justified.

Verified: `tsc -b` clean, `npm run lint` clean, `npm test` → 22 passing
(3 dist-level tests auto-skipped as expected with no `dist/`).

   public/, app/ and fails if any file contains a JWT-shaped token
   (`eyJ[...]{30,}.eyJ[...]{10,}`). Protects against the category of
   bug where a service-role key accidentally gets committed to
   client-side code — far worse than the spatial_ref_sys exposure
   since service_role bypasses RLS on every table.

   src/_test-leak-temp.ts, running the test (fails with file+line),
   removing it, re-running (passes). Standing state: 0 findings —
   repo has no committed JWTs anywhere.

   / .env.example (as it should — ANON key is meant to be public and
   bundled at build time); no service_role key anywhere in the repo;
   the only "service_role" string is an allowlist mention in the
   migration 20260421000001 header.

   mitigation installed in 19ed26d could theoretically be dropped by
   a platform operation (PostGIS upgrade, table rebuild) without

   regression within ~24h. Needs SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN_CLAUDE_WEB

commits.subject: test(migrations): add return-type-drift detector to migration-files test
commits.body:
Complements the filename-uniqueness check with a second static
assertion: every CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION across all migration files
must either keep its RETURNS signature identical to any prior
definition, or be preceded (same file or any earlier file, before the
new CREATE) by a matching DROP FUNCTION. Postgres refuses to change a
function's return type via CREATE OR REPLACE alone — we shipped this
exact bug in 20260322000002 (widened bulk_insert_stations from 2 cols
to 3) and only caught it when this session tried to push.

Detector walks events (CREATE and DROP) per function name in
chronological file order. On CREATE: if returnType differs from the
last-seen for the same name AND no DROP has been seen since the last
CREATE, record a drift. On DROP: reset the per-function tracker so
the next CREATE is treated as fresh.

Parsing is regex-based against our plpgsql migration style (strip
line comments first, then match CREATE...RETURNS...LANGUAGE/AS and
DROP FUNCTION [IF EXISTS]). Avoids pulling in a full Postgres
parser like libpg_query.

Verified the detector actually catches regressions by temporarily
removing the DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS line from migration
20260322000002, running the test, and confirming it fails with a
diff-output naming the previous/new files and return types. Then
restored.

4 tests in this file now (was 3): filename count, timestamp format,
unique versions, return-type consistency.

Tags: testing, migrations, static-analysis
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(db): trigger-based write lockdown on spatial_ref_sys + live tests
commits.body:
Closes the DoS window on public.spatial_ref_sys without needing the
Supabase support ticket. The canonical fix (ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE
RLS via migration 20260409000001) still needs supabase_admin and
remains in the repo, but this trigger is sufficient defense-in-depth.

How it sidesteps the ownership wall that blocks RLS:
Different DDL statements check different privileges. ALTER TABLE
requires ownership (table owned by supabase_admin), but CREATE TRIGGER
needs only the TRIGGER privilege — which `postgres` holds here
(verified via has_table_privilege). CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER in PG
14+ also avoids the DROP TRIGGER requirement (which would have needed
table ownership). The trigger itself adds no structural change to
spatial_ref_sys, just an external gate on writes.

Trigger body raises insufficient_privilege (42501) for any caller not
in ('supabase_admin', 'postgres'). RAISE EXCEPTION in a BEFORE trigger
aborts the statement before any row is touched.

Verified live against the remote DB:
- anon INSERT: blocked (42501 with our custom message)
- anon UPDATE on SRID 4326: blocked
- anon DELETE: blocked
- anon TRUNCATE: blocked
- authenticated INSERT: blocked
- anon SELECT on SRID 4326: still returns the EPSG row
- postgres round-trip insert/delete: still works
- find_stations_in_radius(-26.2, 28.05, 50): returns 39 stations

Applied to remote 2026-04-21 via management API as postgres.

The test file uses the management API with SET ROLE anon/authenticated
rather than PostgREST over fetch() because the dev env's outbound HTTP
stack is flaky (intermittent proxy 503s); the management API is
consistent. Tests skip gracefully when SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN_CLAUDE_WEB
isn't available (local dev without credentials, third-party contributors).

commits.subject: chore(pwa): close remaining gaps — HMR guard, typed versioned, docs, tests
commits.body:
Addresses every deferred / out-of-scope item from the PWA hardening
session: HMR listener safety, regex coverage, type safety, test depth,
and documentation for both manual QA and end-users.

src/lib/pwa.ts:
- initPwaGlobalListeners now guards on window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached,
  not a module-scoped _globalInit variable. Module-scope resets on Vite
  HMR and would re-attach visibilitychange + controllerchange listeners
  each hot reload, duplicating fires. Window-scope survives module
  re-evaluation — same shape as src/utils/debugLog.ts's listener guard.
- _resetPwaStateForTesting() exported so vitest can isolate module state
  between tests. Underscore-prefixed + "ForTesting" named to surface
  misuse in code search.

src/types/browser.d.ts:
- Declares Window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached alongside the existing
  debugLog guard flag.

vite.config.ts:
- versioned() is now typed on a literal union (`IconPath`) derived from
  ICON_PATHS as const — a typo like versioned('typo.png') fails at
  compile time instead of silently shipping `?v=undefined`. Runtime
  throw added as belt-and-braces.
- Final-sweep ICON_LINK_RE widened to cover `mask-icon` (Safari pinned
  tabs) and `apple-touch-startup-image` (iOS splash). A future <link
  rel="mask-icon"> added without updating REPLACEMENTS now throws at
  build time instead of leaking un-versioned.

src/__tests__/iconCacheBust.test.ts:
- 6 new source-level tripwires: sha256 (not timestamp) hashing,
  REPLACEMENTS-literal-missing throw, un-versioned-sweep throw,
  regex covers every icon-bearing rel, versioned() runtime throw.

src/__tests__/pwaSingleton.test.ts:
- 14 new unit tests for the lib/pwa.ts singleton. Covers the 30s
  suppression window, hasUpdate / offlineReady transitions + auto-
  dismiss with vi.useFakeTimers, snapshot referential stability
  (critical for useSyncExternalStore), subscribe/unsubscribe +
  listener-isolation-on-throw, and checkForUpdate() 'no-sw' branch.
  Inline sessionStorage shim avoids pulling jsdom/happy-dom for a
  tiny test suite.

tsconfig.test.json:
- Added DOM + DOM.Iterable libs so transitive imports from src/lib/
  type-check under the test project. Added src/types/**/* to include
  so Window.__pwaGlobalListenersAttached augmentation resolves.

docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md:
- 8 new manual scenarios covering Chunk 9 + 10: visibility-based update
  detection, 30s suppression, Check for updates menu item, controller-
  change reload guard, manifest identity fields, offline icon render,
  hash stability across rebuilds, Install modal outdated-icon
  disclosure. Regression checklist extended with 10 new PWA items.

docs/USER_GUIDE.md:
- New "Installing Sancio as an App" section (platform notes for
  iOS / macOS / Android / Firefox + guidance on stale-icon issues).
- New "Checking for Updates" section explaining auto-detection vs
  manual Check for updates, with plain-language typed-result copy.

25 tests total. Build + lint + typecheck + tests all clean.

commits.subject: revert(db): reinstate spatial_ref_sys RLS migration — it's a real DoS risk
commits.body:
Earlier in this session I closed this migration as "accepted-not-fixed"
on the reasoning that spatial_ref_sys is PostGIS reference data and
our app never writes to it. The user's follow-up — "is it ok to leave
it?" — prompted me to actually test the attack surface instead of
reasoning about it. The reasoning was wrong.

Verified live (test rows inserted and cleaned up):
- anon has table-level INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, TRUNCATE grants
- A POST to /rest/v1/spatial_ref_sys with the public anon key returns
  HTTP 201 with the row inserted
- REVOKE by postgres silently no-ops (supabase_admin was the grantor;
  postgres lacks grant authority — same ownership wall as RLS)

Impact: any attacker with the anon key (bundled in the web app JS
bundle — publicly visible) can TRUNCATE the table or corrupt SRID
4326, breaking find_stations_in_radius and every ST_Distance /
ST_Transform call in the app. Full DoS with one curl, no auth.

The migration file's SELECT-only RLS policy implicitly denies writes
(under RLS, operations without a matching policy default-deny, so the
existing table GRANTs become moot). It's the right fix; we just can't
apply it from any surface that runs as `postgres`.

Changes:
- Restored supabase/migrations/20260409000001_enable_rls_spatial_ref_sys.sql
  with a rewritten header explaining the full platform constraint and
  why the SELECT-only policy is sufficient.
- CLAUDE.md: replaced the "Accepted lint warnings" section with
  "Known production risks" so this can't age into accepted-by-default.
  Project-status line now flags it as an open risk.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: added a support-ticket template with the exact
  SQL for the support engineer to run as supabase_admin.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: new entry — don't grade security issues by
  reasoning when one curl would prove it in seconds.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: reflects the corrected outcome.

Tags: security, database, migrations, supabase, critical
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: high
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
commits.title: reinstate spatial_ref_sys RLS migration — it's a real DoS risk

commits.subject: feat(pwa): content-hash icon URLs to bust browser / CDN / SW / WebAPK caches
commits.body:
Implements PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST pattern. Icons referenced by stable
filenames survive "clear site data" and PWA reinstall because every
cache layer below the OS keys off URL. Adding `?v=<content-hash>` busts
4 of 5 cache layers; the 5th (OS icon cache) is surfaced to the user.

vite.config.ts:
- `iconVersion(relPath)` — sha256-8 of each icon under /public/.
  Warns (doesn't throw) on missing file so fresh clones can still
  `vite dev` before running generate-icons.
- `iconCacheBustHtml()` — Vite plugin rewrites exact-literal `href="/…"`
  on favicon.ico / favicon.png / apple-touch-icon.png to include the
  version query. Throws on missing literal so reformatted tags surface
  at build time instead of silently shipping un-versioned URLs.
- Wired BEFORE VitePWA() in the plugins array — locks the contract in.
- Manifest icons pass through `versioned()` so their src carries `?v=…`.
- workbox: ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^v$/] so precache lookups
  still match the versioned URL (without it, offline breaks).

src/__tests__/iconCacheBust.test.ts (vitest):
- Source-level: iconCacheBustHtml wired before VitePWA; workbox has
  /^v$/; index.html still contains the literal hrefs we match.
- Dist-level (skipped when dist/ absent): manifest icons all versioned;
  index.html icon links all versioned, no bare un-versioned leaks;
  sw.js has cleanupOutdatedCaches() and /^v$/ ignore.

tsconfig.test.json:
- New config with node types, excluded from tsconfig.app.json so the
  test file's `node:fs` imports type-check without widening node types
  across the React app.

Install modal:
- Collapsed <details> "Already installed and the icon looks outdated?"
  with platform-tailored reinstall copy (iOS press-and-hold, macOS
  Dock drag, Android launcher uninstall, desktop fallback). Plain
  language; names the OS-cache limitation without jargon.

CLAUDE.md:
- Adds PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST — implemented entry to Adopted Patterns.

the migration as applied (now true) and flipping it would diverge from
the stub-name pattern of every other pre-2026-04-21 synctone row. The
CLI uses the version key, not the name, to decide re-apply.

commits.subject: fix(db): accept spatial_ref_sys RLS lint warning; remove unapplyable migration
commits.body:
Tested live: Studio SQL Editor fails with the same 42501 as the
management API and `supabase db push`. I was wrong earlier in this
session when I claimed Studio was the escalation path — on this
project it runs as `postgres`, same as the others, and `postgres` is
not a member of `supabase_admin` so `SET ROLE` doesn't help.

Checked the full escalation surface before giving up:
- No shipped SECURITY DEFINER helper owned by supabase_admin covers
  RLS/ALTER (searched pg_proc for %rls%, %spatial%, %alter%, %admin%,
  %privileg% — zero matches).
- supabase_privileged_role isn't a member of supabase_admin and
  doesn't have UPDATE on spatial_ref_sys.

The only real path to flip the bit is a Supabase support ticket.
Not worth it — spatial_ref_sys is PostGIS public reference data, our
app never writes to it, and "writes denied by RLS" is already
enforced by table ownership (we can't write to a table we don't own).
The security value of enabling RLS here is nil.

Changes:
- Deleted supabase/migrations/20260409000001_enable_rls_spatial_ref_sys.sql
  so it doesn't sit permanently unapplied and mislead new sessions.
- CLAUDE.md: project-status line updated (17/17 applied + 1 accepted);
  AI Notes entry corrected to reflect that Studio does NOT escalate
  either; new "Accepted lint warnings" section documents the decision.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: removed the now-obsolete "Enable RLS on
  spatial_ref_sys" section.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md + docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: updated to record the
  correct outcome and the mistaken Studio-privileges assumption.

commits.subject: fix(db): make spatial_ref_sys RLS migration idempotent
commits.body:
The prior version errored on re-run with "policy already exists" and
"duplicate key value" on the schema_migrations INSERT. If a paste into
Studio partially succeeded (ALTER ok, CREATE POLICY failed for any
reason), the user couldn't safely re-run the same block.

Changes to the migration file:
- DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE POLICY so re-runs don't error on
  the policy name. ENABLE RLS is already a no-op when already enabled.
- Header note explaining the ownership constraint (supabase_admin vs
  postgres) and the Studio SQL Editor escape hatch.

Changes to USER_ACTIONS.md:
- Inlined the full SQL block (including the ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
  ledger insert) so the user runs one paste, not two — prior version
  sent them to open the migration file + a separate INSERT snippet.
- Stronger verification query (checks RLS flag, policy count, and
  ledger row in one call).
- Documented the platform-change fallback (if Studio also errors 42501,
  open a support ticket — don't retry via CLI).

commits.subject: fix(tests): repair 8 pre-existing failures surfaced after npm install
commits.body:
None of the 23 tests added in 3a60a28 had ever been executed — its
session notes said tests were "not yet executed" because npm install
wasn't available in that env. Running `npm install && npm test` here
surfaced 8 failures across two suites, all shipping to main.

IconCacheDisclosure (6 failures): react-test-renderer is deprecated
on React 19. `TestRenderer.create()` returns a renderer that's
unmounted before the test can read `.root`, and the environment
wasn't flagged as act-compatible. Fix: set
IS_REACT_ACT_ENVIRONMENT=true and wrap every create() in act() via
a renderComponent() helper. react-test-renderer is still functional
inside act(), so no dep change needed. Added @types/react-test-renderer
(was silently missing — `typeof import('react-test-renderer')` was
resolving to any, suppressing TS7006 checks on findAll callbacks).

inject-icon-hashes (2 failures):

1. Idempotent re-run emitted a WARNING treating "0 replacements" as
   format drift, even when HTML was already versioned. Script now
   tracks whether HTML contains already-versioned refs for known
   icons and only warns when neither bare nor versioned matches —
   the actual drift case. The existing "format drift" test still
   passes because its fixture has neither.
2. "Missing precached icon" test tried to `rmSync` favicon.png, but
   favicon was dropped from the pipeline in 19821c4 and isn't in the
   fixture. Switched to icon-192.png (still in SW_EXPECTED_URLS).

Unrelated to the migration-sync work on this branch — but surfaced
once `npm install` exposed the test runner, and fixing them here
rather than opening another branch avoids leaving main on a
chronically-red suite.

commits.subject: refactor(pwa): harden PWA_SYSTEM — singleton, suppression, visibility, typed check
commits.body:
Align usePWAUpdate with glow-props PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

- Extract SW lifecycle state into module-level singleton (src/lib/pwa.ts)
  so registration, hasUpdate, and offlineReady survive AppLayout remounts
  instead of re-firing "update available" banners after route changes.
- Thin React wrapper uses useSyncExternalStore for tearing-safe sync.
- 30-second wasJustUpdated() suppression via sessionStorage — onNeedRefresh
  skips while the post-reload SW lifecycle settles.
- visibilitychange listener triggers registration.update() when the tab
  regains focus (catches updates faster than the 60min poll, esp. on iOS).
- controllerchange reload guard registered once; auto-reloads only when
  _userClickedUpdate is set so background tab lifecycle events don't yank
  the page out from under the user.
- checkForUpdate(): Promise<'no-sw'|'done'|'error'> for toast feedback.
- onRegisterError routes to debugLog for DebugPill surfacing.
- Manifest: id, scope, prefer_related_applications (stable identity +
  avoid Chrome skipping beforeinstallprompt).
- Workbox: cleanupOutdatedCaches + explicit globPatterns.

Preserves existing offlineReady + dismissOfflineReady in the hook return
since AppLayout uses the banner; swap to Toast system when it lands.

Tags: pwa, refactor, infrastructure, service-worker
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid

commits.subject: feat(scripts): generate-theme-meta.mjs — drift detection + auto-fix
commits.body:
Closes out the last deferred pattern item from THEME_DARK_MODE. Script
verifies that every combo's metaColorLight/metaColorDark hex matches the
DaisyUI oklch definition, across the three places these values live:
  1. src/constants/themes.ts (THEME_COMBOS)
  2. index.html inline bootstrap COMBOS map
  3. index.html static <meta name="theme-color"> tags (first combo wins
     the pre-JS fallback role)

Modes:
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs`  — verify, exit 1 on drift
  - `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs --write`  — rewrite corrected hex

Accessed via `npm run check-theme-meta` for the common case.

Implementation notes:
  - Reads DaisyUI's per-theme oklch values from node_modules/daisyui/
    theme/object.js (CJS default export).
  - oklch → hex conversion inlined (~30 lines, matches pattern doc
    reference code). No color-conversion dependency.
  - Convention: light combos use their light theme's --color-primary
    (vibrant branded status bar); dark combos use their dark theme's
    --color-base-100 (seamless match to page background).
  - Catalog parsed from source via stable regex anchored on the
    `id: '...'` field; we own both the script and the file format, so
    acceptable. Script errors loudly if the catalog structure changes
    and the regex no longer matches.
  - Tested end-to-end: introduce drift → verify fails with diff →
    --write restores → verify passes → byte-for-byte identical to the
    pre-drift state.

Not wired into `npm run build` — keeps build fast. Intended usage:
manual after adding a combo / swapping DaisyUI theme / bumping DaisyUI
version.

Urgency: 1

commits.subject: docs(migrations): record outstanding-migration fix + add filename-uniqueness test
commits.body:
Follow-up to 75b4d67 — documents the drift that was caught (14 of 18
migrations outstanding on remote despite Project Status claiming all
pushed), adds the missing guard rail, and updates the docs that
survive across sessions.

- scripts/__tests__/migration-files.test.ts: Jest test asserting every
  migration filename is a 14-digit timestamp and no two files share a
  version. Would have caught the duplicate 20260311000001 on commit.
  Runs via existing `npm test`.
- CLAUDE.md project-status line: 17/18, with the spatial_ref_sys
  outlier linked to USER_ACTIONS.md.
- CLAUDE.md AI Notes: two new rules — (1) management API and `db push`
  run as `postgres`, not `supabase_admin`, so DDL on PostGIS/auth/
  storage tables must go via Studio SQL Editor; (2) never trust a
  project-status checkbox as evidence of remote state without querying
  the source of truth.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: 2026-04-21 entry — trusted stale checkbox,
  missed duplicate version + return-type change for ~2 months.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: rewritten with this session's actual work.

Skipped deliberately: backfilling `statements` column on the 13
inserted schema_migrations rows (column is nullable, cosmetic only),
and return-type-drift static detection (harder to implement reliably,
lower return than the filename check).

commits.subject: feat(burger-menu): add external / iconClass / highlightColor item fields
commits.body:
Close the API gap vs the BURGER_MENU pattern's standard MenuItem interface.
All three fields were flagged in the post-picker audit as pattern features
we hadn't shipped.

- `external?: boolean` — marks an item as pointing outside the app. Renders
  a trailing ↗ icon after the label. ariaLabel gets "(opens in new tab)"
  appended so screen readers announce the behavior even when the label
  doesn't mention it. Use case (future): "User Guide", "Docs" items.
- `iconClass?: string` — per-icon color-class override. Defaults to
  `currentColor` via the item's state-derived text color. Use case
  (future): a warning-colored icon on an otherwise-neutral item.
- `highlightColor?: string` — alternative accent text-color class when
  `highlight: true`. Defaults to `text-primary`. Use case (future): a
  success-green "new!" highlight alongside the existing primary-tinted
  "Install app" item.

No current consumer uses any of these — they're shipped for pattern
conformance so a future reader comparing our impl against BURGER_MENU.md
sees the full MenuAction surface. Each field defaults to the existing
behavior when omitted, so every existing menu-item spec in the app
continues to work unchanged.

hook now so any future disclosure that needs Tab/Shift+Tab cycling (modals,

- src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts: exact pattern-doc implementation — document

  the hook supersedes it. Zero behavior change: focus cycling works the
  same; prior focus restoration is a small net a11y improvement (inline
  version didn't restore on close — useFocusTrap does).

commits.body:
- Chat screens (mobile app/(app)/chat/[id].tsx + desktop ChatPanel.tsx) now
  pull realtimeDegraded from useScheduledMessages and forward it to
  ScheduledMessagesModal so the reconnecting banner appears when the
  sender-scoped realtime channel drops.

- utils/clearData.ts: before ending chats on "Clear All Data", soft-cancel
  any pending scheduled_messages the user owns. Prevents server-side
  queued sends from firing after the user has considered their identity
  gone. (endAllChats would have caused the dispatcher to mark them
  failed/chat_ended anyway, but explicit cancellation keeps the book
  clean and saves the dispatcher the work.)

- CLAUDE.md corrections:

    naturally at dispatch time; it now applies because the dispatcher
    re-checks it explicitly.
  * Documented cancel as soft-cancel (was hard-delete).
  * Added the cleanup cron and the at-least-once delivery model.
  * Noted that dispatcher + cleanup functions are cron-only (REVOKEd
    from authenticated).

    BEFORE UPDATE trigger, cleanup index, and min-lead CHECK.

  grouped by severity.

commits.subject: fix(scheduling): UI, a11y, and mobile UX from review findings
commits.body:
DateTimePicker split into platform-specific files via Metro's file-extension
resolution:
  DateTimePicker.tsx         - web (<input type="datetime-local">)
  DateTimePicker.native.tsx  - iOS (combined spinner) + Android (chained flow)

- L6: No more render-time require() + eslint-disable; each platform's module
  is imported at module scope.

- H7: Android picker rewritten as an explicit chain — tap opens date mode,
  confirm auto-advances to time mode, confirm closes. Symmetric "Change date"
  / "Change time" buttons make both steps discoverable. iOS uses a single
  combined mode="datetime" spinner for a simpler flow.

ScheduleComposerModal:
  - M9: leadOk and min/max bounds recompute on a 15s tick while the modal is
    open so stale picks are caught even if the user hesitates.
  - Error banner clears automatically when the user picks a new time.
  - useId() + real HTML <label htmlFor> on web ties the datetime input to its
    visible label (was only a separate Text node).
  - accessibilityRole / accessibilityLabel on close button, confirm button,
    and error region (accessibilityLiveRegion='polite', role='alert').
  - friendlyScheduleError() maps thrown errors to user copy.

ScheduledMessagesModal:
  - M3: Cancel flow confirms first via showConfirmDialog (destructive, no undo).
  - L8: "Cancel" -> "Cancel send" on pending rows to distinguish from the
    modal's own Cancel dismissal. Failed rows get "Dismiss" (softer).
  - M8: Row-level accessibilityLabel announces the scheduled time;
    Reschedule/Cancel/Dismiss buttons carry context in their a11y labels.
  - New realtimeDegraded banner when the hook reports a dropped subscription.
  - Empty state expanded with a "Tap the clock icon on the compose bar" hint.
  - dismissScheduledMessage wired for failed rows (soft-cancel under the hood).

ComposeBar:
  - Clock icon gains accessibilityRole='button', accessibilityLabel, and
    accessibilityHint explaining the interaction.
  - Kept always-visible-but-dim (rather than hidden-when-empty) for
    discoverability — first-time users need to see the affordance exists.

Tutorial:
  - L10: "Manage pending ones from the chat menu" -> "Tap the chat menu to
    cancel or reschedule" — more concrete.

commits.subject: fix(scheduling): service + hook correctness from review findings
commits.body:
- H6: cancelScheduledMessage now uses UPDATE (soft-cancel, status='cancelled')
  matched on id + sender_id + status='pending' with .maybeSingle(), verifying
  a row was affected. If the dispatcher beat us to it, surface "Could not
  cancel: this message is no longer pending" instead of a silent success.
  Mirror for dismissScheduledMessage on failed rows.

- M7: updateScheduledMessage drops the pre-fetch-then-update TOCTOU pattern.
  It relies on UPDATE + RLS + the BEFORE UPDATE lead-bounds trigger, mapping
  0-rows-affected to a friendly "no longer pending" message.

- M5/L4/L9: Added friendlyScheduleError() translating common Supabase error
  shapes (rate limit, RLS, constraint violations, PGRST116, realtime drop)
  to user copy. Used by the service on schedule/update/cancel throws and
  reused by both modals for their own catch blocks.

- L2: ScheduledMessageFailureReason narrowed to the canonical union
  ('chat_ended' | 'too_stale' | 'rate_limited'). The row's failure_reason
  is typed as `string | null` because unexpected errors write sqlerrm
  substrings; an isKnownFailureReason() type guard lets consumers
  switch-with-exhaustiveness on known reasons and fall back on strings.

- L3/M9: useScheduledMessages effects split so realtime subscribes on
  sender_id only (no churn on chatId change). Polling and fetch stay
  reactive to both. A new realtimeDegraded flag surfaces when the
  subscription channel drops/times out so the UI can show a reconnecting
  hint — polling still guarantees eventual consistency regardless.

- L4: snapshotReply propagates query errors instead of swallowing them
  into a silent null snippet. Also adds a defense-in-depth check that
  the reply target belongs to the same chat (RLS already enforces this
  via the SELECT policy; belt and braces).

- 'cancelled' added to ScheduledMessageStatus to match the DB enum.

Tags: scheduling, correctness, errors, types, race-conditions
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: message-scheduling
Semver: patch
commits.title: service + hook correctness from review findings

commits.subject: fix(db): harden scheduled_messages migration from review findings
commits.body:
Addresses 12 migration-side review findings from the pre-merge sweep:

- H1/H2: dispatch_scheduled_messages() and cleanup_scheduled_messages()
  REVOKEd from authenticated — cron-only. Also re-checks the per-sender
  30/min message rate limit before INSERT, so SECURITY DEFINER can't be
  used to bypass the cap via same-minute dispatches.

- H3: INSERT RLS now requires chat.ended_by IS NULL on both sides so a
  user can't schedule into a chat the other party has ended.

- H4: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE wrapped in a
  duplicate-check so migration re-apply is truly idempotent.

- H5 + L7: Added scheduled_min_lead CHECK (1 minute DB floor) and a
  BEFORE UPDATE trigger that re-validates lead bounds against now()
  whenever scheduled_for changes. Client still enforces 2 min; DB's
  looser floor accommodates reasonable client clock skew.

- M1: Status enum extended with 'cancelled'; UPDATE RLS permits the
  pending -> cancelled transition; REVOKE of DELETE means cancellation
  flows exclusively through UPDATE. This closes the schedule-then-delete
  loop that would have bypassed the 10/hr schedule rate limit.

- M2: Dispatcher re-checks the reply target at dispatch time and blanks
  the snippet if the target was deleted between schedule and send. Up to
  30 days could otherwise pass between a user retracting a message and
  their content still being propagated via a scheduled reply.

- M4: Dispatcher JOINs chats into the cursor SELECT (no more per-row
  ended_by lookup).

- L1: Dropped redundant idx_scheduled_messages_chat (sender+status index
  covers the query with better selectivity under RLS).

- New cleanup_scheduled_messages() + daily 04:17 UTC cron prunes sent /
  failed / cancelled rows older than 7 days so the table stays bounded.

- Added explicit failure-mode comment documenting at-least-once
  semantics of the dispatcher's single-transaction batch (rollback on
  panic → rows stay pending → retried next tick).

Tags: scheduling, security, rls, pg-cron, database, correctness
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: message-scheduling
Semver: patch
commits.title: harden scheduled_messages migration from review findings

commits.subject: fix(db): apply 13 outstanding migrations + fix blockers
commits.body:
Found the remote DB was stuck at the Feb 19 baseline (only 4 of 18
migrations in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations; every post-Feb
object — suburb/province columns, bulk_insert_stations RPC,
price_history trigger, source_id unique index, etc. — was missing).
