Fixes that would have blocked `supabase db push` regardless:

- Rename 20260311000001_fix_update_coords_not_found.sql to
  ...000002_... — two migrations shared the same version string, so
  schema_migrations would only ever track one.
- Prepend DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS to 20260322000002_upsert_bulk_insert_stations
  because it widens the bulk_insert_stations RETURNS TABLE from 2 cols
  to 3, which CREATE OR REPLACE cannot do.

Applied 13 of the 14 outstanding migrations via the Supabase
management API SQL endpoint, with matching schema_migrations inserts.
The 14th (enable_rls_spatial_ref_sys) can't run as `postgres` because
public.spatial_ref_sys is owned by supabase_admin — documented in
USER_ACTIONS.md with the exact Studio SQL Editor steps.

Verified all expected objects exist: chk_station_coordinates_valid,
station_province_check, suburb/province/loyalty columns, price_history
trigger, bulk_update_regulated_price, update_station_coordinates,

commits.subject: feat(theme-picker): wire combo picker into burger menu
commits.body:
Builds on the previous commit's combo storage. The burger menu now exposes
a theme picker section with one item per combo and a selected-state
checkmark on the active one. With only one combo today (Sancio Default —
corporate + business), the section is hidden via visible:false; it appears
automatically as soon as THEME_COMBOS gains a second entry.

- BurgerMenu: extend the items API from a flat MenuItem[] to a
  discriminated union MenuEntry = MenuAction | MenuSection. MenuAction
  is the existing item shape (kept as `MenuItem` alias for back-compat —
  no consumer churn). MenuSection adds non-interactive header rows
  rendered via DaisyUI's `menu-title` styling, automatically skipped by
  arrow-key navigation (the existing query selector matches buttons only).
  MenuAction also gains `selected?: boolean` which renders a trailing
  checkmark + accent highlight, with `aria-current="true"` for screen
  readers.
- useActiveCombo hook: reactive read of the active combo id. Two
  observers — MutationObserver on <html data-theme> (catches every
  applyTheme call: same-tab setCombo/toggleDark, cross-tab sync after
  theme.ts re-applies, OS-preference change) and storage event listener
  (catches the future case where two combos share a theme-per-mode and
  only the combo id changes — data-theme wouldn't mutate then). setState
  is idempotent so double-fires are harmless.
- AppLayout: wires both the dark-mode toggle (uses toggleDark) and the
  picker section (one MenuAction per THEME_COMBOS entry, calls setCombo,
  selected by useActiveCombo). Section + items use visible:THEME_COMBOS.
  length > 1 so the section is dormant until a second combo lands.
- CLAUDE.md → THEME_DARK_MODE: rewritten to document the combo schema +
  storage migration. BURGER_MENU deviation note updated to mention the
  hidden-when-single-combo picker.

Cross-tab sync verified by inspection: theme.ts storage handler mutates
data-theme; useActiveCombo's MutationObserver fires; AppLayout re-renders
the picker with the new selected state.

Debt: paid

commits.subject: refactor(theme): migrate to combo-based storage (Approach B)
commits.body:
Storage schema changes from Chunk 7's 1-key model to pattern-doc Approach B:

  Before:  localStorage["theme"]      = "corporate" | "business"
  After:   localStorage["darkMode"]   = "true" | "false"
           localStorage["themeCombo"] = combo id

Rationale: a combo catalog is on the roadmap (user direction: "we might
add more combos later"). With combos, the active DaisyUI theme is derived
at runtime from combo + dark state, which needs both keys. The Chunk 7
single-theme model stored the resolved theme name directly, which can't
round-trip through a toggle ("I picked the Luxe combo, then switched to
light" needs to remember it was Luxe, not just the active theme name).

- src/constants/themes.ts: ThemeCombo interface + THEME_COMBOS catalog
  (1 entry today: 'default' pairing corporate/business) + helpers
  (validComboId, getCombo, resolveTheme, resolveMetaColor). Each combo
  carries metaColorLight/Dark hex for the <meta name="theme-color"> tags;
  convention follows the pattern doc — light=primary, dark=base-100.
  Adding a combo requires (1) updating the DaisyUI plugin list in
  index.css, (2) adding an entry here, (3) mirroring in index.html's
  inline bootstrap COMBOS map (documented in both places).
- src/utils/theme.ts:
    * applyTheme() signature narrowed: (dark, themeName, metaColor). Pure
      DOM mutation, no storage side-effects. Previous overloaded behavior
      split into setCombo / toggleDark / initTheme.
    * setCombo(comboId): switch combos while keeping current dark mode.
    * toggleDark(): flip dark for the active combo.
    * initTheme(): one-shot legacy migration of the old `theme` key,
      then resolve state (storage > OS preference), apply, attach
      cross-tab + OS-preference listeners. Cross-tab sync now watches
      both darkMode and themeCombo keys.
    * getActiveComboId(): live read for picker UI.
    * THEME_COMBOS re-exported for convenience.
- index.html inline bootstrap:
    * Embeds the COMBOS map inline (unavoidable — inline scripts can't
      import ES modules, documented).
    * Runs the same legacy-key migration so first paint after upgrade
      matches the migrated storage state.
    * Resolves theme from combo + dark before applying.
- AppLayout.tsx: dark-mode toggle now calls `toggleDark()` instead of
  `applyTheme()` directly. Menu-item behavior unchanged.

Legacy storage migration: one-shot. Reads `localStorage["theme"]` once,
translates to darkMode + themeCombo, removes the old key. Runs in both
the inline bootstrap (so the migrated state applies on first paint) and
theme.ts initTheme (so both code paths are defensive). Safe against
repeated runs because the old key is cleared.

No UI changes yet — picker lands in the next commit.

Tags: refactor, theme, storage, combos, migration
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-picker
Semver: minor
commits.title: migrate to combo-based storage (Approach B)

commits.subject: feat(ui): add BurgerMenu disclosure component
commits.body:
Accessible dropdown nav using the WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern (NOT
role="menu" — that's for application menus like File/Edit/View and causes
screen readers to enter forms mode, suppress normal nav keys, and expect
arrow-key navigation from every item).

Spec-level behavior:
- Trigger `<button aria-expanded aria-controls aria-label>` with 44×44 min
  touch target (btn-circle override for WCAG 2.5.5 + iOS HIG).
- Panel `<nav id role="navigation" aria-label>` positioned top-right of
  the trigger with max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)] so it can't overflow narrow
  viewports.
- Backdrop `<div class="fixed inset-0 cursor-pointer">` at z-40. The
  cursor-pointer is load-bearing: iOS Safari silently drops click events
  on empty divs without it.
- z-50 menu / z-40 backdrop (per Z_INDEX_SCALE). Debug pill at z-[80]
  still renders above the menu.
- Focus management via useDisclosureFocus: open → first item; close →
  back to trigger.
- Escape closes via useEscapeKey.
- ArrowDown / ArrowUp cycle items with wrap. Home / End jump to
  first / last. Tab / Shift+Tab work as browser-default.
- Close-then-act pattern: after any item action, menu closes then runs
  the action 150ms later so navigation / modal-opens happen on a clean
  DOM. Pending timer cleaned up on unmount.

MenuItem interface exposes: label, action, visible, disabled, separator,
destructive, highlight, icon (SVG path string), keepOpen (skip auto-close
for theme-toggle-style repeat-use actions), ariaLabel (screen-reader
override when label flips per state).

Action-error routing: failures in item.action surface through debugLog
('render','error',...) so they land in the DebugPill buffer rather than
vanishing into a rejected Promise.

Tags: feature, ui, disclosure, a11y, daisyui, burger-menu
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: burger-menu
Semver: minor

commits.subject: feat(hooks+version): shared disclosure hooks + Vite-injected app version
commits.body:
Groundwork for the BurgerMenu component. Nothing visible yet — these are
the shared primitives the menu (and future disclosures) will consume.

- src/hooks/useEscapeKey.ts: keydown listener bound only while `active`.
  preventDefault() so the browser doesn't also eat Escape (e.g. exiting
  fullscreen) when a menu consumes it.
- src/hooks/useDisclosureFocus.ts: on open → focus first focusable in the
  content (scheduled via rAF so content has painted); on close → return
  focus to the trigger. `hasBeenOpenRef` guard prevents focus-stealing on
  initial mount.
- src/hooks/useDarkClass.ts: reactive .dark-on-<html> state via
  MutationObserver. DebugPill used to define this locally; extracted so
  BurgerMenu (main tree) and DebugPill (separate React root) share one
  implementation. Both roots read DOM directly, so no React-context
  coupling crosses the root boundary.
- src/components/common/DebugPill.tsx: drop the inline useDarkClass,
  import from the shared hook. Zero behavior change.

App version plumbing:
- vite.config.ts: read package.json once at config-load, inject as
  `__APP_VERSION__` via Vite `define`. No package metadata ships to the
  client bundle beyond the version string.
- src/vite-env.d.ts: ambient `declare const __APP_VERSION__: string`.
- src/constants/app.ts: APP_NAME ('Sancio' — product name; package slug
  is sun-sea-o) + APP_VERSION from the define (with a '0.0.0-dev'
  fallback for non-Vite contexts like tests).

commits.subject: polish(debug-pill): move error/warn badge colors into Palette struct
commits.body:
Two tiny residuals caught by the double-check audit:

1. DebugPill.tsx had `#ef4444` and `#f59e0b` inlined in the JSX for the
   error/warn count badges on the pill chip — escaped the Palette struct
   for no reason. Add them to the Palette type as `errorBadge` /
   `warnBadge`, define identical values in LIGHT + DARK (the pill is
   always-dark by design so both themes render the same chip colors),
   and consume via `palette.errorBadge` / `palette.warnBadge` at render
   time. Zero behavior change; the palette struct is now the single
   source of truth for every color on the pill.

commits.subject: feat(scheduling): add server-side message scheduling
commits.body:
Users can queue a message for future delivery via a clock-icon affordance
next to the send button. Survives app close / offline because dispatch runs
on the database via pg_cron.

Architecture:
  - New scheduled_messages table with RLS (sender-only), check constraints
    mirroring messages, partial index on (scheduled_for) for the hot path
  - dispatch_scheduled_messages() SECURITY DEFINER function + pg_cron
    '* * * * *' schedule moves due rows into messages; existing
    notify_new_message trigger handles pushes for free
  - Dispatcher handles: chat ended (failed/chat_ended), >1hr late
    (failed/too_stale), constraint violations (failed/sqlerrm)
  - check_rate_limit gains 'schedule' action type (10/hour)
  - services/scheduledMessages.ts: schedule/list/update/cancel,
    snapshots reply data at schedule time
  - useScheduledMessages hook with realtime + polling fallback
  - DateTimePicker wrapper: <input type="datetime-local"> on web,
    @react-native-community/datetimepicker on native
  - ScheduleComposerModal for picking send time, ScheduledMessagesModal
    for listing/rescheduling/cancelling
  - ChatHeaderMenu gets 'Scheduled (N)' entry on mobile; ChatPanel shows
    a compact pill on desktop
  - Tutorial gains a new 'Schedule Messages' step

Decisions locked in:
  - Cancel = hard delete (matches user mental model)
  - Modal-only, no ghost bubbles (preserves sender/receiver parity)
  - Native pickers per platform, wrapped in one component
  - 2min minimum / 30d maximum lead time, 5-min picker snap
  - 20 pending per user cap, 10 schedules/hour rate limit

Tags: scheduling, pg-cron, database, ui, tones, rate-limit
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2

commits.subject: polish(ui): audit LOW items — radius tokens, focus ring offsets, tablist ARIA
commits.body:
Close out the LOW-severity findings from the visual/a11y audit. Each item
is a small correctness or polish improvement.

- SigningStatus.tsx / SequenceSignOff.tsx: add explanatory comments for
  the intentional inline-default-rounded / full-width-rounded-none
  distinction and the `flex-col items-start` on SequenceSignOff's
  multi-line info alert. The pattern wasn't wrong — it was just undocumented.
- ModuleSummaryCard.tsx + ContractUploadPreview.tsx: swap `rounded-lg`
  for DaisyUI's `rounded-box` token. They resolve to the same pixel value
  today but `rounded-box` is the theme-controlled radius variable, so
  future theme changes (different border-radius) won't leave these two
  surfaces stuck at a fixed size.
- Modal.tsx close button: add `focus:ring-offset-2`. At 44px with
  `btn-circle`, the circular border was clipping the focus ring; the
  offset pushes the ring outside the radius so keyboard focus is visible.
- DebugPill.tsx: add `role="tablist"` + `aria-label="Debug views"` on the
  tab container, `role="tab"` + `aria-selected` on each tab button, and
  aria-labels on the Copy / Clear action buttons. The pill lives in a
  separate React root so screen readers treated it as an island of
  unlabeled buttons; now the tab pattern is announced correctly.
- Input.tsx / TextArea.tsx: add `block` to the `label-text font-medium`
  wrapper. DaisyUI's `label-text` is inline-block by default; on narrow
  (320px) viewports the label could wrap inline next to the input. `block`
  forces the label to always stack above.

Not changed (verification-blockers — require browser / device):
- The focus-ring offset on Modal close is a best-effort fix based on the
  known clipping behavior of circular focus rings. Real-device verification
  is still needed.

Tags: polish, a11y, ui, daisyui
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor(cards): unify ModuleCard + restore inline-edit focus rings
commits.body:
- ModuleCard was the one card-family outlier using raw `rounded-lg border
  p-4` markup. Migrate to `card card-compact` + `card-body` structure and
  add hover states matching ProjectCard/AgreementCard (hover:border-base-
  content/30 + hover:shadow-sm) when the module is active. Expired cards
  stay static (no hover) since the card is preserved for reference, not
  interaction. Pending-proposal cards get a warning-tinted hover variant.
- AgreementHeader + ProjectDetailPage inline-title edit: `input-ghost` had
  only `focus:border-primary focus:outline-none` — no visible ring. A
  keyboard-focused user couldn't tell which field was active. Add
  `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/30` so there's a persistent halo
  alongside the bottom-border indicator.

Not touched (decisions):
- ProposalCard / NotesList / VersionHistoryPanel lack hover states by
  design — they're display-only surfaces, not clickable cards. Flagged
  by the visual audit but rejected here; consistency with clickable cards
  is not appropriate for static content.
- BatchSignActions uses bg-base-200 intentionally — it's a nested panel
  within page content, a tier below the regular bg-base-100 cards.

commits.subject: refactor(status): centralize STATUS_BADGE maps
commits.body:
Three copies of STATUS_BADGE had drifted from each other:
- AgreementCard.tsx had draft/requested/in_progress/fully_agreed/reopened
  but lacked an `error` variant (intentional — Agreement.status has no
  error state).
- AgreementHeader.tsx omitted `draft` entirely (intentional — header
  doesn't show a badge when the agreement is untouched) but the omission
  was invisible because the map shape didn't document it.
- ProposalCard.tsx had its own Proposal.status map (pending/accepted/
  rejected/countered) inline.

New src/constants/statusBadge.ts exports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE and
PROPOSAL_STATUS_BADGE, typed as Record<AgreementStatus, BadgeSpec> and
Record<ProposalStatus, BadgeSpec>. The exhaustive Record type now catches
any new enum value at compile time.

- AgreementCard: imports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE; `?? fallback` syntax
  replaces `|| fallback` (nullish-coalescing is correct here — BadgeSpec
  objects are always truthy).
- AgreementHeader: imports AGREEMENT_STATUS_BADGE; explicit
  `status === 'draft' ? null` branch now documents why draft renders
  without a badge.
- ProposalCard: imports PROPOSAL_STATUS_BADGE.

Two separate maps rather than a single generic one because Agreement and
Proposal are genuinely different enums with different semantic meanings
(agreement lifecycle vs proposal resolution). Keeping them co-located in
one file makes future drift easy to spot.

commits.subject: fix(a11y): restore WCAG 2.5.5 touch targets + theme-aware skip link
commits.body:
The DaisyUI migration inadvertently regressed several mobile touch targets
and broke the keyboard skip-to-content link's theme adaptation. All caught
by the post-migration a11y/mobile/dark-mode audit.

- src/index.css: .skip-to-content reads `var(--color-primary)` +
  `var(--color-primary-content)` instead of hardcoded #1e293b / #fff. DaisyUI
  exposes these per theme, so the focused skip pill now has high contrast
  against both corporate and business page surfaces (it was near-invisible
  on business's #202020 page bg before).
- NotificationBell.tsx: "Mark all read" btn-xs (24-28px) → btn-sm +
  min-h-[44px]. Delete icon btn-sm btn-square (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px]. Both failed WCAG 2.5.5.
- PwaInstallPrompt.tsx: Install / How-to-Install CTAs swap from naked
  `btn btn-sm` (32px) to `<Button size="sm">` (40px with the wrapper's
  WCAG override). Dismiss btn-sm btn-circle (32px) gets min-h-[44px]
  min-w-[44px].
- Input.tsx: every form input gets `min-h-[44px] text-base`. DaisyUI's
  input-bordered defaults to 40px / 14px — iOS Safari auto-zooms on focus
  when font-size < 16px. text-base (16px) + 44px height fixes both.
- TextArea.tsx: text-base added (no height override — textareas are
  multi-row by nature).
- ProposeChangeForm.tsx + ModuleEditor.tsx content textareas + AddNoteForm
  note textarea: text-base added on the direct DaisyUI `textarea
  textarea-bordered` usage (these bypass the TextArea wrapper).
- AddNoteForm.tsx source select: min-h-[44px] text-base on
  `select select-bordered`.

Net: every primary form input on mobile now prevents iOS auto-zoom + meets
WCAG 2.5.5; every icon button in the migrated code path now hits 44×44.

commits.subject: feat(debug-pill): theme-aware palette + document exceptions
commits.body:
DebugPill lives in a separate React root so it can't consume theme context
or DaisyUI classes. Per the DEBUG_SYSTEM pattern, swap palettes by reading
the `.dark` class from `<html>` directly via a MutationObserver.

- LIGHT / DARK Palette constants: panel bg/border/header, muted tiers,
  tab-active surface, severity bg/fg (error/warn/success/info), diagnostic
  status colors (pass/fail/warn/running), and per-source accent colors.
  Dark variants use slate-900/800 surfaces with slate-300/400 text and
  deep-tinted severity backgrounds (#450a0a for error etc.) so contrast
  stays above WCAG AA.
- `createStyles(palette)` factory replaces the static `S` Record;
  tabStyle/logRowStyle/logSourceStyle/diagStatusStyle take the palette
  as an extra arg.
- `useDarkClass()` hook: subscribes to <html>'s class attribute mutations.
  applyTheme() changes `.dark` in a single mutation, so no storm.
- Pill chip itself stays dark in both themes — it's a floating diagnostic
  that needs to be visible against any page surface; themeing it to
  base-100 would make it invisible on corporate pages.
- Adds `theme` to the recognized sources map (the Chunk 7 debugLog emits
  use it) with a DaisyUI-primary-ish purple accent.

CLAUDE.md: new "Hardcoded-color exceptions (documented)" table in Adopted
Patterns → THEME_DARK_MODE. Lists every file that legitimately keeps
hardcoded hex/colors (tones palette, theme source of truth, inline
bootstrap, print overrides, debug pill) with the reason each is preserved,
plus a grep command for catching regressions.

Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor(modules): migrate module domain to DaisyUI card/badge/alert/tabs
commits.body:
Largest chunk of the migration — 18 files. Every status/decision surface
gets its DaisyUI equivalent so the entire module workflow tints coherently
with the active theme.

- ModuleCard: expired / pending-proposal / default surfaces are bg-base-*
  combinations. Status pills become badge-error/success/info/warning. Move
  up/down become `btn btn-ghost btn-sm btn-square`. Delete button keeps
  ghost styling with `text-error` override.
- ModuleViewer / ModuleEditor: back button → `btn btn-ghost`. Content
  textarea → `textarea textarea-bordered`. Sticky save bar uses bg-base-100
  border-base-300. Success/failure feedback → text-success / text-error.
- ModuleSummaryCard: left-border accent is `border-l-primary` so the
  theme-accent tint carries across. Body surface is bg-base-200.
- ModuleSummaryEditor: wrapper is bg-base-200 + base-300. Fields use
  `input input-bordered input-sm` via new SummaryField form-control shell.
- ProposalCard: `card card-compact` with variant-mapped badges; counter-
  proposal block uses `bg-info/10`; reject button is ghost + text-error.
- NotesList: each row is a `card card-compact`, source chip is `badge
  badge-ghost badge-sm`. Delete button `text-error hover:bg-error/10`.
- AddNoteForm / ProposeChangeForm: `textarea textarea-bordered`, `select
  select-bordered`, `label-text` wrappers. Error text → text-error.
- ContractUploadPreview: batch progress dots use bg-base-300/info/success/
  error; parser/duplicate warnings become `alert alert-warning/error`;
  file drop zone uses base-300 dashed border with base-200 hover; rename
  inline input → `input input-bordered input-sm`; split/merge actions →
  DaisyUI `link`.
- TemplateSelector: tab bar is `tabs tabs-bordered tab/tab-active`.
  Template cards use base-300 borders; upload tab warnings are alert-*.
- VersionHistoryPanel: current version is base-100 card, prior versions
  are base-200 card (softer past surface). Signature chips are `badge
  badge-success badge-sm`.
- SigningStatus: locked = `alert alert-success`, ready = `alert alert-
  warning`. Icons inherit currentColor from the alert.
- ReadyToSignToggle / BatchSignActions / SignatureLog / NudgeExpiryConfig:
  inputs → DaisyUI form controls; surfaces → card bg-base-200.
- ToneSelector: input + add button → input-bordered + btn; label /
  muted text → label-text + base-content/50. Hash-based tone colors stay
  hardcoded (data palette, brand exception documented in the hex map).

Outside print: overrides in PdfPreview, `src/` has zero hardcoded slate /
gray / colored-palette utilities.

commits.body:
- AuthLayout: bg-base-200 page surface.
- AppLayout: bg-base-200 page + bg-base-100 header with border-base-300.

commits.subject: refactor(ui-primitives): migrate common components to DaisyUI classes
commits.body:
Ship 9 primitives as thin wrappers around DaisyUI component classes so theme
colors, focus rings, hover/active/disabled states, sizing, and border-radius
all follow data-theme automatically instead of being hand-rolled.

- Button: `btn btn-{primary|outline|error|ghost}`. Variant map is the only
  app concern; sizes keep WCAG 2.5.5 min-h overrides (sm=40, md=44, lg=48)
  because DaisyUI's native btn-sm is 32px. Removed 60-char base-string and
  per-variant Tailwind strings.
- Input / TextArea: `input input-bordered` / `textarea textarea-bordered`
  inside `form-control` with DaisyUI `label-text` + `input-error` /
  `textarea-error`. Removed hand-rolled border/focus-ring/placeholder
  styling (~80% of the class string).
- Modal: native <dialog> (Z_INDEX_SCALE still requires the top-layer) with
  DaisyUI `modal` + `modal-box` replacing custom backdrop/centering/sizing
  markup. Close button is now `btn btn-ghost btn-sm btn-circle` with a 44px
  touch-target override. Focus trap preserved (native dialog doesn't trap).
- ConfirmDialog: inherits Modal+Button changes; swaps manual action flex
  for DaisyUI `modal-action`.
- Toast: `toast toast-center toast-bottom` + `alert`. Kept the safe-area
  padding and `no-print` + `animate-fade-in` since those are app concerns
  not covered by DaisyUI's toast container.
- LoadingSpinner: DaisyUI `loading loading-spinner loading-{size}`. Replaces
  the inline SVG animation.
- EmptyState: no DaisyUI equivalent fits (vertical content card isn't
  hero/card/alert). Kept plain layout, swapped all slate-* for base-*.
- ErrorBoundary: full-page layout via DaisyUI `hero` + `card` + `card-body`
  + `card-actions`. Icon uses `text-error` so the error color follows the
  theme palette.

Visual changes to expect: slightly different padding/heights on buttons
(DaisyUI's rhythm differs from the prior custom), themed focus rings, and
darker elevated surfaces in corporate mode (base-200 vs old slate-50).

commits.subject: fix(hooks): eliminate set-state-in-effect cascades in useModules + DebugPill
commits.body:
Two pre-existing react-hooks/set-state-in-effect errors, both flagged by
react-hooks v7 under React 19. Each is a real cascading-render pattern
that would cause redundant renders on every dependency change.

useModules.ts: a separate useEffect watched `modules` and called
fetchVersions() synchronously, which setState'd versions. Restructure so
fetchVersions takes an explicit module list and is called inline at the
end of fetchModules (with the just-fetched data) and from the realtime
INSERT subscription via `modulesRef.current`. The reactive-effect pattern
is gone; version fetching now chains directly off each module load.

Side benefit: `fetchVersions` no longer depends on `modules` closure, so
its identity is stable across module changes — the subscription effect
no longer churns either.

DebugPill.tsx: PWA-diagnostics effect called `setDiagnostics(null)` in
the effect body to show a loading state while re-running. Remove the
reset — initial state is already null (first visit shows "Running
diagnostics…"), and on re-open the old results stay visible until new
ones arrive. Brief staleness is fine for a debug surface; the cascading
render it caused was not.

Tags: fix, lint, react-hooks, react-19
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: react-19-compliance
Semver: patch
commits.title: eliminate set-state-in-effect cascades in useModules + DebugPill

commits.subject: fix(pre-react-chrome): align boot loader + overlays + manifest with theme
commits.body:
Pre-React chrome (boot-loader, noscript fallback, fatal-error overlay in
index.html, App-load-failure fallback in main.tsx) used hardcoded slate
hex values — a hard light-mode flash before React mounts under business.
Fix by having the inline bootstrap script set CSS custom properties on
<html> before first paint; every pre-React surface now reads var(--boot-*)
with a hex fallback.

- index.html inline script: sets --boot-bg, --boot-fg, --boot-muted,
  --boot-spinner, --boot-error-bg, --boot-error-fg based on resolved
  theme (stored preference → OS preference).
- index.html boot-loader, noscript, error overlay: colors read var(--boot-*).
- main.tsx App-load fallback: same treatment.
- src/utils/theme.ts: new updateBootChromeColors(dark) called from
  applyTheme so user-explicit theme changes also refresh the palette
  (e.g. if React fails after a runtime theme toggle, overlay matches).
- vite.config.ts PWA manifest theme_color: #1e293b → #202020 (business
  base-100). Static once installed; dark is forgiving under both app
  themes where a branded primary would clash on dark-mode devices.

unavoidable duplication per the pattern (inline scripts can't import
ES modules). Both sites noted so they stay in sync.

Also adds a CSP caveat comment: strict `script-src` without 'unsafe-inline'
will block the bootstrap; precompute a SHA-256 hash if Sancio adopts one.

commits.subject: refactor(theme): narrow types, add debug observability, tighten CSS
commits.body:
- Export Theme union ('corporate' | 'business') so consumers (UI toggle
  in Chunk 8, tests, debug surfaces) can type against the concrete set.
  applyTheme keeps its string signature so ad-hoc themes pass without
  casting — the union is the preferred shape, not a hard gate.
- META_COLORS typed as Record<Theme, string> so the map can't drift
  silently from the theme catalog.
- resolveInitial returns {source: 'storage' | 'os'} for debugLog context.
- HMR guard: module-level `initialized` flag so Vite hot-reload re-running
  main.tsx can't attach duplicate storage/matchMedia listeners.
- debugLog('theme', ...) emissions for init, apply, cross-tab sync, OS
  preference change, and storage-cleared-elsewhere. Theme is infrequent
  enough to log every event without flooding the DebugPill buffer.
- index.css: annotate color-scheme base layer as a belt-and-braces safety
  net (DaisyUI's per-theme CSS already handles it with higher specificity,
  kept only for the CSS-failed-to-load fallback case).
- index.css (print): replace fragile `.bg-slate-50` rule with theme-agnostic
  `html, body, #root { background: white }`. Works whether the printed view
  is rendering corporate, business, or a component still using slate-*
  during migration.

commits.subject: feat(theme): stand up DaisyUI v5 + theme module (corporate/business)
commits.body:
Install DaisyUI 5 and wire the dual-layer theming plumbing per the glow-props
THEME_DARK_MODE pattern, Approach A (data-theme). Corporate is the light theme
(--default), business is the dark theme (--prefersdark). Single localStorage
"theme" key stores the active theme name; dark is derived as "business".

- src/utils/theme.ts: applyTheme(dark, name, skipPersist?) + initTheme(). Sets
  .dark class, data-theme attribute, color-scheme, and both meta theme-color
  tags on every change. Cross-tab sync via storage event. OS-preference
  fallback via matchMedia — only honored when no stored choice, so explicit
  choices aren't overridden.
- src/index.css: @plugin "daisyui" with two themes, @custom-variant dark,
  color-scheme base layer.
- index.html: replace single static meta theme-color with two media-queried
  tags (corporate #0082ce / business #202020, derived from DaisyUI oklch).
  Inline classic <script> in <head> reads localStorage and applies the theme
  before any module script — prevents FOUC.
- src/main.tsx: call initTheme() before React mount to attach listeners.
- CLAUDE.md: document Approach A in Adopted Patterns with rationale and
  file map for next chunks.

No UI toggle yet — Chunk 8. Existing slate-* utilities untouched; components
continue to render as before since data-theme=corporate + .dark-absent keeps
DaisyUI in light mode and no dark: pairs exist yet.

commits.body:

- copyToClipboard now tries ClipboardItem Blob → writeText → textarea
  in order, matching the pattern's 3-method fallback. The Blob path
  handles contexts where writeText is blocked (some iframes, strict
  permission contexts); writeText is the standard path; the textarea
  fallback catches mobile PWA webviews where even the clipboard API
  is locked down.

ErrorBoundary.tsx:
- componentDidCatch now passes the error stack and React component
  stack as structured `details` on the debugLog entry instead of
  splicing the first stack line into the event string. A future
  remote reporter subscribing to debugLog (Sentry etc.) can pick
  these up cleanly without parsing free text.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): harden DEBUG_SYSTEM — 10-part refactor per pattern
commits.body:
Applies the glow-props DEBUG_SYSTEM pattern end-to-end:

src/utils/debugLog.ts (rewritten):
- A) DebugEntry gains numeric `id` (auto-incrementing) for stable React
     keys + dedup.
- B) DebugEntry gains optional `details: Record<string, unknown>` for
     structured post-mortem payloads. debugLog() takes an optional 4th arg.
- C) DebugSource is now a union with `(string & {})` fallback — known
     sources keep autocomplete, ad-hoc strings work without enum edits.
- F) `debugGenerateReport()` moved out of DebugPill into the module;
     reusable by any consumer.
- G) URL query strings redacted in reports — both the env URL line and
     any URL-ish substring inside entry events / stringified details.
- H) Global listeners (console.error/warn interception, window error +
     unhandledrejection) are now installed exactly once via the
     `window.__debugLogListenersAttached` flag, preventing HMR duplicates.
     The old `window.onerror = …` + `addEventListener('error', …)` double
     wiring is gone.
- I) `subscribe()` now replays existing entries to each new subscriber
     so late-mounting consumers see the full boot sequence.
- J) Every subscriber invocation is wrapped in try/catch — one throwing
     listener can't break the rest. `clearEntries()` now sends a 'clear'
     signal via the same notify path (cleaner than the previous
     `message === '__clear__'` sentinel).

The internal `message` field is renamed to `event` to match the pattern.
All external callers pass strings positionally, so no call-site changes
were needed outside DebugPill.

src/components/common/DebugPill.tsx (rewritten):
- D) Every Tailwind class replaced with inline `CSSProperties` style
     objects. The pill renders in an isolated React root (#debug-root),
     so a Tailwind build failure or delayed CSS load no longer affects
     its rendering.
- E) New "PWA" tab alongside Log/Env. Runs live diagnostics on open:
     HTTPS, network, SW support + registration state, manifest fetch +
     validation, standalone mode, beforeinstallprompt receipt. A
     monotonic run counter drops stale results if the tab is closed
     mid-probe.
- Entries are keyed by the new numeric `id` field.
- `Copy` now calls `debugGenerateReport()` from the module.
- `details` payload displayed inline on log entries.

src/types/browser.d.ts:
- Window gains optional `__debugLogListenersAttached` flag so the HMR
  guard is type-safe.

Tags: debug, refactor, diagnostics, fleet-alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: cross-fleet-docs
Semver: patch
commits.title: harden DEBUG_SYSTEM — 10-part refactor per pattern

DebugPill buffer what actually happened during a session:

  in addition to the existing console.warn, so exported debug reports

that fails pushes and PRs to main when AUTH_BYPASS_ENABLED is true.
Feature branches are not checked, so dev work with the shim can continue
unhindered. The gate is a safety net for when the flag is eventually
flipped back off — prevents accidental re-enablement.

commits.subject: refactor(query): split queryParser.ts (536 lines) into src/services/query/*
commits.body:
The old file crossed the 500-line "pause and consider" threshold in
CLAUDE.md. Each concern in the query pipeline is self-contained and
mostly imports from the shared AST types, so the split is mechanical.

New layout:
- src/services/query/ast.ts (39 lines) — AST types, CompareOp,
  GraphContext. Sole source of the query data model.
- src/services/query/tokenizer.ts (73 lines) — Token type + tokenize().
  Lexer for the text query language (label:X, OR, NOT, IN/OUT/CONN,
  paren grouping).
- src/services/query/parser.ts (199 lines) — Recursive-descent Parser
  class + parseQuery() public entry point. Tokenizer is internal;
  parseQuery wraps tokenize + parse.
- src/services/query/evaluator.ts (117 lines) — evaluateNode +
  compareValues + applyOp. Date→number→string coercion lives here
  because it's evaluator-specific, not a shape definition.
- src/services/query/builder.ts (132 lines) — BuilderFilter/BuilderStep
  types + builderFilterToAST / builderStepToAST. Converts the
  interactive query builder's structured output directly to AST
  without round-tripping through text.

src/services/queryParser.ts (22 lines) — Barrel re-export so
`import { ... } from './queryParser'` still works. queryEngine.ts
and its consumers don't need to change. Kept the filename as a
searchable landmark rather than forcing 20+ import-site edits.

All 336 tests pass on the first build. Lint + build clean. Zero
behavior change — this is pure hygiene.

Docs
- CLAUDE.md: project-structure tree updated with the new layout.
- docs/TODO.md: queryParser split item removed (done).
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new section describing the change.

commits.subject: test(graph): oklch-rejection tripwire for Cytoscape stylesheet output
commits.body:
Closes the Console-warning-budget TODO item. Defensive test specifically
for the class of bug that started this branch: DaisyUI's oklch() values
reaching Cytoscape's color parser and getting silently dropped.

src/components/graph/cytoscapeStyles.safety.test.ts (new, 6 cases)
- Seeds document.documentElement with realistic DaisyUI oklch CSS vars
  for the 7 tokens the stylesheet reads (warning/info/success/
  secondary/accent/primary/base-content).
- Installs a canvas mock that simulates the browser's oklch→hex
  normalization for each seeded value.
- Calls getCytoscapeStylesheet() for both 'bezier' and 'taxi' edge-
  curve styles. Walks every string-valued `*color*` property in the
  output (skipping Cytoscape `data(...)` mapper references — those
  resolve to values produced by getNodeColor/getArcColor, which have
  their own direct tests).
- Asserts each value matches /^(#[0-9a-f]{3,8}|rgba?\(.+\)|hsla?\(.+\)|
  transparent|[a-z]+)$/i AND does not start with any of oklch(, lch(,
  oklab(, lab(, color(, color-mix(.
- Separately exercises getNodeColor (labelless path via --color-primary)
  and getArcColor (titled path + untitled fallback).
- Uses a TS-safe narrow (`'style' in block`) for Cytoscape's
  StylesheetJsonBlock union (StylesheetStyle | StylesheetCSS).

Verified the tripwire trips: temporarily replaced the normalizeColor()
call in getCSSColor with a raw pass-through — 3 of 6 cases failed
loudly with messages like "node:selected { border-color: oklch(82% .189
84.429) } — starts with unparseable 'oklch('". Restored the fix before
committing.

What this catches:
- Direct regression: anyone removing/bypassing cssColor normalization
- New style property added with a literal oklch/color-mix/lch color
- PALETTE hex replaced with an oklch literal
- New CSS Color 4 function that Cytoscape doesn't know about

336 tests pass (up from 330). Lint + build clean.

Docs
- docs/TODO.md: console-warning-budget item removed (done).
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new section describing the tripwire.

Debt: paid

commits.subject: feat(debug): centralize caught-error logging via logError helper
commits.body:
Closes the last deferred item from the debug-report sweep. Across the
codebase, ~21 catch sites called showToast() (or setError on state)
but never logged the underlying exception to debugLog. Same class of
blind spot as the oklch + worker-console fixes: the underlying failure
exists only in the user-facing toast, which the user may dismiss before
reporting anything.

utils/errorLog.ts (new)
- logError(source, event, err): normalizes unknown err to
  `${message}\n${stack}` for Error instances, String() for anything
  else, and calls debugLog(source, 'error', event, details).
- Does NOT toast. Logging and user-facing feedback stay orthogonal
  so the same helper fits both toast-emitting catches and state-
  storing catches (ImportModal setError, useTagSuggestions setError,
  etc.). Coupling them would force every caller to plumb showToast
  through, and would break for non-toasting catches.
- 4 unit tests: Error with stack, Error without stack, non-Error
  coercion (string/object/null/undefined), single side-effect contract.

Callsites updated (21 total):
- App.tsx (5): node create, arc create, bulk delete, duplicate node,
  delete node from context menu.
- NodeDetail (2): save, delete.
- ArcDetail (2): save, delete.
- DetailPanel (3): bulk add/remove label, bulk set property.
- WorkspaceManager (3): create, rename, delete.
- AnalysisPanel (1): algorithm failure (includes algorithm name in
  event for easier diagnosis).
- ImportModal (3): JSON parse/validate, import apply, undo import.
- SmartMatchingStep (1): embedding + matching pipeline.
- DuplicateScannerModal (3): scan, node merge, arc merge.
- useTagSuggestions (2): engine init, suggestLabels.
- useEmbeddingEngine (1): engine init.

Skipped (intentional, documented):
- safeStorage, propertyUtils (JSON parse fallback), DebugPill/
  ExportModal/ConventionsModal clipboard fallbacks, usePWAInstall
  analytics — silence IS the contract; no log needed.
- main.tsx, useGraphData, usePWAUpdate, seedService, eventBus,
  cssColor — already log via debugLog.
- Worker catches post error messages back to main via the existing
  message protocol; console.warn/error inside workers are already
  forwarded via workerDebugLog.ts from the previous commit.

330 tests pass (up from 326). Lint + build clean.

Docs
- CLAUDE.md: errorLog.ts added to the utils block.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new section describing the change.
- docs/TODO.md: central error-logging item removed (done).

commits.subject: chore(graph): cap rejectedLogged, extract useStylesReady, move getNodeColor
commits.body:
Self-audit cleanup on top of the worker-console commit.

rejectedLogged cap (#1)
- The cssColor rejection-log dedupe Set had no upper bound, inconsistent
  with the normalization cache I'd capped in the previous sweep. Same
  pathological exposure: a future caller that passes unique bad strings
  per call could balloon the Set. Same fix: FIFO eviction at
  MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES (256). After eviction, a re-seen rejected value
  re-logs once — acceptable tradeoff for bounded memory.
- New test covers the re-log-after-eviction behavior.

useStylesReady extraction (#2)
- GraphCanvas.tsx had crossed the 500-line "pause and consider"
  threshold (546 lines). The document.fonts.ready race-guard is a
  self-contained concern (cache invalidation + boolean flip +
  cancellation), so it moves cleanly into src/hooks/useStylesReady.ts.
- The lazy useState initializer (browsers without Font Loading API
  start ready) replaces a sync setState inside the effect. This avoids
  the `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` lint rule that fires on
  synchronous state mutation during effect execution.
- GraphCanvas.tsx now at 501 lines (back at threshold, not above).

getNodeColor relocation (#2)
- getNodeColor was inline in GraphCanvas despite being a pure function
  that hashes to PALETTE with a DaisyUI fallback. Moved to
  cytoscapeStyles.ts alongside the other 5 color resolvers
  (getArcColor, getNodeShape, getCytoscapeStylesheet). Removes the
  "two-calls-live-here" asymmetry doc comment from GraphCanvas — with
  getNodeColor moved, only the arcFallbackColor single-line call
  remains inline, which needs no commentary.

commits.subject: feat(debug): worker-side console interception forwards to DebugPill
commits.body:
Workers run @huggingface/transformers (ONNX Runtime + tokenizers). Any
console.warn / console.error they emit never reached DebugPill because
debugLog's console interception only patches the main thread. Same
class of blind spot as the oklch Cytoscape rejection — third-party
library warns into the void, nobody notices until a user reports a bug.

workers/workerDebugLog.ts (new)
- patchWorkerConsole() wraps self.console.warn/error in the worker
  global. Each call sends a typed envelope
  `{ type: 'debug', severity, event, details }` via postMessage before
  calling the original console method (so DevTools still shows the log).
- Reentrancy guard prevents infinite recursion if postMessage itself
  warns (e.g., from a polyfill).
- postMessage throws are swallowed so a debug-infrastructure bug can't
  crash the worker.
- Bails out when window is defined (main thread) or postMessage is
  unavailable — makes the module safe to import in tests without
  mutating the runner's console.
- isWorkerDebugEnvelope() type guard keeps the bridge dispatch sites
  type-safe.
- No auto-patch-on-import; workers call patchWorkerConsole() explicitly
  so tests have deterministic behavior.

workers/embeddingWorker.ts + tagSuggestionWorker.ts
- Each imports and calls patchWorkerConsole() at top-level, before
  anything else, so transformers.js warnings emitted during module
  load get forwarded.

services/embeddingWorkerBridge.ts + tagSuggestionService.ts
- handleWorkerMessage checks isWorkerDebugEnvelope FIRST (before init
  events), because transformers.js can warn during the init path.
  Matching envelopes dispatch to debugLog with source 'embedding' or
  'tagSuggestion' and the original severity.

workers/workerDebugLog.test.ts (new, 15 cases)
- Main-thread bail-out, postMessage-missing bail-out, warn/error
  forwarding, original-console passthrough, idempotency, argument
  coercion via String(), postMessage-throws-swallowed, reentrancy
  guard, plus isWorkerDebugEnvelope type-guard coverage (null/undef/
  wrong-type/unknown-severity/missing-event rejection).

Docs
- CLAUDE.md: project structure + Alpha debug system bullet updated to
  reflect worker-side interception and 500-entry cap.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new section describing the change.
- docs/TODO.md: worker-side debug logging item removed (now done).

325 tests pass (up from 310). Lint + build clean.

Urgency: 2

Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: chore(graph): bound cssColor cache, document fallback-call asymmetry, cover sentinel-caching
commits.body:
Three remaining items from the review sweep:

cssColor.ts (#12)
- MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES = 256 with FIFO eviction via a small cacheSet()
  helper. Practical workload is ~14 tokens × a handful of theme combos,
  so the cap is a defense against a future caller feeding unique strings
  per call (e.g., per-node computed colors) rather than a hot-path
  concern. FIFO (oldest-insertion wins eviction) beats LRU here because
  LRU needs delete-on-hit, defeating the cache's purpose.

cssColor.test.ts (#12, #18)
- New test "evicts oldest entry (FIFO) when the cache exceeds
  MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES": fills beyond the cap with unique inputs, mutates
  the shared rules Map in place (getCtx memoizes the first ctx, so a
  second installFakeCanvas wouldn't rebind), verifies color-0 evicted
  and color-259 still cached.
- New test "caches the sentinel itself when passed as raw input": the
  rejection check's `!isSentinel(raw)` short-circuit must let the
  sentinel value through to the cache path — otherwise a caller
  legitimately passing transparent-black pays the canvas cost every
  time.

GraphCanvas.tsx (#15)
- Inline comment documenting why the two getCSSColor calls live here
  rather than being pulled into cytoscapeStyles.ts: they serve per-
  element fallbacks (node without label, arc without title) on the
  data-sync path, not stylesheet-wide colors on the theme-apply path.
  Different lifecycles, different concerns — moving them would couple
  them to the wrong trigger.

Everything else from the review (item #11 zero-alpha forms, item #17
doc callsite count) is either already documented as a known limitation
or was an agent miscount. No code change needed for those.

All 310 tests pass (up from 308). Lint + build clean.

Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(graph): harden cssColor rejection path; restore wheelSensitivity via config; fonts.ready cache invalidation
commits.body:
Follow-up sweep on 2932b79 covering 9 findings from the review pass:

cssColor.ts
- normalizeColor now returns string | null. null signals rejection so
  getCSSColor can route a present-but-rejected CSS variable to its hex
  fallback rather than re-feeding the unparseable value to Cytoscape
  (which would silently drop the style again).
- Sentinel comparison via regex (SENTINEL_RE) tolerates whitespace variants
  — historical WebKit serializes rgba without inner spaces.
- getCtx() no longer marks itself initialized on failure. A first call
  before DOM-ready or in a hardened env no longer locks the module into
  a null ctx for its lifetime.
- try/catch around both fillStyle assignments. Trusted Types / sandbox
  throw is treated as rejection instead of crashing the render path.
- Each rejected raw value logs once via debugLog('canvas', 'warn', ...).
  Set-based dedupe so repeated calls don't flood. clearCSSColorCache()
  resets both the value cache and the dedupe set.

config.ts + GraphCanvas.tsx
- wheelSensitivity exposed as config.wheelSensitivity (default 0.3).
  Cytoscape's library default (1.0) is widely regarded as too aggressive
  on desktop mice; 0.3 is the common override. The one-time Cytoscape
  info warning is documented and expected. Deployments that prefer
  library default set window.__GRAPHIKI_CONFIG__ = { wheelSensitivity: 1.0 }.

GraphCanvas.tsx
- document.fonts.ready effect invalidates cssColor cache and bumps a
  stylesReady flag so the style-apply effect re-runs with fresh CSS
  reads. Fixes the "Cytoscape inits before DaisyUI stylesheet paints"
  race where hex fallbacks got cached under the real variable names
  and later theme-reapply effects hit stale cache.

debugLog.ts
- MAX_ENTRIES 200 → 500. The original oklch flood emitted 28 warnings
  on init alone, erasing boot/render context under the tight cap.
  Memory cost ~100 KB.

pwaDiagnostics.ts
- New "Graph Style Rejections" check scans the debug buffer for canvas-
  normalizer rejections and intercepted Cytoscape "style property is
  invalid" warns. Green when zero, red with count when non-zero —
  surfaces the class of bug as a diagnostic instead of burying it in
  console noise.

cssColor.test.ts
- 12 → 18 tests. Switched to vi.spyOn + vi.restoreAllMocks pattern so a
  mid-test throw can't poison sibling files. New cases: null-on-rejection,
  whitespace variants in sentinel serialization, fillStyle throw, log-
  once dedupe, fallback-on-rejection regression guard, fallback-when-
  both-reject defense, dedupe-set invalidation. Known-limitation test
  documents that non-transparent zero-alpha forms are misclassified as
  rejection (harmless — null routes to caller's hex fallback; DaisyUI
  tokens are all opaque).

Docs
- CLAUDE.md: cssColor and wheelSensitivity bullets updated for new
  behavior (null-on-rejection, fonts.ready invalidation, config gate).
- docs/CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md: Cytoscape Theme Awareness section
  expanded with null-return semantics, fonts.ready race fix, test
  infrastructure, known limitation.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: wheelSensitivity entry reframed — a library
  warning doesn't always mean the default is correct; the right fix
  was exposing the setting via config, not silencing by reverting.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: refreshed summary.
- docs/TODO.md: worker-side debug logging + central error-logging layer
  added as tech-debt items (deferred from this PR).

Deferred out of this PR (tracked in TODO.md):
- Worker-side console interception (embeddingWorker, tagSuggestionWorker
  emit warns that never reach DebugPill).
- Central error-logging layer wrapping the ~20 catch { showToast(...) }
  blocks that swallow underlying errors.

All 308 tests pass (up from 302). Lint + build clean.

commits.subject: fix(graph): normalize oklch() CSS variables before handing them to Cytoscape
commits.body:
DaisyUI v5 emits oklch() values for its semantic color tokens. Cytoscape's
style engine only accepts hex/rgb/named colors, so every themed property
backed by --color-warning / --color-info / --color-success / --color-secondary
/ --color-accent / --color-primary / --color-base-content was silently
rejected — selected-node borders, dual-query A/B/bridge/overlap colors,
analysis highlights, and arc fallback colors reverted to Cytoscape defaults
on every install that rendered the app in a modern browser. Only symptom
in logs was a flood of "The style property ... is invalid" warnings.

cssColor.ts now routes raw computed values through a shared 2D canvas's
fillStyle, which the HTML spec forces to serialize any valid CSS color to
#rrggbb / rgba(...) / #rrggbbaa. Results cached by raw input. A transparent-
black sentinel written before the real assignment detects inputs the browser
silently rejects so they stay un-cached and a later theme load can retry.
getCSSColor's signature is unchanged; all 7 callsites work as-is.

Also removes the custom wheelSensitivity: 0.3 (Cytoscape author's own
advice: "change only if you can guarantee all users will use the same
hardware" — a guarantee a cross-device PWA can't make). Kills the one-time
warning without trading it for unpredictable zoom feel across mice,
trackpads, and touchscreens.

Tests: cssColor.test.ts (12) covers normalization, hex pass-through, cache,
sentinel detection, non-caching of rejections, transparent-vs-sentinel
disambiguation, missing-canvas fallback, empty-input short-circuit, CSS
variable read + fallback, and cache invalidation. Full suite: 302/302.

Docs: CLAUDE.md (cssColor entry, Cytoscape normalization + wheelSensitivity
conventions), CODEBASE_DOCUMENTATION.md (expanded Cytoscape Theme Awareness),
AI_MISTAKES.md (new class-of-bug entry), SESSION_NOTES.md, TODO.md (follow-up
dev-only assertion on Cytoscape warnings).

Tags: bugfix, cytoscape, daisyui, theme, css, oklch, graph
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-and-dark-mode
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(pwa): drop favicon from icon-hash pipeline (Expo emit path unpredictable)
commits.body:
Vercel build failed on commit 3f2a479:

  [inject-icon-hashes] precached icon(s) missing from dist/:
    '/favicon.png'
  SW install would fail on cache.addAll()
  Error: Command '...' exited with 1

Root cause: app.json web.favicon points at assets/images/favicon.png,
but Expo's web export does NOT copy it verbatim — the emitted path is
version-dependent (.ico conversion vs PNG passthrough vs auto-discovery
only). The tiered fail-loud I added in the edges pass then (correctly)
refused to ship a PRECACHE list pointing at a non-existent file.

Resolution: favicon is outside the icon-hash pipeline. Removed from
ICON_URLS, SW_EXPECTED_URLS, PRECACHE_ASSETS, and the test fixture.
+html.tsx now references /favicon.ico (best guess at what Expo emits
+ matches browser auto-discovery); vercel.json passes both .ico and
.png through so whichever Expo emits isn't swallowed by the SPA
catch-all. Favicon is small + HTTP-cached aggressively + dominated by
the OS icon cache for installed PWAs, so skipping ?v= on it is an
acceptable trade.

Verified end-to-end against a fixture with zero favicon files in
dist/ — build chain (expo export -> inject-icon-hashes ->
inject-sw-version) exits 0, all other icons versioned correctly.

Tags: pwa, build, vercel, fix

Urgency: 5
Impact: infrastructure

Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-polish
Semver: patch

   roots expanded to include scripts/__tests__/.

2. src/hooks/__tests__/usePWAUpdate.test.ts (11 tests): unit tests for
   the visibility-aware polling helpers via a new __testing export on
   usePWAUpdate. Exposes handleVisibilityChange, start/stopUpdateInterval,
   checkForUpdate, plus reset/setRegistration hooks so tests can run in
   isolation. Covers idempotent start/stop, correct clearInterval handle,
   null-registration safety, async-rejection swallowing, hidden/visible/
   prerender transitions, and a full visible→hidden→visible roundtrip.

   react-test-renderer-based tests for the extracted disclosure. Covers

commits.subject: feat(theme): declare color-scheme so native form controls match theme
commits.body:
Native browser widgets (scrollbars, default checkboxes, autofill tint,
default buttons) were rendering against the OS-default scheme rather
than the active DaisyUI theme — visible on dark-mode users with the
default-light system pref and vice versa.

Two-part fix:
1. <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark"> in +html.tsx declares
   both modes are supported. Browser picks based on system preference
   for the pre-React paint — no flash before JS boots.
2. useTheme sets document.documentElement.style.colorScheme on each
   theme change so the explicit user choice overrides the system
   default once React is up.

CSS-on-html.dark was rejected as an alternative because it depends on
how Uniwind surfaces the theme in the DOM (not a public contract).

Tags: theme, a11y, ux

commits.subject: perf(pwa): content-hash SW_BUILD over dist/_expo/static/ bundles
commits.body:
SW_BUILD used to be an ISO timestamp injected on every deploy, which
meant STATIC_CACHE / DYNAMIC_CACHE names rotated on every build — even
when no shipped content changed. The activate handler's name-based
cleanup then wiped every user's cache on every deploy, defeating the
purpose of precaching, and every user got an "update available" prompt
for noop deploys.

New approach: SHA-256 over every file under dist/_expo/static/ (sorted
recursive walk, filename + bytes in the digest), truncated to 12 hex
chars. Same content → same version → identical SW bytes → browser's
byte-compare detects no change → no spurious update prompt, cache
persists. Changed content → new version → SW updates as expected.

Fail-loud if the __SW_BUILD_VERSION__ placeholder is missing (idempotent
re-run on same dist/ or public/sw.js drift).

Tags: pwa, performance, caching, fail-loud
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-polish
Semver: patch
commits.title: content-hash SW_BUILD over dist/_expo/static/ bundles

commits.subject: fix(pwa): precache every icon + tiered fail-loud for missing precached files
commits.body:
Two hardenings to the icon-hash infrastructure surfaced by the edges /
skipped audit passes:

1. public/sw.js PRECACHE_ASSETS now includes /favicon.png and the 1024
   maskable icon alongside the existing 192 / 512 / apple-touch entries.
   Previously, a user who installed the PWA while online and then went
   offline could hit missing-icon cases during Chrome WebAPK generation
   or home-screen icon rendering. All install-time-referenced icons are
   now cache-populated on SW install.

2. scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs: a URL in SW_EXPECTED_URLS whose file
   is missing from dist/ is now a hard error (the SW would fail
   cache.addAll() at install, silently breaking offline mode for users).
   Lazy-cached-only icons still warn. Plus a sanity check at script
   load that SW_EXPECTED_URLS ⊆ ICON_URLS catches typos before they
   ship.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): wire icon-hash injector into Vercel build + mark favicon immutable
commits.body:
Vercel uses buildCommand directly, not `npm run build`, so production
deploys were skipping inject-icon-hashes.mjs and shipping un-versioned
icon URLs. Every cache layer below the OS would keep serving the old
icon indefinitely — the exact failure mode the pattern doc warns about.

Also mark /favicon.png as immutable since the URL is now content-addressed
via ?v=<hash>. /assets/images/* already gets the treatment via the
/assets/(.*) rule; /favicon.png was falling through to max-age=0 in the
catch-all. Bytes change → URL changes → browsers/CDN can safely cache
the versioned form for a year.

Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(pwa): cold-pass fixes — fail-loud SW, prerender guard, dead import
commits.body:
Four findings from cold-branch audit:

1. inject-icon-hashes silently skipped missing sw.js PRECACHE literals,
   the exact format-drift failure the pattern warns about. Added
   SW_EXPECTED_URLS set + explicit error with missing list. Regex-match
   accepts bare or already-versioned literal so idempotent re-runs and
   hash refresh both work.
2. statSync imported but unused. Removed.
3. usePWAUpdate.handleVisibilityChange used a bare else, which treats
   'prerender' as 'visible' and starts polling during prerender. Tightened
   to explicit === 'visible'.
4. icon-hashes.json was written to public/ (source tree) in addition to
   dist/. Dropped the public/ write — dist/ is the only consumer — and
   removed the now-unnecessary .gitignore entry.

Verified via dry-run: happy path, idempotent re-run, hash refresh after
icon byte change, and fail-loud on removed PRECACHE literal all pass.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): content-hash icon URLs to bust browser/CDN/SW caches
commits.body:
When icons change, un-versioned URLs survive "clear site data" and
PWA reinstall because every cache layer (HTTP, CDN, SW precache,
WebAPK) keys off URL identity. Add a post-export script that hashes
each referenced icon and rewrites HTML link tags, manifest.json
icons[], and sw.js PRECACHE_ASSETS to ?v=<8-char-sha256>.

Implementation choices:
- Post-export rewrite (not +html.tsx at render) — matches the existing
  inject-sw-version.mjs pattern; keeps Metro bundling of +html.tsx
  unchanged.
- Precache the versioned URL directly (not strip ?v= on lookup) so
  the custom sw.js's caches.match() stays exact-URL.
- Fix CACHE_FIRST_PATTERNS regex to accept query strings — without
  this, versioned icons fall through to network-first and break
  offline.
- Collapsed disclosure in InstallInstructionsModal surfaces the one
  cache layer the web can't fix (OS home-screen/launcher icon).

Verified via dry-run against a simulated dist/: 3 HTML rewrites, 3
manifest icons, 3 sw.js precache entries, idempotent on re-run.

Tags: pwa, infrastructure, caching, build
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-polish
Semver: minor
commits.title: content-hash icon URLs to bust browser/CDN/SW caches

commits.subject: perf(pwa): pause SW update polling when tab is hidden
commits.body:
Current setInterval runs even when the tab is backgrounded, wasting
work and battery. Add a module-level visibilitychange listener that
clears the interval on hidden and resumes with an immediate check on
visible. Also skip starting the interval at init time if the tab is
already hidden.

Attached inside initUpdateDetection() alongside the controllerchange
listener, so the existing _initialized guard prevents duplicate
listeners under HMR and Platform.OS !== 'web' still gates native
builds.

commits.body:
Fresh-eyes branch audit after the ship sweep. Found mostly clean code;
four small cleanups worth applying:

COLD1 — debugLog.ts:207 debugAdd docstring claimed "No-op in
production" but the TEMPORARY marker immediately below removed that
gate for PWA alpha. A reader skimming the docstring would conclude
"this is safe in prod, nothing logs" and skip a PII review. Rewrote
to describe actual behaviour + cross-ref the TODO entry.

COLD2 — debugLog.ts:143 removed the dead `MAX_ENTRIES` alias that
just re-named the imported DEBUG_MAX_ENTRIES. Single-callsite; the
import name is already clear.

COLD3 — consoleCapture.ts describeConsoleArgs docstring clarified
that the `args` field in details holds only args[1..], not the full
console.* args array. A reader could otherwise interpret the field
as the whole argument list.

COLD5 — debugLog.ts:278 renamed `const window = Dimensions.get('window')`
to `const windowDims`. The function body deliberately reaches for
globalThis.navigator / globalThis.location to dodge the shadow; a
future edit using `window.foo` expecting the browser global would
silently get the Dimensions object. Removes the trap.

Skipped (confirmed correct / YAGNI / sub-threshold):
- COLD4: reentrant flag at function scope vs module — paranoia for
  an impossible partial-install scenario (PATCHED_MARK is atomic).
- COLD6: DebugPill.tsx at 493 lines, under the 500 "consider
  extraction" threshold. LogRow is a reasonable future extraction.

commits.subject: test(debug): cover 4 regression gaps identified by ship/tests trigger
commits.body:
T1 — Circular buffer overflow semantics. Fills the buffer to
MAX_ENTRIES (200), adds one more, asserts length stays at 200,
oldest entry shifted out, newest present. Pins the contract so a
refactor of the shift() location can't silently break it.

T2 — debugSubscribe unsubscribe. Direct test of the returned
unsub function: subscribe → replay fires → debugAdd → cb fires
again → unsub() → debugAdd → cb does NOT fire. Previously only
tested implicitly via other tests calling unsub() for cleanup.

T3 — `console.error()` with zero args produces a synthetic
`(empty)` event. Dead-unless-tested branch in describeConsoleArgs.
Rare in practice but pins the contract.

T4 — debugGenerateReport with zero log entries. Contains the
env + `--- Log (0 entries) ---` headers, doesn't throw. Guards
against refactors that assume non-empty entries.

- DebugPill / PwaDiagnosticsTab render tests: react-native-web

commits.subject: a11y(debug): role=alert on boundary, tablist on tabs, live region on Copy feedback
commits.body:
Three small accessibility gaps from ship/a11y trigger. All three are
state-announcement issues — the visible state is correct, but screen
readers weren't getting the information.

A11Y1 — DebugRootBoundary.tsx: the DOM fallback div rendered when
the pill crashes had no role="alert". Screen readers stayed silent
on crash. Added role="alert" to match the pre-React boot pill's
behaviour (+html.tsx also uses role="alert" for the same
"something-broke" banner semantics). Inconsistency resolved.

A11Y2 — DebugPill.tsx: the three tab buttons each had
accessibilityRole="tab" + accessibilityState.selected, but the
wrapping View had no accessibilityRole="tablist" — per ARIA spec,
role="tab" elements should live inside a role="tablist" container.
Added it to the parent View. Spec-complete now.

A11Y3 — DebugPill.tsx: the Copy button's visible text transitions
between 'Copy' → 'Copied' / 'Failed' → 'Copy' after user action.
Sighted users see the confirmation; screen readers got nothing.
Added accessibilityLiveRegion="polite" to the feedback Text so the
state change is announced without interrupting current SR speech.

Verified correct, no changes:
- Collapsed pill's accessibilityLabel enumerates counts/severities.
- Collapse / Copy / Clear buttons all have role=button + descriptive
  labels.
- numberOfLines truncation doesn't hide content from SRs.
- Severity dots are sighted-only visual redundancy (label covers
  equivalent info).
- Boot pill already has role=alert.

Noted, not fixed: collapsed pill is 56×28 (below WCAG AAA 44×44
but above AA 24×24). Debug tool in a corner — deliberate tradeoff
to not crowd the app.

tsc 0, jest 92/92, expo web build clean.

commits.subject: fix(debug): drop location-PII from debug entries + document console-capture scope
commits.body:
Four debugAdd callsites captured user-identifying location data in
plaintext. When a user copies the debug report to a support chat,
those values went along verbatim. URL redaction doesn't catch bare
lat/lng pairs or address labels.

PRIV1 — src/hooks/useLocation.ts:
  - Line 82 (GPS location acquired): was including 4-decimal lat/lng
    (~11m precision, identifies home/work). Now just the event label.
  - Line 112 (Manual location set): was falling back to `${lat}, ${lng}`
    when no label was provided. Now just the event label (and the
    label itself is dropped per PRIV2 below when it IS provided,
    via LocationSearch — which is where the label originates).
  Devs who need coords for a specific debug session can read
  `location.coords` directly in DevTools.

PRIV2 — src/components/search/LocationSearch.tsx:
  - Line 185 (Location selected from suggestions): was including the
    user-selected short label (e.g., "123 Main Street"). User search
    selections often identify home / workplace / frequent destinations.
    Now just the event label.
  - Line 205 (Recent search selected): same. Recent searches from
    AsyncStorage could expose a usage pattern.

PRIV3 — src/debug/consoleCapture.ts: the capture stores console args
raw (as `details.args`). redactUrls only handles URLs — not email-
like patterns, JWT-like tokens outside URLs, or arbitrary user-field
objects passed to console.*. Documented the caveat in the module's
header block, pointed at the existing TODO.md entry for reverting
the always-on state post-alpha (which eliminates this surface in
production).

Verified clean (no fix):
- useAuth.ts: never logs email/password. `error.message` from Supabase
  is generic ("Invalid credentials"), not PII.
- Supabase session in localStorage: platform default, not branch.
- PWA install analytics (last 50 events): event type + browser +
  timestamp, no PII.
- AsyncStorage recent searches: on-device only, not in debug surface.
- sessionStorage recovery counter / just-updated timestamp: non-PII.
- env.url: already redacted at construction (prior commit).
- Debug report clipboard copy: URL redaction already in place.

commits.subject: fix(debug): LogRow renders event + details through redactUrls (security/SEC1)
commits.body:
The Env tab redacts env.url at construction and debugGenerateReport
runs a final redactUrls sweep before the clipboard copy — so Env-tab
screenshots and pasted reports are both screenshot-safe. But the
Log tab's on-screen rendering of entry.event and entry.details did
NOT redact. A screenshot of the open pill's Log tab could leak URLs
that the report-copy path would redact.

Concrete leak:
1. App does console.error('Failed:', new Error('POST https://api/?apikey=X ...'))
   or debugAdd('supabase', 'error', 'Request failed',
     { url: 'https://api.com/?apikey=SECRET' })
2. Entry lands in buffer; event + details contain the URL.
3. User opens DebugPill → Log tab. URL + apikey visible on screen.
4. Screenshot → leaked.

Fix: apply redactUrls in LogRow at render time to both
  - entry.event (string)
  - the stringified entry.details (whether details is already a
    string or an object that needs safeStringify)

Matches the screenshot-safety pattern already applied to env.url.
The raw entry.details are still unmodified in the in-memory buffer
(preserves programmatic access for any future debug tooling); only
the displayed strings go through redaction.

New test covers the exact chain LogRow uses:
  redactUrls(typeof details === 'string' ? details : safeStringify(details))
Three assertions: structured detail with URL, string detail with URL,
event string with URL. All three paths must not leak the query.

Other security findings from the sweep:
- window.__debugPushError / __debugClearLoadTimer exposed globals:
  low-risk, XSS-safe by design (textContent, not innerHTML).
- 'unsafe-inline' CSP for scripts: pre-existing tradeoff, not touched
  by this branch.
- Supabase anon key + Mapbox token: intentionally public per each
  platform's security model (RLS server-side; URL-restricted token).
- localStorage writes: timestamps + browser name, no PII.
- Linking.openURL / window.open: hardcoded URLs only, no user-input
  redirect surface.
- eval / new Function: none.
- Exposed secrets in branch code: none.

commits.subject: fix(debug): redactUrls preserves trailing sentence punctuation (edges/EDG1)
commits.body:
The existing char classes in both redactUrls passes only excluded
whitespace and URL-delimiter chars (`#'"<>`). That greedily consumed
trailing sentence punctuation — the `.` at end of a prose sentence,
commas before conjunctions, closing parens / brackets wrapping the
URL, exclamation marks. Before:

  "Reported at https://a.com/?t=1."    → "...https://a.com/?[redacted]"
  "Check https://a.com/?t=1, thanks"   → "...https://a.com/?[redacted] thanks"
  "See (https://a.com/?t=1)"           → "See (https://a.com/?[redacted]"
  "[https://a.com/?t=1]"               → "[https://a.com/?[redacted]"
  "Error: https://a.com/?t=1!"         → "...https://a.com/?[redacted]"

The debug report is pasted into support chats; characters from the
user's prose going missing makes it look like a bug on the report
side.

Fix: extend the regex char class with a conditional — `.,;!)]` are
excluded ONLY WHEN followed by whitespace or end-of-string. Inside
URL values they still match (e.g., `?v=1.2.3` keeps the dots inside
the redaction; OAuth JWT fragments `#access_token=abc.def.ghi` keep
all three dots in the redacted body). After:

  "Reported at https://a.com/?t=1."    → "...https://a.com/?[redacted]."
  "Check https://a.com/?t=1, thanks"   → "...https://a.com/?[redacted], thanks"
  "See (https://a.com/?t=1)"           → "See (https://a.com/?[redacted])"
  "[https://a.com/?t=1]"               → "[https://a.com/?[redacted]]"
  "Error: https://a.com/?t=1!"         → "...https://a.com/?[redacted]!"
  "https://a.com/?v=1.2.3"             → "https://a.com/?[redacted]" (unchanged)
  "https://a.com/?v=1.2.3."            → "https://a.com/?[redacted]." (period preserved)
  "https://app.com/cb#access_token=abc.def.ghi"
                                       → "https://app.com/cb#[redacted]" (unchanged)
  "See https://app.com/#access_token=abc."
                                       → "See https://app.com/#[redacted]."

Regex pattern used for each URL body segment:
  (?:[^\s#'"<>.,;!)\]]|[.,;!)\]](?!\s|$))*

Applied to both redactUrls passes (query + fragment). 10 new test
cases — 5 for the punctuation-preserved scenarios, 5 for the
punctuation-inside-value scenarios.

All existing redactUrls tests still pass unchanged.

Other edges verified during the sweep:
- Circular buffer at exactly MAX_ENTRIES (200): notifyAll runs after
  the shift, subscribers always see ≤ 200.
- safeStringify with undefined/null/0/''/Symbol/BigInt: all caught
  by the `typeof arg !== 'object'` early return before JSON.stringify
  (important — BigInt would otherwise throw).
- Boot pill IIFE with `document.body === null` (runs in `<head>`):
  falls back to `documentElement`. position:fixed renders correctly.
- console.error() with 0 args: synthetic `(empty)` event.
- Hash-routes with query-style params: step 1 requires `?` right after
  URL; fragment with `/` prefix blocks step 2. Preserved.
- 0 entries rendering: "No log entries yet." shown.

assertions each), expo web build clean.

commits.subject: refactor(types): tighten two loose casts from ship/types trigger
commits.body:
T1 — src/debug/debugLog.ts:291. The Safari-standalone check used a
looser cast than the same check in PwaDiagnosticsTab.tsx:133 and
usePWAInstall.ts:100. All three check `navigator.standalone === true`;
the other two used `Navigator & { standalone?: boolean }`, but
debugLog used `unknown as Record<string, unknown>` which types
`standalone` as `unknown` and relies on `=== true` coincidence.
Aligned with the established pattern.

a double-loose cast (`unknown as Record<string, unknown>` + downstream
`as BeforeInstallPromptEvent | undefined` on each property). Replaced

      __deferredInstallPrompt?: BeforeInstallPromptEvent;
    }
Both downstream assertions removed — properties are now typed
directly. Consistent with the `Window & { … }` pattern used on lines
90 and 100 of the same file.

assertion) — pre-existing, idiomatic, not touched by this branch.

commits.subject: fix(debug): cancel PwaDiagnosticsTab diagnostics on unmount (race/R1)
commits.body:
The diagnostics runner (runPwaDiagnostics) is async — fetches
/manifest.json and awaits navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistration().
Each step calls the `add` callback (which is setLines) to append a
result line. If the user switches debug tabs, collapses the pill,
or unmounts mid-flight, subsequent add() calls setState on an
unmounted component. React 18+ silently drops the update, so there
was no visible warning — but the closure + pending I/O still held
memory until the promises resolved.

Fix: `let cancelled = false;` flag set in the effect's cleanup
function. The add callback short-circuits when cancelled, so the
React state update path terminates immediately on unmount even
though the underlying fetch/getRegistration promises continue to
settle in the background.

Kept the rest of the audit findings as confirmed-correct:
- setInterval in usePWAUpdate intentionally page-lifetime.
- Boot pill clear() vs window.error race: safe either way (JS single-
  threaded; both orderings leave a consistent state).
- DebugPill subscriber closure: [] deps + module-stable references,
  no stale-closure risk.
- consoleCapture reentrant flag: local scope, shared between
  wrappedError and wrappedWarn, try/finally managed.
- debugRootMount DOMContentLoaded + `{ once: true }` + idempotency
  flag: triple-safe.

commits.subject: fix(debug): 4 errors from ship/errors trigger — consistent errDetails + copy-guard
commits.body:
E1 — useLocation.ts:92 was catching `} catch {` (no binding), so the
GPS failure reason (GeolocationPositionError's `code` + `message`)
was discarded. debugAdd got 'GPS location failed' with no details.
Bound the error and pass errDetails(err) — user-facing string stays
the same, but the debug log now carries the underlying failure.

E2 — useStations.ts:158 was still on the legacy string-detail
pattern: `debugAdd('supabase', 'error', '...', message)` where
`message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown error'`.
All ten error-coercing callsites were migrated to errDetails in the
earlier refactor; this one was missed because the extracted `message`
is also used for the user-facing state.error string. Kept the `msg`
extraction for the UI, passed `errDetails(err)` to debugAdd so the
stack is preserved.

E3 — same pattern in three admin flows (bulk-update, bulk-import,
station/[id].tsx). All deliberately un-migrated in the original
errDetails pass because `msg` is used for showToast. Same shape
applied: keep `msg` for UI, pass `errDetails(err)` to debugAdd.

E4 — DebugPill.handleCopy didn't wrap `debugGenerateReport()` +
clipboard in try/catch. A crash in either would escape as an
unhandled rejection → global rejection listener → boot-pill banner.
A failed copy would masquerade as a boot failure. Wrapped the whole
body; on any throw, setCopyFeedback('Failed') — same UX as a
clipboard-permission denial.

Deferred/skipped per the findings: usePersistedState.ts:111,
TutorialModal.tsx (3x), InstallInstructionsModal.tsx,
usePWAInstall.ts (4x) — all documented-intentional silent catches
for non-critical persistence / localStorage-unavailable paths.
Also skipped: debugLog's three internal try/catch blocks (safeStringify
fallback, notifyAll subscriber isolation, debugSubscribe replay) —
all documented isolation policies.
