Audit finding extension to #16 — sections/Timeline.jsx was 735 lines,
235 over the 500-line component soft-limit. The five chart-data
useMemo blocks (activityChart, codeChangesChart, urgencyTrend,
debtTrend, impactTrend) made up ~350 lines of the file and had no
React-render surface area — they're pure data shaping with theme
colour resolution.

Extracted into a new hook:

  dashboard/js/hooks/useTimelineCharts.js  (416 lines)

The hook calls useApp() internally and returns all five chart-data
objects from a single call. Each useMemo retains its original
dependency array
(filteredCommits, commitsLoaded, state.data?.summary?.daily,
isMobile, state.darkMode, state.themeAccent, state.themeMuted) so
fine-grained memoization behaviour matches the previous inline
version exactly — a single chart still rebuilds independently of
the others when its inputs change.

After:

    under the 500-line soft-limit)
  dashboard/js/hooks/useTimelineCharts.js: new (416 lines)

Timeline.jsx now imports the hook and replaces the five useMemo
blocks with a single destructuring call. The summaryData useMemo,
commitListContent useMemo, handleCardClick / handlePeriodClick
callbacks, and the entire JSX render tree are unchanged.

The hook's `urgencyTrendData` includes a `sortedMonths` field so
`impactTrendData` can reuse the date axis (alignment requirement
preserved from the pre-extraction version where impactTrendData
read `urgencyTrendData?.sortedMonths` from the closure).

Imports trimmed in Timeline.jsx — getAdditions, getDeletions,
getUTCMonthKey, excludeIncompleteLastMonth, getSeriesColor,
withOpacity, buildRepoColorMap are no longer needed in the
component file (they live in the hook now).

  read theme accent" test now reads dashboard/js/hooks/useTimelineCharts.js

commits.subject: refactor(debug): split DebugPill into pill chrome + tabs + styles
commits.body:
Audit finding extension to #16 — DebugPill.jsx was 527 lines,
27 lines over the 500-line component soft-limit from CLAUDE.md
"Code Organization". The file held three concerns: the pill /
panel chrome (state, subscriptions, copy actions), the three
tab body components (Log / Environment / PWA Diagnostics), and
a ~140-line inline styles object with severity / source colour
maps.

Split into three files inside a new `debug/` subdirectory so
they stay grouped without polluting the top-level components/
listing:

  dashboard/js/components/DebugPill.jsx              — 200 lines
  dashboard/js/components/debug/debugStyles.js       — 180 lines
  dashboard/js/components/debug/DebugTabs.jsx        — 187 lines

debugStyles.js exports the `styles` object plus `SOURCE_COLORS`
and `SEVERITY_COLORS` lookup maps. Standalone JS module (no JSX)
because both consumers (DebugPill chrome + DebugTabs bodies)
need access. The hex-literal exception for the debug pill is
documented in CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts" — these
files inherit that exception with an extended rationale block
in the file header explaining why DaisyUI semantic tokens can't
be used here.

DebugTabs.jsx exports `LogTab`, `EnvironmentTab`,
`PWADiagnosticsTab`, and the `runDiagnostics()` async helper.
Pure presentation + the live-probe code; the parent owns all
state and stale-run cancellation via a monotonic counter ref.
Brings `getBrowserName()` along with `runDiagnostics()` because
it's only called from there.

DebugPill.jsx (now 200 lines, well under the limit) is reduced
to: pill / panel chrome render, debug-entry subscription,
auto-scroll effect, diagnostics fetch effect, copy/clear
handlers, and the manual-copy textarea fallback. Imports the
three tab components by name and renders them via the existing
`activeTab === N` discriminator.

Build clean (562.30 kB → 562.43 kB, +0.13 kB module scaffolding).
60/60 tests pass.

commits.subject: docs(history): split HISTORY.md, archive pre-April-2026 entries
commits.body:
Audit finding #15 — HISTORY.md had grown to 4401 lines covering every
significant change since 2026-01-18. The active section (April 2026
entries from the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep through the audit cleanup) was
buried under three months of older history, and reading the file
top-to-bottom to find recent context was impractical.

Split into two files at the `## 2026-03-27` heading:

  docs/HISTORY.md         — 1518 active lines (2026-04-15 → 2026-04-01)
  docs/HISTORY_ARCHIVE.md — 2898 archived lines (2026-03-27 → 2026-01-18)

The cut is line-based — no entry content was modified. Each file gets
a header explaining the split rule and pointing at the other:

  - HISTORY.md ends with an "Older Entries" pointer to the archive.
  - HISTORY_ARCHIVE.md opens with a header explaining when entries
    should be moved here ("only when HISTORY.md becomes unwieldy
    again — the 2026-04-15 split happened at ~4400 lines").

CLAUDE.md docs table updated:
  - HISTORY.md row label clarifies "active = April 2026 onward"
  - New HISTORY_ARCHIVE.md row with rule "only when trimming
    HISTORY.md once it grows unwieldy again"

This pattern lets the same split happen again in the future without
ambiguity — a future agent reading CLAUDE.md sees the rule, reads the
archive header, and knows to make a fresh split rather than touching
the existing archive.

commits.subject: docs(session): rewrite SESSION_NOTES.md as compact snapshot
commits.body:
Audit finding #14 — SESSION_NOTES.md had grown to 624 lines, mostly
historical entries from the round-3 custom-CSS sweep, the
post-migration audit pass, the vanilla-DaisyUI 8-phase migration, and
the zero-arbitrary-values sweep. Per CLAUDE.md the doc is supposed to
be a "compact context snapshot for session continuity" — rewritten at
session end.

Replaced with a 70-line snapshot that captures only what the next
session needs:
  - Current branch + current state (1-paragraph dashboard status)
  - Vanilla-DaisyUI policy invariants (zero custom classes, documented
    exceptions list)
  - Testing posture (Playwright removed, single tripwire active)
  - Last-session work summary with commit shas (1 line each)
  - Open items requiring next-session action (aggregate.js
    investigation, live browser spot-check, TODO.md pointer)
  - Doc pointers for deeper context

The 554 lines of historical narrative removed all duplicated content
that's already in HISTORY.md and the git log — keeping it in
SESSION_NOTES inflated the file and made finding the actually-current
state harder. New entries should follow the same compact structure:
the next agent reads SESSION_NOTES first, so it should give them
exactly the context to start work, not a re-tellling of what
HISTORY.md already documents.

commits.subject: docs(styles): trim styles.css from 521 → 164 lines
commits.body:
Audit finding #13 — styles.css carried ~360 lines of "tombstone"
comments documenting CSS classes that were deleted during the
2026-04-13/14 vanilla-DaisyUI sweep. Each deleted class had a
multi-line block describing what it used to do and what it migrated
to, layered on top of months of similar archaeology from earlier
refactors.

The tombstones served their purpose (helped reviewers verify nothing
was missed during the sweep) but they're now dead context — every
deleted class is gone, every consumer is on DaisyUI utilities, and
the migration history lives in HISTORY.md and git log. Keeping them
inflates the file 3.4x with no consumer benefit.

Kept (everything in the file is still load-bearing):
  - @import "tailwindcss"
  - @source not data dirs (phantom utility prevention)
  - DaisyUI @plugin block with BEGIN/END GENERATED markers

commits.subject: chore(hygiene): inline-style → utility, mobile copy, z-index doc
commits.body:
Audit findings #11, #12, #18 — three small hygiene fixes batched
together because each one is a single-line touch.

#11 Progress.jsx semver doughnut sizing (line 347):
  Was: style={{ height: isMobile ? '180px' : '200px' }} — inline
       JS expression for what's a static breakpoint switch.
  Now: className="h-45 sm:h-50" — Tailwind h-45 = 11.25rem = 180px,
       h-50 = 12.5rem = 200px. The sm: breakpoint (640px) matches
       isMobile's window.innerWidth < 640 check exactly so the
       behaviour is identical without the inline style override.
       Per CLAUDE.md "no inline style={} unless runtime-computed
       from data" — this is breakpoint-derived, not data-derived.

#12 QuickGuide.jsx step 3 copy:
  Was: "Use the filter button to focus on..." — only mentioned the
       mobile filter button, ignored the desktop sidebar pattern.
  Now: Covers both: "On larger screens, use the filter panel on the
       left ... On phones and tablets, tap the filter button in the
       header to open the same panel." Aligns the help with the
       actual responsive layout where lg: shows a permanent sidebar.

#18 styles.css :root z-index comment:
  Was: 4-line cryptic note ("Sub-layers: 21 = sticky-header above
       tabs (20), 58 = modal backdrop above menu (50)").
  Now: Documents the actual project pattern — most consumers use
       stock z-N Tailwind utilities directly, the named CSS vars
       only exist for sub-layer values that need
       `z-[var(--z-...)]` arbitrary utilities (z-21, z-70). Lists
       every consumer with a one-liner so a reader can trace any
       z-utility back to its layer in the stacking scheme.

Build clean, 60/60 tests pass.

commits.subject: refactor(cleanup): drop 5 dead exports, simplify Header filter callback
commits.body:
Audit findings #7, #8, #9, #10 — five exported symbols had zero
consumers in the dashboard JS code, and Header's filter-open callback
had a stale guard pattern from before the OPEN_FILTER_SIDEBAR action
existed.

Dead exports removed (verified zero consumers via repo-wide grep):
  - urlParams.js searchParams       — relic from when chartColors.js
    parsed ?colors= overrides; the override system was deleted in
    the 2026-04-14 vanilla-DaisyUI sweep
  - chartColors.js getSemanticPalette + SEMANTIC_CYCLE_VARS — only
    callers were each other; getSeriesColor() is the sole consumer
    pattern in JSX
  - utils.js getAllTags             — never consumed; tag aggregation
    happens via aggregateByTag() inside section components instead

Demoted from exported to module-private (still used internally):
  - utils.js getAnonymousName       — only sanitizeName() calls it
  - utils.js buildHolidaySet        — only the module-level
    holidaySet constant calls it
  - utils.js holidaySet             — only getWorkPattern() reads it

Header.handleOpenFilters refactor:
  - Was: dispatch TOGGLE behind a `if (!filterSidebarOpen)` guard
    with [filterSidebarOpen, dispatch] deps — re-bound on every
    sidebar state change.
  - Now: dispatch OPEN_FILTER_SIDEBAR (idempotent reducer action,
    sets to true regardless of current state). Stable callback,
    only [dispatch] in deps. The guard pattern dated back to when
    only TOGGLE existed; OPEN_FILTER_SIDEBAR has been in the
    reducer since the layout simplification.

Build clean (562.18 kB → 562.18 kB, identical), 60/60 tests pass.

commits.body:
Audit finding #5 + #6 — the existing CLAUDE.md exception lists were
terse one-liners that didn't explain why each escape hatch exists.
A reader reviewing a PR that adds a new bracket value or hex literal
couldn't tell if it fits an established pattern or is a new violation.

Bracket-value list expanded from 3 → 4 entries with per-entry rationale:
  - z-[var(--z-sticky-header)]: design-token reference, explains why
    z-21 isn't in stock Tailwind scale
  - z-[var(--z-toast)]: design-token reference, explains why z-70 is
    above stock Tailwind ceiling of z-50
  - grid-cols-[auto_repeat(7,1fr)]: functional grid template needed
    for hour-label/day-column row alignment
  - max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]: NEW. HamburgerMenu portal dropdown
    clamps to viewport width minus safe margin, calc() not stock

Hex-literal list expanded from 2 → 3 entries with per-entry rationale:
  - DebugPill.jsx: isolated React root that survives stylesheet
    failure, can't depend on Tailwind classes loading
  - themes.js #808080: single safety-net default for getMetaColor()
  - generated/themeMeta.js: NEW. Auto-generated file containing PWA
    meta theme-color hex values, browser parses as literal hex

commits.subject: fix(a11y): SettingsPane View Level uses native fieldset + radio inputs
commits.body:
The View Level radiogroup was a custom ARIA pattern with three
`<div role="radio" tabIndex={0}>` elements. Three accessibility issues:

  1. Every option had `tabIndex={0}` — Tab cycled through all 3
     individually instead of jumping past the radiogroup as a unit.
  2. No arrow-key navigation between options. WAI-ARIA radiogroup
     pattern requires Up/Down/Left/Right to move focus between radios;
     the original onKeyDown handled only Enter/Space.
  3. Screen readers announced "1 of 3" three times per group (once
     per div), instead of once for the whole group with each radio
     just announcing its label + checked state.

Replaced with the native HTML pattern:
  - `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` for the group container/label
  - `<input type="radio" name="view-level">` per option (HTML radio
    group association via shared `name` attribute)
  - `<label>` wrapping each input + descriptive content (clicking
    anywhere on the label toggles the input via native `for`-less
    label association)
  - DaisyUI `radio radio-primary` for the visual radio bullet
  - `aria-describedby` linking each input to its description text

Native HTML handles all three problems for free:
  - Roving tabindex: only ONE radio in a group is in the tab order
    at a time (the checked one if any, otherwise the first)
  - Arrow-key navigation: Up/Down/Left/Right move focus between
    radios in the same `name` group, automatically checking the
    focused one
  - Screen reader announces "View Level, radio group, Executive,
    1 of 3, checked" for the group + first radio, then "Management,
    2 of 3, not checked" etc. as user navigates

The visual layout (TOGGLE_ROW_CLASSES) is unchanged. The radio
bullet is `order-2` so it appears on the right of the row (matching
the previous checkmark icon position) instead of the default left.

Verified:
  - vite build clean
  - 60/60 tests pass
  - .radio + .radio-primary classes ship in built CSS

Tags: fix, a11y, ui, daisyui
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-audit
Semver: patch
commits.title: SettingsPane View Level uses native fieldset + radio inputs

commits.subject: fix(audit): pane z-index symmetry, font-sans override, Date filter perf
commits.body:
#1 — SettingsPane backdrop z-index asymmetry + misleading comment
  DetailPane backdrop was z-30, SettingsPane backdrop was z-40, both
  panes were z-40. The settings backdrop being at the SAME level as
  the detail aside (z-40) only worked via DOM source order — the
  comment falsely claimed "Higher z-index so it stacks above detail."
  Future readers would see the equal z-index and either "fix" it
  (breaking the stacking) or be confused by the lying comment.

  Unified both panes to use the same pair: z-30 backdrop + z-40 aside.
  Stacking still works via DOM source order (settings markup comes
  after detail, so at equal z-index the later-painted wins). Comment
  rewrote to describe the actual mechanism instead of the wrong
  z-index claim.

#2 — `font-sans` Tailwind utility overrode global Figtree font
  App.jsx:348 and EmbedRenderer.jsx:160 had `font-sans` on their
  embed/error fallback divs. Tailwind's font-sans = `font-family:
  ui-sans-serif, system-ui, ...` (system stack), which has higher
  CSS specificity than the global `* { font-family: var(--font-sans) }`
  rule that sets Figtree. Result: error messages rendered in the OS
  system font instead of the project's Figtree, visually inconsistent
  with the rest of the dashboard.

  Dropped `font-sans` from both fallbacks. They now inherit Figtree
  from the global `*` rule like every other element.

#4 — Date construction inside filterCommits filter callback
  The dateTo branch did:
    if (filters.dateTo) {
      const toDate = new Date(filters.dateTo);
      toDate.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999);
      const commitDate = new Date(commit.timestamp);
      if (commitDate > toDate) return false;
    }
  Two `new Date()` constructions per commit (toDate + commitDate),
  plus a setHours mutation, plus a Date comparison. For a 10k-commit
  dataset that's 20k+ Date allocations per filter operation.

  Hoisted both date bounds out of the .filter() callback into the
  function body, converted to `getTime()` numeric millisecond
  comparison. Per-commit overhead drops from 2 Date constructions +
  Date comparison to 1 Date construction + numeric comparison.
  Total filter overhead: O(2N) → O(N+2) Date allocations.

  Also removed the local Date mutation — toDate.setHours() is now
  called once at the top, on the single hoisted instance, not a
  side-effect inside what should be a pure filter callback.

Verified: vite build clean, 60/60 tests pass.

Tags: fix, perf, accessibility, ui
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-audit
Semver: patch
commits.title: pane z-index symmetry, font-sans override, Date filter perf

The 2026-04-13 commit `af0f02d` added Playwright + e2e dir + visual
regression spec + 5 npm scripts + ~170MB browser binary install
recipe, but never produced any baseline screenshots. The session that
committed it had no Chromium binary in its sandbox — the spec files
sat unrun across every subsequent session. Several later audit reports
incorrectly warned "Playwright baselines will drift" when there were
no baselines to drift from.

Removed in this commit:

  - dashboard/e2e/README.md
  - dashboard/e2e/daisyui-surfaces.spec.js (~390 lines, 14 functional tests)
  - dashboard/e2e/visual/theme-baselines.spec.js (~130 lines, 48 visual tests)
  - dashboard/e2e/ directory
  - @playwright/test ^1.59.1 devDependency
  - 5 npm scripts: test:e2e, test:e2e:install, test:e2e:ui, test:visual,
    test:visual:update

    playwright-report, playwright/.cache, *-actual.png, *-diff.png)

    note from docs/SESSION_NOTES.md

@playwright/test, playwright, playwright-core all gone from
node_modules.

docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md "Automated coverage" section rewrote to describe
ONE layer (source-level node:test tripwire) instead of three. The
manual checklists below it are unchanged.

docs/TODO.md gained a new "Browser test coverage (future)" backlog
entry with notes on what to do differently when re-introducing
browser tests:
  - Verify the runner can actually execute in the target environment
    BEFORE writing specs (the previous attempt set up everything
    without ever running it once)
  - Decide scope first — visual regression has high maintenance cost,
    functional smoke tests are higher value-per-byte; start with smoke
    only

    replaces them — anything that can be checked at the source level
    should stay there
  - Reference for the previous attempt: commit af0f02d in git history.
    Resurrect as a starting point if useful, but treat as a draft —
    most assertions reference DaisyUI v5 phase markers that have
    shifted with subsequent refactors

with full rationale.

commits.subject: refactor(layout): #15 simplify drawer architecture — 1 drawer + 2 fixed panes
commits.body:
The Phase 4a vanilla-DaisyUI sweep nested THREE DaisyUI drawers in App.jsx (filter → detail → settings) to migrate all three slide-over panes to the same component. Three concerns in the post-sweep audit: 1. DaisyUI v5 drawer is designed for one-drawer-per-page UX. Nesting three of them was an undocumented pattern. 2. CSS-grid-based drawer composition with three layers had unverified z-index / click-outside / focus-trap interactions across the three drawer-overlay labels. 3. Without a browser to test, I had no way to confirm the nested architecture actually rendered correctly. Reverted to a simpler, more proven architecture: - **Filter sidebar**: ONE DaisyUI `drawer lg:drawer-open` wrapper. This is the documented standard pattern — inline at lg+, overlay below lg. Well-supported, no nesting. - **Detail pane**: stock Tailwind `
` with a sibling `
` backdrop. Slide animation via Tailwind's transform + transition. - **Settings pane**: same pattern as detail, wider z-index (z-40) so it stacks above detail when both are open. Both pane backdrops use onClick → dispatch CLOSE_* for click-to-close. React reducer state still owns the open/closed flag for both panes. DetailPane and SettingsPane components themselves are unchanged from their content-only state — they just render inside `
` instead of inside a DaisyUI drawer-side now. App.jsx structural changes: - Replaced 3 nested
...
blocks with 1 outer drawer (filter) + 2 fixed-positioned aside elements - Wrapped the main tab content area in
for proper landmark semantics (was a plain div) - Removed two nested ErrorBoundary scopes — only the one wrapping the tab router remains, plus per-pane ErrorBoundaries on Header, TabBar, FilterSidebar, DetailPane, SettingsPane AppContext changes: - Removed unused 'OPEN_SETTINGS_PANE' reducer case (only added in Phase 4a for the drawer onChange that no longer exists). The TOGGLE_SETTINGS_PANE / CLOSE_SETTINGS_PANE actions stay — they're used by the settings button + close button + escape key handler. 'OPEN_FILTER_SIDEBAR' stays — still used by the filter drawer's onChange. 'OPEN_DETAIL_PANE' stays — used by the openDetailPane callback that programmatically opens the pane with payload. Stock Tailwind utilities only — no custom classes, no custom CSS, no @utility, no arbitrary brackets. The pane animations use: fixed top-0 right-0 h-screen w-full max-w-{lg|sm} transform transition-transform duration-300 translate-x-0 / translate-x-full bg-black/60 z-30/z-40 opacity-100 / opacity-0 pointer-events-none print:hidden All vanilla-policy compliant. Z-index hierarchy: - Detail backdrop: z-30 - Detail pane: z-40 (above its own backdrop, below settings) - Settings backdrop: z-40 (above detail pane — covers detail when both open) - Settings pane: z-40 (above settings backdrop via DOM order) Verified: - vite build clean (2.77s) - 60/60 tests pass - Built CSS contains .translate-x-0, .translate-x-full, .bg-black/60, .transition-transform, .transition-opacity, .pointer-events-none Remaining untested (cannot verify without a browser): - Mobile slide direction (fixed right-0 + translate-x-full slides in from the right on all viewports — was the same on desktop in the old architecture, was a bottom-sheet on mobile in the pre-vanilla architecture. Bottom-sheet is gone) - Focus trap behaviour across the new aside elements (DetailPane and SettingsPane still call useFocusTrap internally — the trapRef is on their content div which is the focusable container) Tags: refactor, layout, ui, daisyui, drawer Complexity: 4 Urgency: 2 Impact: user-facing Risk: medium Debt: paid Epic: daisyui-v5-migration Semver: patch
commits.title: #15 simplify drawer architecture — 1 drawer + 2 fixed panes

commits.subject: chore(audit): hygiene pass + document element-selector exceptions
commits.body:
#10 — SettingsPane.jsx broken indentation
  Lines 105-216 had inconsistent indentation after the drawer
  migration: body div opened at indent 16 but children sat at indent
  20-28 (off by 4). The Work Hours hint div sat at indent 20 (sibling
  of mb-6 visually) when it should have been a child at indent 24.
  React parsed it fine, but the indentation misled readers about the
  tree structure. Re-indented the entire body block consistently.
  Pure cosmetic, no behavior change.

#11 — Dead --z-drawer / --z-drawer-backdrop CSS vars
  The :root z-index scale had `--z-drawer-backdrop: 28` and
  `--z-drawer: 30` left over from the pre-DaisyUI-drawer
  `.filter-sidebar` / `.detail-pane` / `.settings-pane` custom CSS.
  Those classes are gone; nothing references the vars anymore.
  Deleted both lines + updated the comment block.

#14 — Stale 30-line heatmap migration comment in styles.css
  The lines 276-308 comment described the `.heatmap-grid` /
  `.heatmap-header` / `.heatmap-label` / `.heatmap-cell` / `.heatmap-cell-sm`
  / `.heatmap-tooltip-inner` migration with arbitrary-bracket values
  (`text-[9px] sm:text-[11px]`, `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]`,
  `shadow-[0_2px_8px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3)]`). All those arbitrary values
  were also deleted in Phase 3 — the comment described a transitional
  state that no longer exists. Replaced with a 7-line summary
  pointing to Timing.jsx for the current implementation.

#8 + #9 — Element-selector exceptions documented in code + CLAUDE.md
  styles.css has 4 element-selector / pseudo-element rules that
  technically violate "vanilla only, no custom CSS":
    - `* { font-family: var(--font-sans) }` global font reset
    - `body { background-color, color }` DaisyUI fallbacks
    - `h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-mono) }` mono headings
    - `body::before` decorative grid background

  These were deemed unavoidable:
    - Pseudo-elements cannot be Tailwind utilities
    - The font-family reset / heading styling is app-wide design
      intent applied via semantic HTML rather than per-consumer
      utility classes (~15-20 JSX edits to migrate)
    - The grid background is the dashboard's visual signature

  Added a 30-line block comment in styles.css explaining the four
  exceptions with rationale and rejected alternatives. Also added a
  reference in CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts" so future
  sessions know these element-selector rules are sanctioned.

Verified: vite build clean (2.82s), 60/60 tests pass.

commits.subject: fix(audit): heatmap grid alignment, embed per-chart isolation, hooks order
commits.body:
Fresh-eyes audit found 4 coupled issues in the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep. #1 — Timing.jsx full heatmap rows broken at most viewport widths Phase 3 replaced `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]` with a flex row containing a fixed-width label column (`w-10 sm:w-12`) and a separate `grid-cols-7 flex-1` day grid. Label cells were aspect- square at 40/48px while day cells were aspect-square at (flex-1 / 7) = ~35px mobile / ~91px desktop. Rows didn't line up — the "09:00" label rendered next to a different hour's data on most screens. Restored a single CSS grid with `grid-cols-[auto_repeat(7,1fr)]` so the label column and 7 day columns share the same rows. Labels align perfectly at every viewport width. CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts" updated to document this as the ONE permitted arbitrary bracket value exception (grid templates with mixed auto + repeat() aren't expressible as stock Tailwind utilities, and row alignment is a functional data-correctness requirement — not cosmetic). #2 — EmbedRenderer can't isolate individual charts within sections Phase 4b+4g made CollapsibleSection return `<>{children}` (Fragment, no DOM wrapper) in embed mode. Then EmbedRenderer's DOM traversal walked up from `[data-embed-id]` to "the top-level child of the container". For multi-chart sections like Timeline (5 embeddable charts in one section), all charts shared the same top-level parent (Timeline's `
`). `?embed=activity-timeline` showed all 5 Timeline charts stacked. Fix: - CollapsibleSection.jsx: return `
{children}
` in embed mode instead of a Fragment. Each CollapsibleSection now has its own DOM ancestor. - EmbedRenderer.jsx: useLayoutEffect hides all `[data-embed-wrapper]` elements and shows only those whose subtree contains a matching `[data-embed-id]`. Per-chart isolation restored. #3 — CollapsibleSection calls useState after an early return The `if (isEmbedMode) return ...` short-circuit was BEFORE `useState(defaultExpanded)`. React's Rules of Hooks requires hooks at the top of the function body, before any early returns. Since `isEmbedMode` is a module-level constant, this didn't crash at runtime — but ESLint's rules-of-hooks flags it, and any future refactor that made `isEmbed` dynamic would silently crash with "Rendered fewer hooks than expected." Moved useState above the embed-mode early return. Dropped the unused `sectionId` variable (was only used to build a checkbox `id` that no `
commits.title: heatmap grid alignment, embed per-chart isolation, hooks order

commits.subject: fix(charts): include themeAccent/themeMuted in chart useMemo deps
commits.body:
Double-check audit of the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep found that Progress,
Contributors, and Tags chart useMemo blocks only listed state.darkMode
in their deps. Since chart data builders call getSeriesColor() (which
reads CSS variables at call time), the useMemo didn't re-run on
inter-theme switches where darkMode stays constant — e.g. lofi → nord
(both light) or black → dim (both dark). Chart datasets would keep
resolving the OLD theme's --color-primary / --color-secondary / etc.
until darkMode flipped or a new filter triggered re-render.

state.themeAccent and state.themeMuted DO update on every theme change
via the SET_THEME_COLORS dispatch from AppContext's darkMode+activeTheme
useEffect, so adding them to the chart useMemo deps closes the gap.

Files updated:
  - Progress.jsx: 3 useMemo deps arrays (featFix, complexity, semver)
  - Contributors.jsx: 1 useMemo deps array (complexityChartData)
  - Tags.jsx: 2 useMemo deps arrays — also normalised the rationale
    comment to reference themeAccent/themeMuted instead of the earlier
    lightTheme/darkTheme fields I'd added as a first-pass fix (both
    work, but themeAccent is more semantically correct since it's
    what the charts actually consume)

Timeline.jsx and Timing.jsx already had correct deps from the Phase 2
commit — verified.

Verified: vite build clean, 60/60 tests pass. Next theme-switch on a
browser with, say, lofi → nord (or similar same-mode inter-theme) will
correctly rebuild every chart dataset with the new semantic tokens.

Also verified during this audit pass:
  - All DaisyUI drawer + drawer-end + drawer-toggle + drawer-content
    + drawer-side + drawer-overlay + lg:drawer-open classes ship
    correctly in built CSS (my earlier doubts about nested-drawer
    behaviour turned out to be misreading the CSS — DaisyUI v5 drawer
    IS a fixed-positioned slide-over with translate-based animation,
    which is exactly the behaviour we need)
  - All collapse + collapse-arrow + collapse-open + collapse-close +
    collapse-title + collapse-content classes ship
  - All 7 DaisyUI badge semantic variants (primary/secondary/accent/
    info/success/warning/error/neutral) + badge-sm + badge-xs ship
  - HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES array values (bg-base-300 + bg-primary/N)
    all generate utility rules via Tailwind content scanning
  - Zero orphaned imports of deleted exports (getTagColor,
    getTagStyleObject, TAG_COLORS, DYNAMIC_TAG_PALETTE, hashString,
    getDynamicTagColor, accentColor, mutedColor, seriesColors,
    hasUrlAccentOverride) anywhere in dashboard/js
  - Focus management hooks (useFocusTrap, useEscapeKey, useScrollLock)
    still wired correctly on DetailPane + SettingsPane after the
    drawer migration

Tags: fix, charts, daisyui, audit
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
commits.title: include themeAccent/themeMuted in chart useMemo deps

commits.subject: fix(audit): vanilla-DaisyUI sweep post-audit cleanups
commits.body:
Second-pass audit of the 8-phase vanilla sweep caught 6 loose ends.

Playwright e2e spec (daisyui-surfaces.spec.js Phase 9):
  The test asserted the now-deleted `.hamburger-dropdown` +
  `.dashboard-header` + `.hamburger-backdrop` custom classes. Updated
  selectors to match the vanilla state:
    - `nav[aria-label="Secondary actions"]` for the portal content
    - `[data-testid="hamburger-backdrop"]` for the backdrop
    - `<header>` tag check instead of `.dashboard-header` classList
  Added `data-testid="hamburger-backdrop"` to the HamburgerMenu
  backdrop div for test isolation (inline Tailwind doesn't give a
  stable selector otherwise).

Header.jsx filter toggle button:
  Added `lg:hidden` to the filter toggle button so it disappears at
  lg+ breakpoint. The filter drawer uses `lg:drawer-open` in App.jsx
  which forces the filter sidebar open on desktop — the toggle was
  a no-op at lg+ because CSS overrode the React state. Now the button
  only appears on mobile where it's actually useful.

AppContext.jsx themeAccent/themeMuted init seed:
  Previously `themeAccent: ''` / `themeMuted: ''` at reducer init
  with a comment claiming "chart sections gate their dataset builders
  on non-empty themeAccent". Grep confirmed no such guard exists —
  first render would have used empty strings as chart colours.
  Replaced with `resolveRuntimeAccent()` / `resolveRuntimeMuted()`
  direct calls at init time. Vite builds main.jsx with styles.css
  linked before the script, and index.html's flash-prevention
  script has already set `<html data-theme>` before React mounts,
  so getComputedStyle returns correct values at reducer init. If
  for some reason CSS isn't ready, the functions return '' and the
  useEffect dispatch backfills on mount — no worse than before.

AppContext.jsx stale brand-blue comment:
  Updated the `#2D68FF` reference to describe the current vanilla-
  DaisyUI runtime resolver instead.

docs/EMBED_IMPLEMENTATION.md:
  "How It Works" step list rewrote to describe the vanilla-DaisyUI
  flow (isEmbedMode short-circuit in CollapsibleSection, runtime
  getComputedStyle resolution, no brand palette). "Color Architecture"
  section rewrote to list the new `chartColors.js` API. Files table
  updated with current roles. Testing checklist removed 6 stale
  checkboxes for deleted `?palette`, `?colors`, `?accent`, `?muted`
  URL parameter tests.

docs/TODO.md:
  Removed the "Data-viz palette (out of scope by design)" note that
  documented the deleted 20-colour `seriesColors` palette as
  intentionally hardcoded. Replaced with a post-sweep verification
  checklist:
    - Re-capture Playwright visual regression baselines
    - Live browser verification of nested drawers, collapse, heatmap
  Updated the "Last updated" trailer to reflect the 2026-04-14 state.

Verified: vite build clean (3.52s), 60/60 tests pass.

Tags: fix, audit, docs, daisyui, cleanup
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch

commits.subject: docs: Phase 6 — vanilla-DaisyUI sweep documentation
commits.body:
Updates CLAUDE.md + DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md + SESSION_NOTES.md +
HISTORY.md to reflect the completed 8-phase vanilla-DaisyUI sweep.

CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts":
  - Replaced the 6-tier precedence rule with a vanilla-only policy:
    only DaisyUI component classes + DaisyUI semantic tokens + stock
    Tailwind v4 utilities. Zero custom CSS, zero @theme extensions,
    zero @utility directives, zero arbitrary bracket values, zero
    brand hex literals.
  - Listed the two remaining exceptions: DebugPill.jsx inline hex
    (isolated root that survives CSS failure) and themes.js:137
    fallback `#808080` (single safety-net).

DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md:
  - "Project conventions" rewritten as vanilla-only policy. Migration
    path for new patterns: stock utility → DaisyUI token → component
    class → runtime CSS var resolution → out of scope.
  - "Tag chips" section rewritten to document the 7-variant DaisyUI
    badge system (success/error/warning/secondary/info/accent/neutral)
    replacing the deleted 34-hex palette.
  - "Custom CSS policy: zero" section enumerates every deleted class
    and its migration target.
  - "Previously deliberately NOT used — now used" section flips
    `drawer` and `collapse` from the rejected list to adopted,
    with rationale for why the previous objections were overblown.
  - `dropdown` + `menu` stay in "deliberately NOT used" — HamburgerMenu
    still uses React-state disclosure for the same reasons.
  - Chart.js dataset colours section rewritten for runtime
    getComputedStyle resolution of 8 semantic vars.
  - Toast convention updated to note `z-[var(--z-toast)]` is the only
    permitted arbitrary bracket (design-token CSS var reference).
  - Drawer + Collapse conventions added with full JSX examples.

HISTORY.md:
  - Added top-level "Vanilla-DaisyUI sweep — 8 phases, zero custom CSS"
    entry with per-phase commit hashes, visual regressions, and URL
    params removed.

SESSION_NOTES.md:
  - Added current-state block at the top summarising the 8-phase sweep
    and final state (zero custom classes, full DaisyUI adoption).

Verified: vite build clean (2.66s), 60/60 tests pass, final audit
confirms:
  - 0 custom class primary rules in dashboard/styles.css
  - 0 arbitrary `text-[Npx]` usages in dashboard/js
  - 0 hex colour literals in chartColors.js or utils.js (tag palette)

Branch state after this commit: the full vanilla-DaisyUI sweep is
complete. The dashboard uses exclusively DaisyUI components, DaisyUI
semantic tokens, and stock Tailwind utilities.

Tags: docs, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
commits.title: Phase 6 — vanilla-DaisyUI sweep documentation

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 4d-j — strip remaining custom classes
commits.body:
Deletes every remaining custom CSS class from styles.css. The allowlist
is now empty — the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep is complete.

Deleted custom classes + migrations:

  .dashboard-header + ::after gradient — removed entirely. Header.jsx
    now uses `border-b border-base-300` for the divider. Decorative
    primary-tinted gradient line is gone.

  .hamburger-dropdown + @keyframes hamburger-fade-in — HamburgerMenu.jsx
    now uses inline Tailwind utilities for the dropdown surface:
    `fixed min-w-52 max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)] bg-base-200 border
    border-base-300 rounded-md shadow-xl z-50 py-1`. The 0.12s fade-in
    animation is gone; dropdown appears immediately.

  .hamburger-update-dot + @keyframes hamburger-pulse — Header.jsx now
    uses `w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary animate-pulse` (Tailwind's
    stock animate-pulse utility) for the PWA update indicator.

  .scrollbar-hide + ::-webkit-scrollbar — TabBar.jsx mobile tab row
    now shows the native scrollbar when overflowing. Minor aesthetic
    regression accepted per vanilla directive.

  .header-filter-hint — Header.jsx filtered-state button now uses
    DaisyUI `btn btn-link btn-sm p-0 min-h-0 h-auto text-primary`
    (native underlined link-style button).

  .root-error-message / .root-error-detail / .root-error-hint —
    main.jsx root error boundary fallback now inlines Tailwind
    utilities directly. If CSS load fails, the error message falls
    back to browser default styling. Acceptable given the vanilla-only
    directive — previously the root-error classes existed specifically
    to survive CSS failure.

  @media print block trimmed: removed references to deleted
    `.dashboard-header`, `.hamburger-menu`, `.header-filter-hint`.
    Only element selectors (body, a, section) and DaisyUI overrides
    remain. Print-hiding of chrome is handled via Tailwind's `print:hidden`
    variant on JSX consumers instead.

daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs:
  - LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist: set to empty Set — every
    custom class has been deleted. The allowlist guard now enforces
    a zero-custom-class policy.
  - No allowlist entries remain to verify against.

Visual regressions (all accepted per vanilla directive):
  - Header no longer has decorative gradient accent line below it
  - Hamburger dropdown appears without fade-in animation
  - Mobile tab bar shows native scrollbar when overflowing
  - Root error fallback has simpler typography

Verified: vite build clean (2.75s), 60/60 tests pass, allowlist is empty
and the guard test passes trivially.

Final custom-CSS count: 0.
Total custom classes deleted in the full vanilla sweep: 24 → 0.

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 4a — drawers to DaisyUI drawer component
commits.body:
Replaces the three custom slide-over panes (filter sidebar, detail
pane, settings pane) with DaisyUI's native `drawer` component.
Positioning, slide animation, overlay rendering, and click-outside-
to-close are all handled by DaisyUI CSS + native `<input type="checkbox">`
state synced to React via controlled onChange.

App.jsx rewritten to nest three drawer wrappers:
  - Outer: `drawer lg:drawer-open` for FilterSidebar (left side,
    inline on lg+, overlay below lg)
  - Middle: `drawer drawer-end` for DetailPane (right side slide-over)
  - Inner: `drawer drawer-end` for SettingsPane (right side slide-over)
  - Innermost content wraps Header + TabBar + the main tab router

Each drawer input is controlled:
  checked={state.xxxOpen}
  onChange={e => dispatch({ type: e.target.checked ? 'OPEN_XXX' : 'CLOSE_XXX' })}

The `drawer-overlay` label[htmlFor] handles click-outside-to-close
without any JS — DaisyUI's drawer-side component generates the
backdrop as a sibling label that unchecks the checkbox when clicked.

FilterSidebar.jsx simplified:
  - Dropped the wrapper div + mobile overlay sibling
  - Now renders only the inner content as an `<aside className="menu
    bg-base-200 min-h-full w-80 p-4 space-y-4">` which lives inside
    the drawer-side in App.jsx

DetailPane.jsx simplified:
  - Dropped `.detail-pane-overlay` sibling and `.detail-pane` wrapper
  - Now renders as `flex flex-col h-full w-full max-w-lg bg-base-200
    border-l border-base-300`
  - DaisyUI drawer-end provides the transform/slide animation
  - useFocusTrap + useEscapeKey hooks still run for keyboard UX

SettingsPane.jsx simplified:
  - Same pattern as DetailPane — bare content inside drawer-side
  - Max width w-sm for the narrower settings panel

AppContext new actions:
  - OPEN_FILTER_SIDEBAR: sets filterSidebarOpen = true
  - OPEN_SETTINGS_PANE: sets settingsPaneOpen = true
  These complement the existing TOGGLE_/CLOSE_ actions so the drawer
  onChange can explicitly open (e.target.checked) without flipping
  current state.

styles.css deletions (10+ CSS rules):
  - .detail-pane + .detail-pane.open + mobile bottom-sheet @media
  - .detail-pane-overlay + .detail-pane-overlay.open
  - .detail-pane-header::before (mobile drag handle pseudo)
  - .settings-pane + .settings-pane.open + mobile bottom-sheet @media
  - .settings-pane-overlay + .settings-pane-overlay.open
  - .settings-pane-header::before (mobile drag handle pseudo)
  - .filter-sidebar + .filter-sidebar.open + .filter-sidebar.collapsed
  - .filter-sidebar-overlay + .filter-sidebar-overlay.open
  - @media (max-width: 768px) .filter-sidebar transforms block
  - .dashboard-enter + @keyframes dashboard-enter (page-load fade-in
    animation — vanilla sweep removes cosmetic entrance animations)

Tests (daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs):
  - LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist: removed filter-sidebar,
    filter-sidebar-overlay, detail-pane, detail-pane-overlay,
    detail-pane-header, settings-pane, settings-pane-overlay,
    settings-pane-header, dashboard-enter (9 classes)

Visual regression:
  - Mobile bottom-sheet variant for detail/settings panes is gone —
    on mobile both panes now slide in from the right (DaisyUI drawer
    doesn't ship a bottom-sheet variant). Functionally equivalent,
    visually different direction.
  - Mobile drag-handle pills (::before pseudos on pane headers) are
    gone. DaisyUI doesn't include one.
  - Page-load fade-in animation removed.
  - DaisyUI drawer uses its own transform transition timing which
    may feel slightly different from the custom 0.3s ease values.

Allowlist status: custom CSS classes dropped from 18 → 9.

Verified: vite build clean (2.74s), 60/60 tests pass.

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla, drawer
Complexity: 5
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
commits.title: Phase 4a — drawers to DaisyUI drawer component

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 4b+4g — CollapsibleSection to DaisyUI collapse, embed-mode to conditional render
commits.body:
Tightly coupled phases — CollapsibleSection and embed-mode share state
assumptions so they migrate together. Replaces the hand-rolled card +
max-height:none transition with DaisyUI's native `collapse` component,
and strips the `.embed-mode` root class + its 7 descendant selectors
in favour of a conditional short-circuit in CollapsibleSection.

CollapsibleSection.jsx rewritten:
  - Imports isEmbedMode from urlParams
  - Embed mode short-circuit: `if (isEmbedMode) return <>{children}</>`
    — no card wrapper, no header, no collapse mechanism, just the
    children so embedders get the bare chart
  - Non-embed: DaisyUI `collapse collapse-arrow` with native checkbox
    wired to React state via onChange. `collapse-open` / `collapse-close`
    modifier classes force the React-controlled state to survive
    DaisyUI's default click-to-toggle behaviour
  - No custom className, no custom CSS — the component renders
    entirely through DaisyUI + stock Tailwind utilities

EmbedRenderer.jsx updated:
  - Dropped `className="embed-mode"` wrapper, replaced with
    `p-4 bg-base-100` stock Tailwind
  - DOM traversal no longer walks to `.card` ancestors (since
    CollapsibleSection doesn't render cards in embed mode). New
    implementation walks from `[data-embed-id]` to the top-level
    child of the container and toggles its display — simpler and
    matches the new embed-mode JSX tree
  - Removed `card.classList.add('embed-target')` — the matching
    embed-target class rule was deleted from styles.css

styles.css rule deletions:
  - .collapsible-content + .collapsible-content.expanded
    (the custom max-height:0 → max-height:none transition)
  - .embed-mode root rule (padding + bg-base-100)
  - .embed-mode .collapsible-header rule
  - .embed-mode .card.embed-target rule
  - .embed-mode .card.embed-target:hover rule
  - .embed-mode .collapsible-content .pt-2 rule
  - .embed-mode .collapsible-content rule
  - body:has(.embed-mode)::before rule (hid decorative grid)
  - .embed-mode ~ #debug-error-banner / body:has(.embed-mode) #debug-error-banner rules
  All replaced with rationale comment block explaining where each
  behaviour moved.

Tests (daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs):
  - LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist: removed `collapsible-content`
    and `embed-mode`
  - "Phase 5 — CollapsibleSection uses DaisyUI card + card-body"
    renamed to "Phase 5 — CollapsibleSection uses DaisyUI collapse
    component" with updated assertions matching the new structure
    (collapse/collapse-arrow/collapse-title/collapse-content) plus
    isEmbedMode short-circuit check

Visual regression:
  - DaisyUI's collapse component has its own default animation (less
    custom than our previous max-height:none transition). Open/close
    feel slightly different but functionally identical.
  - Embed iframes no longer get the `.embed-mode` padding/bg —
    replaced with inline `p-4 bg-base-100` on the EmbedRenderer root
    div, which is visually equivalent.
  - The decorative page grid `body::before` rule that hid for embeds
    is gone — embeds never had the grid in the first place because
    they don't render body chrome, so this was a dead rule.

Verified: vite build clean (2.81s), 60/60 tests pass.

Allowlist status: custom CSS classes dropped from 24 → 18
(removed: collapsible-content, embed-mode, heatmap-0..4 from Phase
4c earlier).

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla, embed
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
commits.title: Phase 4b+4g — CollapsibleSection to DaisyUI collapse, embed-mode to conditional render

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 2 — chart colours to vanilla DaisyUI semantic cycle
commits.body:
Rewrites chartColors.js to use only DaisyUI semantic CSS variables
resolved at runtime. Deletes every static brand palette, every URL
override, and every hardcoded hex colour.

chartColors.js rewritten end-to-end:
  Deleted:
    DEFAULT_SERIES (20-hex default palette)
    DEFAULT_ACCENT (#2D68FF brand blue)
    DEFAULT_ACCENT_MUTED (#94a3b8)
    PALETTES.default / .warm / .cool / .earth / .vibrant / .mono
      (5 alternate 20-hex palettes)
    DISCONTINUED/INTERNAL repo hex constants (#4b5563, #d1d5db)
    parseColorOverrides() URL parser
    isValidHex / toHex helpers
    seriesColors / accentColor / mutedColor exports (static bootstrap)
    hasUrlAccentOverride / hasUrlMutedOverride export flags
    palettes export (for settings UI that doesn't exist yet)

  Added:
    SEMANTIC_CYCLE constant — 8 DaisyUI CSS variable names in cycle order
      (primary → secondary → accent → info → success → warning → error
      → neutral)
    readCssVar(name) — getComputedStyle helper
    getSemanticPalette() — returns all 8 resolved colours as an array
    SEMANTIC_CYCLE_VARS export — the list of variable names
    withOpacity(color, opacity) — now returns color-mix(in oklab, color N%,
      transparent) instead of rgba() so it works on oklch / color-mix
      inputs as well as hex

  Kept:
    resolveRuntimeAccent() — now always reads --color-primary
    resolveRuntimeMuted() — color-mix of --color-base-content at 40%
    getSeriesColor(index) — cycles through SEMANTIC_CYCLE
    getRepoColor / buildRepoColorMap — repo category helpers now resolve
      --color-neutral (discontinued) and --color-base-content (internal)
      via runtime CSS vars

URL parameters removed:
  - ?palette=name (no more named presets)
  - ?accent=hex   (no more brand override)
  - ?muted=hex    (no more muted override)
  - ?colors=h1,h2,h3 (no more per-series override)
  embedders now get whatever DaisyUI theme they configured via ?theme=.

Consumers updated:
  - main.jsx: dropped the --chart-accent-override CSS var bridge that
    ran on module load when an embedder supplied ?accent=. The heatmap
    migration in Phase 4c already dropped the CSS var read that used
    it, so nothing consumes --chart-accent-override anymore.
  - AppContext.jsx: dropped the import of { accentColor, mutedColor }.
    state.themeAccent / state.themeMuted now seed as empty strings and
    the darkMode useEffect dispatches SET_THEME_COLORS on mount with
    runtime-resolved values. Chart sections already gate their dataset
    builders on state.themeAccent via useMemo deps so this is safe.
  - Timeline.jsx: dropped unused seriesColors import and rewrote the
    rationale comment block to describe the vanilla-DaisyUI approach
    (delete references to the deleted "static 20-color palette").
  - urlParams.js: deleted references to ?palette / ?accent / ?muted /
    ?colors in the header doc block. searchParams export stays because
    the embed/data/theme/bg params still use it.

Tests (daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs):
  - "chartColors.js exposes runtime theme resolvers" replaced with
    "chartColors.js is vanilla DaisyUI (no brand hex, no URL overrides)"
    which regression-guards the deletion of DEFAULT_SERIES / PALETTES /
    hasUrlAccentOverride / any hex literal in the file.

Visual regression:
  - Charts with >8 categories now repeat colours across the 8-slot
    semantic cycle. Previously had 20 brand hexes for more distinct
    multi-category rendering. Acceptable per user directive.
  - Embedders who supplied ?accent=hex lose that customisation — they
    now see the active DaisyUI theme's primary. They can still pick a
    theme via ?theme= (light vs dark; full theme name via applyTheme).
  - The "low complexity" muted chart tone switched from hardcoded
    slate-400 to color-mix(base-content 40%, transparent) which tracks
    the theme's text tone.

Verified: vite build clean (3.23s), 60/60 tests pass, chartColors.js
has zero hex literals, zero URL override references.

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla, charts
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
commits.title: Phase 2 — chart colours to vanilla DaisyUI semantic cycle

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 1 — tags to vanilla DaisyUI badge variants
commits.body:
Replaces the 34-tag brand-hex palette with 7 DaisyUI semantic badge
variants that track the active theme. The daisy theme IS the brand.

utils.js:
  Deleted:
    TAG_COLORS (34 brand hex mappings)
    TAG_TEXT_OVERRIDES (8 text-color lifted variants)
    DYNAMIC_TAG_PALETTE (10-colour fallback for unknown tags)
    hashString / getDynamicTagColor (hash-based colour picker)
    getTagColor (brand-hex accessor)
    getTagStyleObject (rgba() bg/border/color builder + Map cache)

  Added:
    TAG_SEMANTIC (34 tag names → DaisyUI badge variants, grouped into
      7 semantic categories: success, error, warning, secondary, info,
      accent, neutral)
    getTagBadgeClass(tag) — returns 'badge-{variant}' for JSX chip render
    TAG_SEMANTIC_VAR (tag → --color-<semantic> CSS variable name)
    resolveTagSemanticColor(tag) — reads the active theme's CSS var at
      runtime, returns the resolved value for Chart.js datasets (which
      can't consume CSS classes directly)

JSX consumers updated (5 files):
  - DetailPane.jsx: <span className="badge badge-sm ${getTagBadgeClass(tag)}">
  - Timeline.jsx: 3 tag-chip sites + "+N more" overflow chip
  - Health.jsx: 2 security badge sites
  - Contributors.jsx: tag chip + progress-bar colour (resolveTagSemanticColor)
  - Tags.jsx: list chips, Chart.js doughnut dataset.backgroundColor

Tags.jsx: Added state.darkMode / state.lightTheme / state.darkTheme to
the tagData useMemo deps so Chart.js doughnut colours re-resolve when
the user switches theme.

Tests (daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs):
  - Replaced "Tag color duplication stays collapsed" + "getTagClass is
    deleted" with a single "Tag brand-hex palette stays deleted" test
    that enforces the removal of TAG_COLORS/DYNAMIC_TAG_PALETTE/
    getTagColor/getTagStyleObject and the presence of the new
    getTagBadgeClass/resolveTagSemanticColor exports
  - Replaced ".tag base class stays deleted" regression guard with a
    "Tag chips use vanilla DaisyUI badge component" check that requires
    every tag consumer to use `badge badge-sm` + getTagBadgeClass()
    and rejects the deleted inline Tailwind combo

Visual regression:
  - 34 distinct tag colours → 7 semantic variants. Tags within a
    category (feature/enhancement/seed/init → success) look identical.
    Users lose fine-grained tag discrimination but gain a palette that
    tracks every DaisyUI theme. This is the intended tradeoff.

Verified: vite build clean (3.22s), 60/60 tests pass (count dropped
by 1 because the ".tag base class" test's second sweep was merged
into the new single assertion).

commits.body:
Deletes the 5 .heatmap-0..4 custom CSS classes from styles.css and
replaces them with a JS constant palette in sections/Timing.jsx that
uses stock Tailwind utilities tracking DaisyUI's --color-primary.

dashboard/js/sections/Timing.jsx:
  const HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES = [
    'bg-base-300',    // level 0 — no activity
    'bg-primary/20',  // level 1
    'bg-primary/40',  // level 2
    'bg-primary/60',  // level 3
    'bg-primary',     // level 4 — max activity
  ];

  className={`... ${HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES[level]}`}

Tailwind's content scanner picks up the 5 string literals from the
array and generates the utility rules without needing a safelist.

Removed from styles.css:
  .heatmap-0 { background-color: var(--color-base-300); }
  .heatmap-1..4 { color-mix(primary 15/35/60%) + color-primary-content }
  The --chart-accent-override URL embed hook (will be fully removed
  in Phase 2 alongside chartColors.js URL params)

Removed from daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs:
  - heatmap-0 through heatmap-4 from LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist
  - "Heatmap CSS uses color-mix" regression test replaced with a new
    assertion that Timing.jsx uses HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES and that
    styles.css does NOT re-introduce the deleted .heatmap-N rules

Visual difference:
  Previous gradient used color-mix 15/35/60/100% opacity over primary.
  New gradient uses Tailwind alpha 20/40/60/100% over primary.
  Very close visual match; slight saturation shift on levels 2-3.

Verified: vite build clean (3.11s), 61/61 tests pass, new test
enforces the migration.

Part of the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep. Removes every custom @theme token
and @utility directive from styles.css, restoring stock Tailwind
utilities at every JSX consumer.

Deleted from styles.css:

  text-13 → text-sm (14px)
  shadow-tooltip → shadow-lg
  shadow-dropdown → shadow-xl
  shadow-dropzone-glow → ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-2

  max-w-viewport-margin → DaisyUI modal-box default

Timing heatmap restructured:

  - Full heatmap: restructured from `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]`
    to flex with fixed label column + inner grid-cols-7 for day cells
  - All cells drop inline text labels — tooltip-only count display

Tests: 61/61 pass. daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs TabBar Phase 7 assertion
updated from /text-shadow-primary-glow/ to /font-semibold/ to match
the new vanilla active-tab style.

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): zero-arbitrary-values sweep via @theme + @utility
commits.body:
User asked for "theme tokens for everything always" — a full sweep of
every remaining arbitrary Tailwind bracket value into either @theme
tokens (standard namespaces) or @utility directives (non-standard) in
dashboard/styles.css, so the JSX stops shipping `text-[11px]`,
`shadow-[0_2px_8px...]`, `grid-cols-[36px_repeat...]`, `[text-shadow:...]`
and similar arbitraries.

@theme tokens (standard Tailwind v4 namespaces, auto-generate utilities):
  - --text-8/9/10/11/13 → text-8, text-9, text-10, text-11, text-13
    (8/9/10/11/13 px — sizes below/between Tailwind's 12/14/16 default)
  - --shadow-tooltip / --shadow-dropdown / --shadow-dropzone-glow
    → shadow-tooltip / shadow-dropdown / shadow-dropzone-glow
    (higher-opacity shadows than stock shadow-md/lg/xl which use ~10%
    and read too subtly against base-200/300 section backgrounds)

@utility directives (non-standard namespaces):
  - grid-heatmap-m / grid-heatmap-d — 36px/50px + repeat(7,1fr)
  - w-modal-responsive — min(420px, calc(100vw - 32px))
  - max-w-viewport-margin — calc(100vw - 32px)
  - min-h-hero — 60vh
  - text-shadow-primary-glow — 0 0 10px color-mix(primary 50%)
    (Tailwind v4 has no text-shadow utility out of the box)

JSX replacements across 11 files:
  - text-[Npx] → text-N (14 occurrences: Timing ×5, SettingsPane ×5,
    TabBar, HeatmapTooltip, Projects, HamburgerMenu, EmbedRenderer)
  - shadow-[0_2px_8px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3)] → shadow-tooltip (HeatmapTooltip)
  - shadow-[0_10px_25px_rgb(0_0_0/0.25)] → shadow-dropdown (FilterSidebar)
  - shadow-[0_0_20px_color-mix(...)] → shadow-dropzone-glow (DropZone)
  - [text-shadow:0_0_10px_color-mix(...)] → text-shadow-primary-glow (TabBar)
  - grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)] → grid-heatmap-m (Timing)
  - sm:grid-cols-[50px_repeat(7,1fr)] → sm:grid-heatmap-d (Timing)
  - w-[min(420px,calc(100vw-32px))] → w-modal-responsive (QuickGuide)
  - max-w-[calc(100vw-32px)] → max-w-viewport-margin (InstallInstructionsModal)
  - min-h-[60vh] → min-h-hero (DropZone)

Test update: the Phase 7 TabBar daisyui-surfaces assertion regex was
updated from /\[text-shadow:0_0_10px_color-mix/ to
/text-shadow-primary-glow/ to match the new utility name.

CLAUDE.md: added a new rule under "Frontend: Styles and Scripts"
listing the 6-tier precedence for replacing arbitrary values:
  1. Stock Tailwind utility
  2. DaisyUI semantic token
  3. Theme-scale CSS var inside an arbitrary
  4. @theme token in styles.css
  5. @utility directive in styles.css
  6. Arbitrary bracket value (last resort, document rationale)

DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md: added inventory of every defined @theme token and
@utility directive with rationale + the hard rule that arbitrary
bracket syntax is banned.

Verified:
  - vite build clean (2.92s)
  - npm test 61/61 pass
  - All new utilities confirmed in built CSS:
    .text-8, .text-9, .text-10, .text-11, .text-13,
    .shadow-tooltip, .shadow-dropdown, .shadow-dropzone-glow,
    .grid-heatmap-m, .sm\:grid-heatmap-d,
    .w-modal-responsive, .max-w-viewport-margin, .min-h-hero,
    .text-shadow-primary-glow
  - Grep of dashboard/js for
    text-\[[0-9]|shadow-\[[0-9_]|\[text-shadow|grid-cols-\[[0-9]|
    min-h-\[[0-9]|max-h-\[[0-9]|w-\[[0-9]|min-w-\[[0-9]|
    max-w-\[calc|w-\[min\(
    returns zero actual-code hits (one comment in CollapsibleSection
    explaining why max-h-[9999px] would clip long sections)
  - 24-class allowlist test still passes — @theme and @utility don't
    write .classname { primary rules in styles.css, so the regex
    scanner doesn't count them as new custom classes

Remaining legitimate non-stock usages (intentional, documented):
  - Dynamic style={{ width: `${pct}%` }} progress bars — computed
  - Dynamic style={{ height: chartHeight }} chart containers — computed
  - DebugPill.jsx inline styles — isolated React root (rationale)
  - main.jsx error boundary inline layout — pre-CSS-load (rationale)
  - Tags/Contributors style={{ backgroundColor: getTagColor }} —
    brand-fixed per-tag palette (rationale in TAG_COLORS)

commits.subject: refactor(dashboard): replace arbitrary Tailwind values with stock utilities
commits.body:
User asked for an audit of hardcoded / custom / inline code that could
be replaced with stock Tailwind utilities or DaisyUI tokens. Phase 1 =
safe drop-ins with no visual change.

Spacing arbitraries → Tailwind v4 stock scale:
  - App.jsx, EmbedRenderer.jsx: min-h-[200px] → min-h-50
  - FilterSidebar.jsx: max-h-[200px] → max-h-50, w-[280px] → w-70
  - Timing.jsx: min-w-[280px] → min-w-70, sm:min-w-[400px] → sm:min-w-100
  - InstallInstructionsModal.jsx: w-[420px] → w-105

  Tailwind v4's dynamic spacing scale (--spacing: .25rem) generates
  these utilities on demand from integer indices, so `w-70` = 70*4 =
  280px, `w-105` = 420px, etc. No arbitrary brackets needed.

Theme-scale z-index restoration:
  - Header.jsx: z-[21] → z-[var(--z-sticky-header)]

  The --z-sticky-header: 21 custom property already lives in styles.css
  :root alongside the other z-scale tokens. The round-3 migration
  hardcoded the literal 21 instead of keeping the CSS var reference,
  which silently decoupled the header from the design-token scale.
  Fixed.

Inset focus ring → Tailwind ring utilities:
  - HamburgerMenu.jsx:
      focus-visible:shadow-[inset_0_0_0_2px_var(--color-primary)]
    → focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-inset focus-visible:ring-primary

  Tailwind's ring utility with ring-inset modifier is exactly an inset
  2px box-shadow in the primary colour — no need for the arbitrary
  bracketed shadow syntax when a stock utility does the same job.

Toast z-index inline style → arbitrary class:
  - Toast.jsx:
      style={{ zIndex: 'var(--z-toast)' }}
    → className includes `z-[var(--z-toast)]`

  Both resolve the CSS var via the same DOM path at runtime; the class
  form removes the last inline style from the toast container while
  still pinning the z-index above DaisyUI's default.

Verified:
  - vite build clean (5.50s), 61/61 tests pass
  - All new utilities ship in built CSS:
      .min-h-50, .max-h-50, .w-70, .min-w-70, .sm\:min-w-100, .w-105,
      .focus-visible\:ring-2, .focus-visible\:ring-inset,
      .focus-visible\:ring-primary, .z-\[var\(--z-sticky-header\)\],
      .z-\[var\(--z-toast\)\]
  - Grep confirms no remaining min-h-[200px], w-[280px], max-h-[200px],
    min-w-[280px], min-w-[400px], w-[420px], z-[21], or the inset
    shadow arbitrary anywhere in dashboard/js

Out of scope (reported separately for user decision):
  - text-[Npx] arbitraries below 12px or between 12/14/16 — design-
    precise pixel values with no stock equivalent
  - shadow-[...] arbitraries with non-stock opacity or color-mix glow
  - grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)] — grid template string
  - w-[min(420px,calc(100vw-32px))], max-w-[calc(100vw-32px)] — calc/min
  - DebugPill.jsx inline styles — isolated React root (documented)
  - main.jsx error boundary inline layout — resilience (documented)
  - Dynamic style={{}} for progress % widths and chart heights

commits.subject: fix(audit): follow-up — toggle architecture, cascade grid, dead code, stale docs
commits.body:
Second pass of the post-migration audit after the user asked for a
no-shortcuts review of the first commit's deferred/skipped items.

SettingsPane UTC toggle — proper native architecture:
  The first fix kept `<div role="switch">` + `onClick` + `onKeyDown`
  + a `readOnly` + `aria-hidden` presentational checkbox. Two issues:
  `readOnly` is a no-op on HTML checkboxes (React only accepts it to
  silence the controlled-without-onChange warning), and the parent
  click handler raced the native checkbox toggle. Refactored to a
  native `<label>` wrapping `<input type="checkbox" class="toggle
  toggle-primary" onChange={...}>`. HTML's label-for association
  makes the whole row clickable, the input owns focus/keyboard/ARIA
  natively, and the handler now reads `e.target.checked` instead of
  `!state.useUTC` (no stale-closure risk).

TOGGLE_BASE_CLASSES no-op hover:
  Renamed to TOGGLE_ROW_CLASSES and replaced `hover:bg-base-300` with
  `hover:bg-base-content/5`. The original CSS had `--bg-tertiary` →
  `--bg-hover` (two different colours) but the migration mapped both
  to `bg-base-300`, silently breaking the hover affordance on the UTC
  row and the ViewLevel radio rows.

FilterSidebar MultiSelect highlighted+selected tint:
  The first fix only addressed the mouse-hover branch of the 2×2
  `isSelected × isHighlighted` state grid. Keyboard-highlighted
  selected rows still collapsed to `bg-base-300`, losing the primary
  tint. Enumerated all four combinations explicitly — highlighted
  +selected gets `bg-primary/30` (strongest tint so selection stays
  visible during arrow-key navigation).

Dead code removed:
  `FOCUS_RING_CLASSES` export in utils.js had zero consumers (grepped
  all of dashboard/js). Deleted the export + its ~20-line rationale
  block per CLAUDE.md's dead-code rule.

Stale docs updated:
  - styles.css 645-651 comment block described the UTC switch as
    "pure Tailwind with after: pseudo variant" — rewritten to
    reference DaisyUI `.toggle` + TOGGLE_ROW_CLASSES
  - DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md — added "Toggles (switches)" bullet with the
    <label> + <input type="checkbox" class="toggle toggle-primary">
    pattern + warnings against hand-rolled alternatives
  - AI_MISTAKES.md — 2026-04-14 entry covering all 5 regressions the
    audit caught with 5 prevention rules (state truth tables, cascade
    source order, attribute-selector grep, mouse+keyboard diff,
    prefer native DaisyUI form controls)
  - SESSION_NOTES.md + HISTORY.md — follow-up commit block

Deferred intentionally (documented in SESSION_NOTES):
  - Playwright visual regression baselines will drift; re-capture
    with --update-snapshots after visual check
  - SettingsPane ViewLevel radiogroup a11y (tabIndex=0 on every item,
    no arrow-key nav) — larger refactor outside audit scope

Verified: vite build clean (2.75s), 61/61 tests pass,
`hover:bg-base-content/5` + `bg-primary/30` + `toggle` +
`toggle-primary` all present in built CSS, no FOCUS_RING_CLASSES or
TOGGLE_BASE_CLASSES references remain in dashboard/js.

commits.subject: fix(audit): 8 post-migration issues — toggle, hover regressions, focus ring hygiene
commits.body:
Fresh-eyes audit of the round-3 custom-CSS sweep caught 2 CLAUDE.md
violations, 2 user-visible UX regressions, 1 a11y regression, and 3
hygiene items.

- SettingsPane UTC toggle reimplemented DaisyUI's native .toggle and
  hardcoded `after:bg-white` (invisible thumb violation). Replaced with
  `<input type="checkbox" className="toggle toggle-primary" readOnly
  aria-hidden="true" />` — parent row still owns role="switch".
- FilterSidebar MultiSelect selected-row hover inverted the cascade:
  `hover:bg-base-300` replaced the primary tint so hovering a selected
  row visually deselected it. Changed to `hover:bg-primary/20`.
- DetailPane commit row `hover:bg-white/5` was invisible on light themes
  (lofi/nord/emerald/caramellatte) and violated "never hardcode theme
  values". Changed to `hover:bg-base-content/5`.
- DropZone `focus-visible:outline-none` was an a11y regression —
  keyboard users lost the focus outline. Replaced with explicit
  `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-primary
  focus-visible:outline-offset-2` to match TabBar.
- FilterSidebar stale comment referenced deleted `.filter-multi-select-*`
  classes; stale `mb-0` override for deleted descendant selector
  removed.
- Redundant `focus-visible:outline` alongside `focus-visible:outline-2`
  (9 files) — in Tailwind v4, `.outline-2` already sets both style and
  width via `--tw-outline-style` (defaults to `solid`). Dropped the
  bare `outline` from utils.js FOCUS_RING_CLASSES, TabBar,
  CollapsibleSection, FilterSidebar, DropZone, Progress, Health,
  Timeline, HealthAnomalies, HealthBars.

Verified: vite build clean, 61/61 tests pass, DaisyUI `.toggle` +
`.toggle-primary` both ship, `hover:bg-base-content/5` +
`hover:bg-primary/20` + `focus-visible:outline-2` all present in built
CSS. No `focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2` pairings remain
in dashboard/js.

commits.subject: fix(cleanup): drop zone hover state + phantom class + dead heatmap markers
commits.body:
Post-round-3 audit turned up four real issues + confirmed the rest of
the sweep is sound. Fixing the real ones here.

=== Real UX regression — drop zone hover state ===

Round 3 migrated `.drop-zone` from its custom CSS rule to inline
Tailwind. The old CSS had a `:hover` state:

  .drop-zone:hover, .drop-zone:focus-visible {
      border-color: var(--color-primary);
      background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 5%, transparent);
  }

My migration kept `focus-visible:border-primary focus-visible:bg-primary/5`
but dropped the `:hover` equivalent. Result: mouse users no longer see
the border highlight as they pass the drop zone — they lose the
"this is clickable" discovery hint that was there before.

Fix: add `hover:border-primary hover:bg-primary/5` to `dropZoneBase` in
DropZone.jsx. Inline `hover:*` Tailwind variants map directly to the
old `:hover` CSS rule.

=== Minor — phantom `[text-shadow:...]` class in built CSS ===

Tailwind v4's content scanner picked up the literal string
`[text-shadow:...]` from doc comments in TabBar.jsx and styles.css
(where I explained WHY I used arbitrary-property syntax). Tailwind
thought that was a class reference and generated a dead rule:

  .\[text-shadow\:\.\.\.\]{text-shadow:...}

The rule is syntactically valid but does nothing (the value is three
literal dots). A few bytes of dead CSS in the bundle.

Fix: rewrite the doc comments to break the pattern — use `[prop:value]`
placeholder syntax in the TabBar.jsx comment and
"arbitrary-property syntax" in the styles.css rationale block. Neither
string matches Tailwind's class-name regex, so the scanner ignores them.

Verified in the built CSS:
  - phantom rule `.\[text-shadow\:\.\.\.\]{text-shadow:...}` → 0 matches
  - real rules `.\[text-shadow\:0_0_10px_color-mix(...)\]{...}` → 4 matches
    (base + theme variants + focus/hover compositions from DaisyUI)

=== Dead marker classes — `.weekly-heatmap` + `.daily-heatmap` ===

Cross-reference sweep of every JSX className token vs every styles.css
rule head caught two more dead marker classes I'd missed earlier:

  sections/Timing.jsx:359  <div className="weekly-heatmap">
  sections/Timing.jsx:385  <div className="daily-heatmap">

Neither has a CSS rule anywhere (verified via grep). They're the same
"zero-style wrapper marker" pattern as `stat-card` / `metric-selector`
/ `pin-btn` that earlier audits caught. Removed the className attributes
entirely — the wrappers are now plain `<div>` elements.

Strengthened `daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs` dead-marker sweep to include
`weekly-heatmap` and `daily-heatmap` so future regressions fail the
source-level tripwire immediately.

=== Verified sound ===

The audit also verified the round-3 sweep is otherwise correct:

  ✓ All 24 allowlisted custom classes still have their CSS rules
  ✓ Every `detail-pane-header` / `settings-pane-header` / `collapsible-header`
    marker class is still applied in JSX (for mobile drag-handle
    pseudo-elements + embed-mode display:none hook)
  ✓ `after:content-['']` + `after:absolute` + `after:top-0.5` +
    `after:left-0.5` + `after:w-5` + `after:h-5` + `after:translate-x-5`
    + `after:rounded-full` + `after:bg-white` + `after:transition-transform`
    all ship in built CSS (settings toggle switch thumb)
  ✓ `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]` + `grid-cols-[50px_repeat(7,1fr)]`
    arbitrary values both ship (heatmap grid)
  ✓ `shadow-[inset_0_0_0_2px_var(--color-primary)]` ships (hamburger
    focus-visible ring)
  ✓ `shadow-[0_0_20px_color-mix(in_oklab,var(--color-primary)_25%,transparent)]`
    ships (drop-zone drag-over glow)
  ✓ `shadow-[0_2px_8px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3)]` ships (heatmap tooltip)
  ✓ `max-md:w-full` + `max-md:h-full` + `max-md:rounded-none` + `max-md:p-4`
    + `max-md:px-4` + `max-md:py-3` + `max-md:pt-6` all ship (mobile
    drawer overrides)
  ✓ `focus-visible:outline-primary` ships (focus ring utility for
    non-DaisyUI role="button" tiles)
  ✓ `rotate-180` ships (collapsible chevron state-based rotation)
  ✓ `.dashboard-header:after` pseudo still renders the gradient
    accent line
  ✓ `.detail-pane-header:before` + `.settings-pane-header:before`
    still render the mobile drag handles
  ✓ `.embed-mode .collapsible-header { display: none }` still targets
    the marker class
  ✓ `.embed-mode .card.embed-target` still scopes to DaisyUI card
    overrides inside embed iframes
  ✓ No orphan CSS rules (every allowlisted class has at least one
    consumer, either as a React className attribute or via dynamic
    template literal `heatmap-${level}`)
  ✓ 61/61 test pass including the strict allowlist guard
