commits.subject: feat(modal): Phase 1 — migrate QuickGuide + InstallInstructionsModal to DaisyUI modal
commits.body:
First of 10 phases from the DaisyUI component-class audit. This phase
migrates the two self-contained modal components to DaisyUI's `modal`
component classes, eliminating ~35 custom CSS class definitions.

Changes:

QuickGuide.jsx — replaced bespoke .quick-guide-overlay + .quick-guide-modal
with DaisyUI's `<div className="modal modal-open">` + `<div className="modal-box">`
+ `<div className="modal-backdrop">` structure. Close button is now
`btn btn-sm btn-circle btn-ghost` (absolutely positioned with Tailwind
utilities). Skip/Next buttons are `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` / `btn btn-primary
btn-sm` in a `modal-action` footer. Pagination dots converted from
.quick-guide-dot to inline Tailwind (w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary /
bg-base-300). Content text uses text-base-content / text-base-content/80
for the title/body hierarchy.

Modal-box width preserved via inline Tailwind (w-[min(420px,calc(100vw-32px))])
so the small-screen responsive cap still applies. Padding adjusted from
px-28-pt-32-pb-24 to the DaisyUI default with small overrides. Focus trap
and useEscapeKey hooks unchanged — they operate on the ref-held
container regardless of its class.

InstallInstructionsModal.jsx — same modal structure. The data-driven
numbered-step list is now a flex column with each step rendered as
<li class="flex items-start gap-3"> + a circular primary-tinted span for
the number. The optional warning note moves from .install-modal-note
to `<div role="alert" className="alert alert-warning alert-soft">` —
proper ARIA role AND DaisyUI alert component class. Benefits list keeps
its leading checkmarks as Unicode characters in the text (was a
::before pseudo-element before — simpler to put them in the JSX now
that the surrounding list is a flex column). Final "Got it" button is
`btn btn-primary btn-block` inside a modal-action wrapper.

styles.css — deleted two blocks (~130 lines total):
  - .quick-guide-overlay / .quick-guide-modal / .quick-guide-close /
    .quick-guide-content / .quick-guide-icon / .quick-guide-title /
    .quick-guide-body / .quick-guide-footer / .quick-guide-dots /
    .quick-guide-dot / .quick-guide-dot.active / .quick-guide-actions /
    .quick-guide-btn-primary / .quick-guide-btn-secondary + 3 keyframes
  - .install-modal-overlay / .install-modal-backdrop / .install-modal /
    .install-modal-header / .install-modal-steps / .install-modal-step /
    .install-modal-step-number / .install-modal-note /
    .install-modal-benefits / .install-modal-close

Each block replaced with a one-line comment pointing at the component
file so future contributors know why the class was removed.

Print override (@media print) — updated the "hide interactive chrome
when printing" rule from `.quick-guide-overlay, .quick-guide-modal` to
just `.modal` (DaisyUI's wrapper class, which now hosts both QuickGuide
and InstallInstructionsModal).

What's preserved:

- Focus trap via useFocusTrap — ref still threads through the modal-box
- Escape-to-close via useEscapeKey — unchanged
- Click-outside-to-close via onClick on .modal-backdrop — now a
  semantically-named DaisyUI class instead of custom .quick-guide-overlay
- Responsive width cap (min(420px, calc(100vw - 32px))) via inline
  Tailwind arbitrary value — matches the pre-migration visual
- Scroll lock via useScrollLock — unchanged (QuickGuide only)

  - npm run build: passes, 84 modules. CSS 160.06 → ~160 KB (tiny delta)
  - DaisyUI modal, modal-box, modal-backdrop, modal-action, modal-open
    classes all present in built CSS
  - All 4 QuickGuide step titles and all 3 install-instruction UI
    strings still present in built JS

commits.subject: feat(theme): burger menu stays open during theme picker interactions
commits.body:
Closes the one actionable gap found in the glow-props comparison: our
burger menu closed after every item click, including theme picker clicks,
forcing users to reopen the menu to try a second theme. glow-props
deliberately omits the `data-close` attribute on theme controls so the
menu stays open — users can rapid-preview multiple themes in a single
menu session. Adopting the same UX.

Changes:

1. HamburgerMenu.handleItem() now takes the full item object and checks
   item.keepOpen. Two paths:

     - keepOpen: true → run the action immediately, skip close(), skip
       the 150 ms setTimeout. Used for theme picker items and the
       dark/light mode toggle so users can rapid-preview multiple themes
       or switch modes then pick a theme without reopening the menu.

     - default → close the menu first, then run the action after 150 ms
       (matches the CSS fade animation so state changes from the action
       don't cause visual glitches while the menu is fading out). Used
       for Quick Guide, User Guide, Save as PDF, Install App, Update Now.

   Shared error handling extracted into a runAction(action) helper so
   both paths route errors through debugAdd('render', 'error', ...).

   Call site updated from handleItem(item.action) to handleItem(item).
   handleItem is internal to HamburgerMenu; no other callers.

   JSDoc updated to document the new keepOpen item field.

2. Header.jsx menuItems memo now sets keepOpen: true on:

     - The dark/light mode toggle item. Users can toggle modes and then
       pick a theme for the new mode in a single menu session. The theme
       list below the toggle swaps to the new mode's themes when the
       toggle action dispatches.

     - All 4 theme picker items for the current mode. Clicking Nord →
       theme applies, menu stays open, Nord is now marked active with
       checkmark. Clicking Emerald → theme applies, menu stays open,
       Emerald is now marked active. All 4 themes previewable in 4 clicks
       from a single menu open.

   Other menu items (Quick Guide, User Guide, Save as PDF, Install App,
   Update Now) keep their existing close-then-act behavior — only theme
   controls got keepOpen.

Why this is safe:

- Menu items are keyed by item.label in HamburgerMenu's render loop, and
  theme names are stable across re-renders. When activeThemeId changes
  after a picker click, React reconciles the buttons in place — the
  clicked button's DOM element is preserved, focus stays on it, only
  icon/highlight/ariaLabel props update.

- The ariaLabel change ("Use Nord theme (Cool blue-gray)" → "Use Nord
  theme (Cool blue-gray), currently active") provides natural
  screen-reader feedback confirming the theme was applied.

- click-outside, Escape, and the backdrop click handler all still work
  — keepOpen only suppresses the per-item auto-close. Users can always
  dismiss the menu.

- The 150 ms setTimeout path still applies to non-keepOpen items so
  close-animation-vs-action ordering is preserved for those.

Things from glow-props deliberately NOT adopted (documented in docs/HISTORY.md):

- 35-theme catalog. Our curated 4+4 is the right tradeoff for a utility
  app per the reference doc's own advice.
- Random theme on load. Wrong for an analytics dashboard.
- Auto-classify from color-scheme. Only helps with full catalogs; we
  already validate against DaisyUI's classification.
- Regex-based multi-file rewrite. Fragile to reformatting.
- Manual generator invocation. We prebuild for dev/build/build:lib.

Build and tests:
- npm test: 21 passing, 0 failing, 6 suites, ~145 ms
- npm run build: passes, 84 modules. CSS 160.06 KB (unchanged — no
  CSS change this pass). JS 555.75 → 556.03 KB (+0.28 KB for the
  refactored handleItem logic + keepOpen: true literals).
- Generator idempotent — all 4 downstream files report "unchanged"
- Smoke test via vite preview + curl verified: keepOpen identifier
  survived minification into the JS bundle; all 8 theme names,
  descriptions, aria-labels, CSS theme selectors, and flash
  prevention hex values still present; all 11 critical custom class
  families still emitted.

Documentation updated:
- docs/HISTORY.md — full entry with rationale for every not-adopted item
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md — sixth pass summary at the top
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md — 4 new test scenarios: rapid-preview behavior,
  mode toggle keepOpen, focus preservation after theme click, non-theme
  items still close the menu
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md — updated Theme section to mention rapid-preview

commits.subject: feat(theme): canva-grid alignment — oklchToHex module+tests, COLOR_KEY_OVERRIDES, debug flow tracing, Approach A/B docs
commits.body:
After cross-referencing our theming against canva-grid's implementation
(the sibling project using "Approach B" named combos), five patterns
from canva-grid were worth adopting without changing our Approach A
storage shape. All implemented this pass.

1. Extracted scripts/oklchToHex.mjs as a standalone module with 21 unit
   tests via Node's built-in node:test runner (no Jest dependency —
   node:test has been stable since Node 20, we're on Node 22). Tests
   cover CSS Color Level 4 edge cases (boundary values, achromatic
   colors, optional hue, alpha channel, whitespace, out-of-gamut
   clamping, invalid input) and DaisyUI fixtures (nord primary,
   autumn primary, dracula base-100, lofi base-300). npm test runs
   the suite in ~150 ms.

2. Fixed the L=1 percentage-vs-decimal edge case the old inlined
   version had. Old heuristic: `if (L > 1) L /= 100`. Couldn't
   distinguish `oklch(1 0 0)` (decimal, meaning white) from
   `oklch(1% 0 0)` (percent, near-black). New regex captures the
   percent character explicitly: `([\d.]+)(%?)` and only divides by
   100 when isPercent === true. Three dedicated tests pin the fix.
   DaisyUI always uses percent form so this never triggered in
   practice, but the fix is free and matches the CSS spec.

3. Added COLOR_KEY_OVERRIDES to scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs.
   Default rule now matches the reference:
     - Light themes: --color-primary (the brand accent)
     - Dark themes: --color-base-100 (the main surface)
   With overrides for monochrome/warm-minimal light themes whose
   primary is near-black:
     - lofi → --color-base-300 (→ #ebebeb, from canva-grid)
     - caramellatte → --color-base-300 (→ #ffd6a7, our addition —
       DaisyUI ships caramellatte with primary = oklch(0% 0 0)
       literal black, a DaisyUI design decision)

   New PWA status bar colors:
     lofi          light  #ebebeb  base-300  (was #ffffff)
     nord          light  #5e81ac  primary   (was #ffffff)
     emerald       light  #66cc8a  primary   (was #ffffff)
     caramellatte  light  #ffd6a7  base-300  (was #fff7ed)
     black         dark   #000000  base-100  (unchanged)
     dim           dark   #2a303c  base-100  (unchanged)
     coffee        dark   #261b25  base-100  (unchanged)
     dracula       dark   #282a36  base-100  (unchanged)

   Light themes now have theme-appropriate brand colors instead of
   uniform white. Mobile users see a distinct status bar per theme.

4. Added theme-change flow tracing via debugLog. New `theme` source
   color (lavender #c4b5fd) in DebugPill SOURCE_COLORS so theme
   events are visually distinct from render errors and boot events
   in the debug pill's log tab. themes.js imports debugAdd from
   debugLog.js (zero-import module, no circular dependency risk)
   and calls logThemeEvent('theme-applied', {dark, requested,
   validated, skipPersist}) at the end of every applyTheme()
   invocation. `requested !== validated` signals a stale or
   cross-mode theme id was dispatched. Every theme-apply path gets
   logged once: mount, toggle, picker click, cross-tab event, embed
   override. Pattern from canva-grid's useDarkMode hook.

5. Documented Approach A vs B tradeoff in CLAUDE.md Dashboard
   Architecture section. We use Approach A (per-mode independent,
   3 storage keys: darkMode/lightTheme/darkTheme) with 4 light + 4
   dark = 16 possible (mode, theme) combinations. canva-grid and
   other sibling projects use Approach B (named combos, 2 keys:
   darkMode/themeCombo) with typically 2-5 combo options. Our
   rationale: the dashboard is a utility app with no brand-coupled
   theme pairings, so constraining users to pre-vetted combos is
   unnecessarily restrictive compared to independent per-mode
   selection. Tradeoff: slightly more complex picker UX (two
   clicks: mode then theme) vs canva-grid's single-click combo
   buttons.

Deliberately NOT adopted from canva-grid (documented rationale):

- Combo-based persistence (Approach B). Structural change that
  would lose per-mode-independent theme choice. Conscious decision.
- Theme picker in page header instead of burger menu. canva-grid's
  inline join component is more discoverable but our dashboard
  header is already crowded with tab nav, filter controls, and
  settings. Burger menu placement is a deliberate UX decision.
- Regex-based index.html rewrite. canva-grid uses
  html.replace(/var combos = [^;]+;/, ...) which silently fails on
  reformat. Our marker-based BEGIN/END GENERATED approach fails
  loudly on missing markers and survives reformatting.
- Manual npm run generate-theme-meta. canva-grid's generator runs
  only on-demand. Ours runs as the first step of every dev/build/
  build:lib so drift is impossible.

Files changed:

New:
- scripts/oklchToHex.mjs (standalone module, improved parsing)
- scripts/__tests__/oklchToHex.test.mjs (21 tests)

Modified:
- scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs (import oklchToHex, add
  COLOR_KEY_OVERRIDES, per-theme colorKey in collected metadata,
  richer stdout report)
- dashboard/js/themes.js (import debugAdd, logThemeEvent helper,
  call from applyTheme)
- dashboard/js/components/DebugPill.jsx (add theme source color)
- dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js (regenerated hex values)
- dashboard/index.html (regenerated flash prevention META map)
- package.json (add "test" script)
- CLAUDE.md (Approach A vs B section)
- docs/HISTORY.md + docs/SESSION_NOTES.md (full chronology)

Build + test:
- npm test: 21 passing, 0 failing, 6 suites
- npm run build: passes, 84 modules, CSS 157.40 → 160.06 KB
  (+2.66 KB from new SOURCE_COLORS entry scanned into Tailwind)
- Generator idempotent: second run reports all 4 files unchanged
- themes.js loads cleanly via Node direct import with browser stub,
  applyTheme() runs through debugAdd path without throwing
- All 8 [data-theme=...] selectors present in built CSS
- All 8 new hex values present in inline flash prevention META map
- All 15 critical custom class families still emitted

Side catch: tried to write `theme/*/object.js` inside a JSDoc block
comment in the test file during initial draft. The */ sequence inside
the path terminated the block comment early and produced a Node syntax
error. CLAUDE.md prohibits this and AI_MISTAKES has a 2026-04-12 entry
for the same mistake. Fixed by converting the header to // line
comments with a note explaining why. This is the third time this
mistake happened in this session — every future block comment should
avoid filesystem paths containing stars.

commits.subject: fix(theme): self-audit cleanup — 3 bugs + 2 dead code + 1 optimization
commits.body:
Self-audit pass that caught findings the previous passes missed. Six real
issues plus documentation for two known quirks and test coverage gaps.

1. Bug: themes.js __allThemes / __isDark exports referenced undefined
   THEME_NAMES / IS_DARK after an earlier pass removed those imports.
   Rollup tree-shook them out of the browser bundle (nothing imports
   them) so the dashboard worked, but `node --input-type=module -e
   "import('./dashboard/js/themes.js')"` threw ReferenceError. Deleted
   the dead debug-helper exports — they were speculative "exposed for
   ad-hoc console inspection" helpers with zero consumers, violating
   CLAUDE.md's "Don't design for hypothetical future requirements."

2. Bug: persistTheme left stale lightTheme / darkTheme keys on
   revert-to-default. Example: user picks Nord then reverts to Lo-Fi
   (default). Old code wrote `darkMode=false` and SKIPPED the lightTheme
   write. lightTheme='nord' stayed in localStorage. On next reload,
   flash prevention applied Nord — the user's most recent pick was lost.
   Fix: safeStorageRemove() the per-mode key on revert-to-default instead
   of skipping the write. Added safeStorageRemove to the themes.js
   imports. Documented the "remove key means revert to default" contract
   in the function comment.

3. Bug: cross-tab listener filtered e.newValue === null events. Old
   handler had `if (e.key === 'lightTheme' && e.newValue)` which treated
   null as "nothing to do". But fix #2 now removes keys on revert, firing
   storage events with newValue=null — other tabs need to see that and
   revert their reducer state to the default. Fix: drop the && e.newValue
   filter; validLightTheme / validDarkTheme already handle null → default
   via the .has(id) check failing. Symmetric darkMode key removal falls
   back to matchMedia. Comment rewritten to document the contract.

4. Dead code: getStoredTheme(dark) in themes.js — defined, exported,
   never called anywhere (grep confirmed). Holdover from an earlier pass
   where the darkMode effect called applyTheme(state.darkMode,
   getStoredTheme(state.darkMode)) — replaced with direct reducer state
   reads. Deleted the function entirely.

5. Dead code: getStoredTheme import in AppContext.jsx — same reason.
   Removed from the import line.

6. Clarity: loadInitialState matchMedia fallback now reads matchMedia
   directly instead of document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark').
   The old code relied on the flash prevention script having set the
   class from the same matchMedia query — functionally equivalent but
   indirect. Reading matchMedia directly keeps the function independent
   of DOM side effects and makes it unit-testable without a DOM.

7. Optimization: darkMode useEffect dependency narrowed from
   [darkMode, lightTheme, darkTheme] to [darkMode, activeTheme].
   activeTheme is derived outside the effect as `state.darkMode ?
   state.darkTheme : state.lightTheme`. With the old deps, a cross-tab
   event that updated the NON-active mode's theme triggered a no-op
   effect re-run with wasteful getComputedStyle + Chart.js defaults
   write. With the new deps the effect only re-runs when the
   currently-applied mode's theme changes.

End-to-end verification (new this pass):

Added a Node-based smoke test that stubs localStorage, window, document,
getComputedStyle, dynamically imports themes.js, and exercises:
  - Catalog shape (4 light + 4 dark { id, name, description } objects)
  - DEFAULT_LIGHT_THEME / DEFAULT_DARK_THEME constants
  - Validators: null, undefined, unknown, cross-mode, valid inputs
  - persistTheme: write on non-default, REMOVE on revert-to-default
  - Approach A per-mode persistence for both light and dark
This test caught bug #2 during the audit before any rebuild.

All tests pass. Output excerpt:
  After persistTheme(false, nord):       lightTheme = nord
  After persistTheme(false, lofi) revert: lightTheme = undefined (removed)
  After persistTheme(true, dracula):  darkTheme = dracula
  After persistTheme(true, black):    darkTheme = undefined (removed)

Documentation:

- CLAUDE.md: architecture line for js/themes.js updated — removed
  getStoredTheme mention, documented persistTheme / getMetaColor.
- docs/HISTORY.md: new entry with full finding list + rationale.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: new "Known quirks" section documenting
  mount-time darkMode persistence (limits OS fallback to first paint,

commits.subject: feat(theme): single-source config + 4x4 catalog + burger-menu picker + reference-shape cross-tab handler
commits.body:
Closes all seven residual gaps against docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md
documented in the previous "differences" audit. The user asked for "no
shortcuts, no skipping", so this pass implements every actionable gap and
explicitly documents why the two remaining items would be speculative
abstraction (CLAUDE.md prohibition).

Gaps closed:

1. (C#1) Theme catalog was string arrays — now [{ id, name, description }, ...]
   per reference. Validator sets rebuilt via .map(t => t.id).

2. (C#2 + C#3) Generator only rewrote themeMeta.js — now rewrites FOUR files
   from a single source (scripts/theme-config.js) via BEGIN/END GENERATED
   marker blocks:
     - dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js (full rewrite)
     - dashboard/js/themes.js theme-catalog block
     - dashboard/styles.css daisyui-plugin block
     - dashboard/index.html flash-prevention-meta block
   Each write is content-equality checked so idempotent reruns don't bump
   mtimes / trigger Vite HMR. Validates config at load time: fails fast on
   invalid defaults, wrong color-scheme classification, or nonexistent
   DaisyUI theme ids.

3. (C#5) Cross-tab handler filtered theme events by current mode — now
   dispatches unconditionally for darkMode / lightTheme / darkTheme keys,
   matching the reference's setIsDark/setLightThemeState/setDarkThemeState
   shape. AppContext reducer gains lightTheme/darkTheme state with no-op
   early-return guards to skip re-renders when cross-tab events replay
   the current value.

4. (C#7) No theme picker UI — new menu items in Header.jsx filtered to
   the current mode via LIGHT_THEMES / DARK_THEMES. Active theme gets
   the highlight class + checkmark icon, inactive themes get a palette
   icon. Each item has an explicit ariaLabel like "Use Nord theme
   (Cool blue-gray), currently active".

5. Catalog expanded from 1+1 to 4+4 curated themes (prerequisite for C#7):
     light: lofi / nord / emerald / caramellatte
     dark:  black / dim / coffee / dracula
   All DaisyUI stock — no custom theme definitions. Sits inside the
   reference's "2-5 curated combos (or 8-10 themes per mode)" range.

6. Generator fail-fast validation (new this pass) — error paths tested:
     - Invalid DEFAULT_*_THEME          -> error + exit 1
     - Nonexistent theme id             -> error + exit 1
     - Wrong color-scheme classification -> error + exit 1

7. setTheme(themeName) helper — new useAppDispatch() export that infers
   which mode the theme belongs to via validLightTheme/validDarkTheme and
   dispatches SET_LIGHT_THEME or SET_DARK_THEME. Unknown ids silently
   ignored (reducer guards prevent garbage state).

Deliberately NOT addressed (speculative abstraction per CLAUDE.md):

- (C#4) CSP hash for inline flash prevention script. No CSP deployed,
  computing a hash that nothing verifies is dead code. Revisit when
  CSP is added to vercel.json.

- (C#6) Legacy localStorage key cleanup (theme / colorMode / dark-mode).
  Our pre-DaisyUI system used the same darkMode key, nothing to clean up.
  Adding a defensive safeStorageRemove would be a no-op runtime cost.

New files:
- scripts/theme-config.js

Rewritten:
- scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs (reads theme-config.js, propagates to 4 files,
  idempotent writes, fail-fast validation)

Modified:
- dashboard/js/themes.js (BEGIN/END markers, object-shape catalog, updated
  validators, removed obsolete "currently one theme per mode" caveat)
- dashboard/styles.css (BEGIN/END markers around @plugin "daisyui" block)
- dashboard/index.html (BEGIN/END markers inside inline flash prevention script)
- dashboard/js/AppContext.jsx (lightTheme/darkTheme reducer state, new action
  types + guards, darkMode effect reads from reducer state, unconditional
  cross-tab dispatch, setTheme helper via dispatch context)
- dashboard/js/components/Header.jsx (imports LIGHT_THEMES/DARK_THEMES, new
  palette/check icons, menuItems memo builds theme picker for current mode)
- dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js (regenerated with 8 themes' hex values)

Docs:
- CLAUDE.md (architecture list: theme-config.js + expanded generator description)
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md + docs/HISTORY.md (full chronology, gap-by-gap resolution)
- docs/TODO.md (collapsed — only optional component-class migration left)
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md (generator tests, error path tests, idempotence
  cross-check, theme picker tests, "add a new theme" developer flow)
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md (theme picker explanation with all 8 themes)

Build: passes. CSS bundle 150.64 KB -> 157.40 KB (+6.76 KB, +4.5%) from 6
additional DaisyUI theme CSS-variable blocks (each ~1 KB, expected and
unavoidable). 84 modules transform. Only CSS warning is DaisyUI's internal
@property --radialprogress (not ours).

- Inline HTML flash prevention script: all 4+4 theme ids + 8 hex values
- Built CSS: all 8 [data-theme="..."] selectors (lofi..dracula)
- Built JS: all 8 theme names and descriptions (Lo-Fi/Minimal monochrome
  through Dracula/Dev classic)

commits.subject: refactor(theme): align with THEME_DARK_MODE reference — catalog module, applyTheme helper, meta generator, allowlist
commits.body:
After the full DaisyUI migration landed on this branch, a side-by-side
comparison with docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md surfaced seven
divergences from the reference pattern. None were bugs that would
immediately manifest, but all were the kind of structural drift that
decays into bugs the moment someone adds a theme or changes a default.
This pass closes the gap on every actionable divergence.

Divergences addressed:

1. No theme catalog module — theme names and meta colors were hardcoded
   module constants at the top of AppContext.jsx.
2. No applyTheme() helper — the same dual-layer DOM mutation (.dark +
   data-theme + meta theme-color + Chart.js defaults) was inlined in
   three places (AppContext darkMode effect, App.jsx embed override,
   inline flash prevention script). Any drift between them = visual bugs.
3. PWA meta theme-color hex values hardcoded — reference wants a build
   script that reads DaisyUI's theme/*/object.js and converts oklch to
   hex at build time so the values never drift.
4. No theme ID allowlist in flash prevention script — a removed theme
   ID in localStorage would produce an unstyled first paint.
5. Cross-tab storage listener only handled darkMode — no forward-compat
   for lightTheme/darkTheme keys a theme picker would write.
6. No explicit aria-label on the theme toggle menu item — the visible
   label "Light mode"/"Dark mode" describes a destination, not an action.
7. No getStoredTheme/persistTheme/validLightTheme/validDarkTheme helpers
   exposed as part of a catalog module.

New files:

scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs — imports each registered DaisyUI theme's
  object.js via dynamic import(), runs an inlined oklch->hex converter
  (oklch -> oklab -> linear sRGB via LMS -> gamma-corrected sRGB -> hex,
  ~30 lines, no color library dependency), and writes
  dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js with META_COLORS, IS_DARK, and
  THEME_NAMES exports. Validated against known values: lofi base-100
  oklch(100% 0 0) -> #ffffff, black base-100 oklch(0% 0 0) -> #000000.

dashboard/js/themes.js — theme catalog module. Exports LIGHT_THEMES,
  DARK_THEMES, DEFAULT_LIGHT_THEME, DEFAULT_DARK_THEME, validLightTheme,
  validDarkTheme, getStoredTheme, persistTheme, getMetaColor, and most
  importantly applyTheme(dark, themeName, skipPersist) — the single
  source of truth for .dark class + data-theme attribute + meta
  theme-color content + Chart.js defaults. Every theme-affecting caller
  now routes through this function. Imports META_COLORS/IS_DARK/
  THEME_NAMES from ./generated/themeMeta.js and Chart from chart.js
  (ES modules cache the Chart singleton shared with main.jsx and
  AppContext.jsx).

dashboard/js/generated/themeMeta.js — auto-generated, committed so fresh
  clones work without running the generator first. Header says DO NOT
  EDIT BY HAND. Regenerated by the prebuild hook on every npm run dev
  and npm run build.

Refactored:

dashboard/js/AppContext.jsx — removed LIGHT_THEME/DARK_THEME/
  LIGHT_META_COLOR/DARK_META_COLOR module constants and the direct
  `import { Chart as ChartJS } from 'chart.js'` (both moved to
  themes.js). darkMode useEffect collapsed from 30 lines of inlined DOM
  mutations to a single applyTheme(state.darkMode,
  getStoredTheme(state.darkMode)) call. Cross-tab storage listener now
  handles three keys — darkMode dispatches to the reducer, lightTheme
  and darkTheme call applyTheme(..., skipPersist=true) directly when
  the key matches the current mode.

dashboard/js/App.jsx — imports applyTheme + DEFAULT_*_THEME from
  themes.js. Embed override ?theme=light|dark calls applyTheme with
  skipPersist=true instead of doing its own classList + setAttribute
  dance. ?bg=... still sets --color-base-100 directly since it's a
  pure surface override, not a theme swap.

dashboard/index.html — inline flash prevention script gained
  LIGHT_THEMES/DARK_THEMES allowlists, a validTheme() helper, and a
  META map inline. Reads darkTheme/lightTheme from localStorage in
  addition to darkMode, validates each, falls back to defaults on
  miss. Applies .dark class + data-theme attribute + meta theme-color
  content before first paint. Script comment documents the four-place
  sync requirement (this script + themes.js + styles.css @plugin
  config + generate-theme-meta.mjs REGISTERED_THEMES).

dashboard/js/components/Header.jsx — theme toggle menu item gained an
  explicit ariaLabel: state.darkMode ? 'Switch to light mode' :
  'Switch to dark mode'. Visible label stays the same ("Light mode" /
  "Dark mode"). Comment explains why the destination-style label needs
  an action-style aria-label.

dashboard/js/components/HamburgerMenu.jsx — accepts an optional
  item.ariaLabel prop on menu items, applied as the button's aria-label
  attribute. Falls back to item.label for menu items that don't need
  a distinct screen-reader label.

package.json — dev, build, and build:lib scripts all prefix with
  `node scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs &&` so the generated file is
  always fresh. Added `npm run generate-theme-meta` for manual
  invocation after adding themes.

Not addressed (feature decisions, not bugs):

- Approach A vs Approach B storage schema. This project has one theme
  per mode and no picker UI, so neither approach is "chosen" — we ship
  Approach A's key schema (darkMode + lightTheme + darkTheme) as a
  superset with the extra keys being forward-compat plumbing only.
  Documented in themes.js top comment.
- Theme picker UI in burger menu — tracked as optional follow-up in
  docs/TODO.md. Infrastructure (catalog, cross-tab sync, allowlist,
  persistTheme) is all in place.
- DaisyUI component classes instead of custom CSS — already tracked
  as optional follow-up. Separate incremental pass from theming.

Build: passes. CSS bundle 150.64 KB (unchanged — no CSS changes this
pass, only JS + HTML refactoring). 84 modules transform (up from 82
because themes.js and generated/themeMeta.js are new). Only CSS warning
is DaisyUI's internal @property --radialprogress (not ours).

Smoke test via vite preview + curl verified:
- HTML <html lang="en" class="dark" data-theme="black">
- Both <meta name="theme-color"> tags with media queries
- Inline flash prevention script contains LIGHT_THEMES, DARK_THEMES,
  'lofi': '#ffffff', 'black': '#000000', validTheme
- Built CSS contains both [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"]
- All 14 critical custom class families present (.heatmap-0..4,
  .heatmap-cell, .filter-multi-select*, .settings-pane*, .detail-pane*,
  .error-boundary-card, .dashboard-header, .card, .btn-primary,
  .toast-success)

Grep verification: themes.js is the only file that touches
classList.add/remove('dark'), setAttribute('data-theme', ...), or
ChartJS.defaults. Zero duplication.

commits.subject: fix(theme): restore custom CSS silently dropped by broken comment, finish semantic token migration
commits.body:
The previous commit on this branch (c7a3469) claimed a 16% CSS bundle
reduction (147 KB -> 123 KB). That reduction turned out to be a build
artifact of a broken CSS comment, not a genuine savings.

Root cause:

In the rewritten :root block at the top of dashboard/styles.css, the
migration-rationale comment contained the literal text:

    - Keep legacy --bg-*/--text-*/--border-* aliases: Rejected — ...

The `*/` sequence inside `--bg-*/--text-*` terminates the CSS comment
early (at `--bg-*`), and the parser sees everything after it as bad CSS.
Tailwind v4 / esbuild's minifier silently drops everything following the
parse error, which meant ~27 KB of custom class definitions were never
emitted to the built bundle:

  - All .heatmap-* classes (intensity levels 0-4, cell, label, header,
    grid, tooltip-inner)
  - All .filter-multi-select* classes (dropdown, option, trigger)
  - All .settings-pane* classes (overlay, header, content, close, title)
  - All .detail-pane* classes (overlay, header, close, content, empty,
    loading, subtitle, title)
  - .root-error-message, .root-error-detail, .root-error-hint,
    .error-boundary-card
  - Several others

The build passed with no top-level errors — the "Unexpected token Delim
('*')" was buried as Issue #1 inside the "Found 62 warnings while
optimizing generated CSS" summary, alongside DaisyUI's unrelated
@property / @keyframes warnings. The dashboard would have shipped with
heatmaps, detail panes, settings panes, and filter multi-select all
rendering without their custom CSS, falling back to browser defaults.

Fix:

1. Rephrased the comment in dashboard/styles.css to avoid the `*/`
   sequence. "Keep legacy --bg-*/--text-*/--border-* aliases" became
   "Keep legacy --bg, --text, --border aliases" with a trailing note
   warning future sessions never to write asterisk-slash inside CSS
   comments.

2. Added a CLAUDE.md Frontend checklist item prohibiting asterisk-slash
   sequences inside CSS comments and explaining why.

3. Added a detailed AI_MISTAKES.md entry with the smoke-test bash snippet
   that would have caught this.

Verification:

After the fix, the built CSS is 150.6 KB (vs the misleading 123 KB from
the broken build, and vs the pre-migration 147.16 KB). The +3.5 KB delta
is DaisyUI's theme selector blocks + semantic component classes being
added, partially offset by deleted custom variables. Verified all
critical custom class families are present via curl against the preview
server:

  OK: .heatmap-0 .heatmap-1 .heatmap-2 .heatmap-3 .heatmap-4
  OK: .heatmap-cell .heatmap-tooltip-inner
  OK: .filter-multi-select .settings-pane .detail-pane
  OK: .error-boundary-card .root-error-message
  OK: .dashboard-header .card .tab-btn-active
  OK: .btn-icon .btn-primary .toast-success .collapsible-header
  Both [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"] selectors present.

Second pass — additional semantic token migration (while I was in there):

While investigating the root cause I also found several residual raw
Tailwind status colors that should have been migrated in the first pass
but were missed. Migrated:

  Timing.jsx author threshold indicators
    text-green-600/amber-600/red-600 -> text-success/warning/error
  HealthAnomalies.jsx debt balance net indicator
    text-red-500/green-500 -> text-error/success
  Discover.jsx pin button
    text-blue-500 hover:text-blue-500 -> text-primary hover:text-primary
  Discover.jsx metric selector focus ring
    focus:ring-blue-500 -> focus:ring-primary
  Summary.jsx / Timeline.jsx / Progress.jsx / HealthWorkPatterns.jsx
    hover:ring-blue-500 -> hover:ring-primary on stat cards

All these are semantic status / brand indicators, not data viz category
colors, so they belong in DaisyUI's semantic token system rather than
raw Tailwind.

Documentation corrections:

Updated docs/SESSION_NOTES.md and docs/HISTORY.md to correct the false
"147.16 -> 123.27 KB (-16%)" claim. Real numbers: 147.16 -> 150.6 KB
(+3.5 KB, +2%). Added a "Regression caught and fixed" section to both
explaining what happened and why the first commit reported wrong
numbers.

commits.subject: refactor(theme): complete DaisyUI v5 migration — all six reference phases
commits.body:
Follow-up to the initial "infrastructure + partial" commit on this branch.
User requested no shortcuts, so this completes Phases 1-6 of the reference
doc (docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md) in a single pass: full CSS
variable removal, component migration across 21 files, and cleanup.

Phase 1 — Audit:
  202 var(--bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-*/--chart-grid/--glow-*/--shadow*)
  references in styles.css, 166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed
  references across 21 JSX files, 39 dark: Tailwind pairs across 10 JSX
  files, and ~15 hardcoded rgba(45, 104, 255, ...) brand-blue values.

Phase 2 — Custom variable removal (dashboard/styles.css):
  Rewrote :root to hold ONLY non-theme tokens (spacing, radius, z-index,
  fonts). Deleted the entire html.dark override block — DaisyUI's
  [data-theme] selectors handle switching now. Bulk-replaced via
  replace_all:
    var(--bg-primary/secondary/tertiary/hover) →
      var(--color-base-100/200/300/300)
    var(--text-primary) → var(--color-base-content)
    var(--text-secondary/tertiary/muted) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 80%/60%/40%, transparent)
    var(--border-color/light) → var(--color-base-300/200)
    var(--glow-primary) →
      0 0 20px color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 25%, transparent)
    var(--chart-grid) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 10%, transparent)
    var(--color-primary-alpha) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 10%, transparent)

  Replaced hardcoded brand-blue rgba(45, 104, 255, X) values with color-mix
  against var(--color-primary) — focus rings, btn-primary hover, drop-zone
  states, tab-btn-active text-shadow, body::before techy grid background.
  The brand accent now follows the active theme's primary color.

  Deleted 3 legacy CSS blocks: Tailwind gray overrides (.bg-gray-50/100,
  .text-gray-400/500/600/700/900, .border-gray-100/200, etc.), semantic
  card backgrounds (.bg-amber-50, .bg-indigo-50, ..., .bg-red-50), and
  semantic text colors (.text-green-600, .text-red-600, .text-amber-600,
  .text-blue-600, .border-blue-500).

  Deleted 8 utility class definitions: .text-themed-primary/secondary/
  tertiary/muted, .bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary, .border-themed.

  Swapped color: white / #1a1a1a / #fff hardcoded foregrounds to DaisyUI
  *-content tokens in .toast-success/error/warning, .btn-primary,
  .quick-guide-btn-primary, .filter-preset-btn:hover/.active,
  .filter-mode-toggle button.active, .project-link-primary, .filter-badge.

  .install-modal-note amber rgba literals → color-mix against warning.
  .drop-zone-heading color: #e5e7eb → var(--color-base-content).

  Print styles @media print intentionally keep hardcoded white/black/#e5e7eb
  per the reference doc's Print Override section.

Phase 3 — Component migration (21 JSX files, all 39 dark: pairs):
  Zero dark: class names remain in JSX (only in migration comments).

  166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed references migrated to
  DaisyUI Tailwind utilities:
    text-themed-primary → text-base-content
    text-themed-secondary/tertiary/muted →
      text-base-content/80, /60, /40
    bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary → bg-base-100/200/300
    border-themed → border-base-300

  Summary.jsx Activity Snapshot cards: 4 data viz pastel bg/dark pairs →
    bg-warning/15 + text-warning (after-hours),
    bg-info/15 + text-info (weekend),
    bg-accent/15 + text-accent (holiday),
    bg-secondary/15 + text-secondary (complex).
  Timeline.jsx complexity badges: purple/blue/gray tier colors →
    bg-secondary/20 (high) / bg-info/20 (medium) / bg-base-300 (low).
  Health.jsx security panels: all bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20 + border-
    red-200/800 + text-red-600/400 → bg-error/10 + border-error/40
    + text-error.
  HealthBars, HealthAnomalies, Contributors, Tags, Timing, Progress,
    Discover: progress-bar rails → bg-base-300, hover states →
    hover:bg-base-200 / hover:bg-base-300.

  App.jsx embed override now sets --color-base-100 (DaisyUI token) instead
  of the removed --bg-primary alias, and toggles data-theme alongside .dark.

  Tags.jsx removed its per-component useChartTextColor hook and explicit
  color: CHART_TEXT_COLOR overrides — now relies on ChartJS.defaults.color
  (kept fresh by AppContext's darkMode effect) as the single source of
  truth. Chart rebuilds on theme toggle via existing state.darkMode useMemo
  dep.

Chart.js theme sync:
  AppContext.jsx darkMode effect no longer reads removed --text-secondary /
  --chart-grid. It reads DaisyUI's --color-base-content oklch token
  directly and passes color-mix(in oklab, X 80%/10%, transparent) to
  ChartJS.defaults.color / borderColor. Canvas parses color-mix() since
  Chrome 111 / Firefox 113 / Safari 16.2 — same baseline as DaisyUI.

Phase 5 — Verification:
  TESTING_GUIDE.md Theme section rewritten with full 10-point checklist:
  system preference, dual-layer attributes, flash prevention, cross-tab
  sync, PWA status bar, charts, surface auto-switching, semantic tokens,
  print override. Build passes and Vite preview serves correctly — DaisyUI
  [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"] selectors present in the
  served CSS (verified via curl).

Phase 6 — Cleanup:
  Zero custom --bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-primary-alpha/--chart-grid/
  --glow-*/--shadow* variables remain anywhere in the codebase (verified
  via comprehensive Grep — only remaining matches are migration comments).
  Updated CLAUDE.md Frontend standards to enforce DaisyUI semantic tokens
  and prohibit re-introducing the deleted variables.

Build: passes. CSS bundle 147.16 KB → 123.27 KB (−23.9 KB, −16%) from
deleted variables, gray overrides, and utility classes.

Docs updated: CLAUDE.md, docs/SESSION_NOTES.md, docs/HISTORY.md,
docs/TODO.md, docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md, docs/USER_GUIDE.md.

Remaining (optional) future work: replace raw Tailwind / custom-class
elements with DaisyUI component classes (btn, card, input, badge) —
tracked in docs/TODO.md. Not required for the migration to be complete.

commits.subject: feat(theme): migrate to DaisyUI with dual-layer theming and meta theme-color
commits.body:
Partial migration to DaisyUI per docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md.
DaisyUI semantic tokens now coexist with the existing custom CSS variable
system — incremental replacement to stay low-risk rather than a big-bang
rewrite of the 2000+ line styles.css.

Infrastructure:

1. Installed daisyui@5 (5.5.19) as devDependency. Registered two themes
   via @plugin "daisyui" { themes: lofi --default, black --prefersdark }
   in styles.css. --default / --prefersdark set the initial theme before
   JavaScript runs. Added @layer base { html { color-scheme: light } html.dark
   { color-scheme: dark } } so native form inputs and scrollbars match.

2. Dual-layer theming wired up end-to-end: <html> gains data-theme="black"
   alongside class="dark" default. Flash prevention inline script in
   index.html now sets BOTH .dark class AND data-theme attribute before
   first paint, and also overwrites <meta name="theme-color"> content so
   the PWA status bar matches the active theme from the first frame.

3. Added two <meta name="theme-color"> tags with (prefers-color-scheme: ...)
   media queries. Defaults #ffffff (lofi base-100) and #000000 (black
   base-100). AppContext.darkMode effect now calls setAttribute('data-theme',

   correctly via the shared darkMode effect.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): deduplicate helpers, fix strict mode double-mount race
commits.body:
1. Extract formatDebugTime and safeStringify from DebugPill into
   debugLog.js as shared exports. Eliminates duplicate timestamp
   formatting logic (DebugPill formatTime vs inline in report
   generation) and duplicate safe-stringify logic (DebugPill
   safeStringify function vs inline try/catch in report).

2. Fix React strict mode double-mount race: in dev mode, effects
   run twice (mount → cleanup → mount). Without a reset, the second
   subscription replay appends to the first mount's state, doubling
   every entry. Adding setEntries([]) before subscribing ensures
   each mount builds cleanly from empty.

commits.subject: refactor(debug): use debugAdd for pre-React error bridge instead of manual entry construction
commits.body:
The bridge code was bypassing debugAdd() — directly constructing entry
objects, pushing to the internal entries array, and incrementing nextId
manually. If debugAdd changes (new fields, validation, side effects),
the bridge would be out of sync.

Fix: add optional timestamp parameter to debugAdd (defaults to
Date.now()), then call debugAdd from the bridge with the original
error timestamp. All entry construction now flows through one function.

commits.subject: fix(debug): preserve pre-React error timestamps, stop inline pill DOM work
commits.body:
Two fixes to the inline pill / debugLog bridge:

1. Inline pill now stores Date.now() (numeric epoch) instead of
   toLocaleTimeString() (formatted string). Matches the spec's inline
   pill code. The bridge in debugLog.js creates entries with the
   original error timestamp instead of module-load time, so pre-React
   errors show accurate times in the React DebugPill's Log tab.
   Added fmtTime() helper for the inline pill's own display and copy.

2. Inline pill render() now bails early when window.__debugReactMounted
   is true. The error listeners still fire (can't remove from IIFE
   closure), but DOM operations on the hidden banner stop. Eliminates
   wasted work after React mounts.

commits.subject: fix(debug): harden against circular refs, throwing toString, timer leaks
commits.body:
Three defensive fixes for edge cases that could crash or warn:

1. Circular references in entry details: JSON.stringify throws on
   circular objects, crashing the Log tab (separate root, no
   ErrorBoundary). Added safeStringify() in DebugPill and try/catch
   in debugGenerateReport — both fall back to '[unserializable]'.

2. Throwing toString in console interception: args.map(String) could
   throw if an argument has a Proxy or throwing toString(). Added
   safeString() wrapper — falls back to '[unstringifiable]'. Original
   console output is unaffected (called before debugAdd).

3. Timer leak in handleCopy: setTimeout for resetting copy label had
   no cleanup. Added copyTimerRef with clearTimeout on unmount and
   on re-click. Prevents React state-on-unmounted-component warning.

commits.subject: fix(debug): fix duplicate entries, HMR accumulation, clear state, source colors
commits.body:
Four bugs found via deep audit:

1. Duplicate entries on initial mount: setEntries(debugGetEntries()) +
   debugSubscribe replay doubled every entry. The subscription replays
   all current entries immediately, so the direct set was redundant.
   Removed it — subscription alone handles initial population.

2. Console interception HMR accumulation: console.error/warn patching
   had no HMR guard. Each Vite hot reload re-captured the already-patched
   methods as "originals," creating nested wrapper chains. After N reloads,
   each console.error produced N debug entries. Added __debugConsolePatched
   window flag matching the existing __debugLogListenersAttached pattern.

3. Clear button didn't reset reportText: if the visible textarea fallback
   was showing (clipboard failed), clicking Clear emptied entries but left
   the stale report text visible. Now resets reportText to null.

4. Missing import/export source colors: App.jsx uses debugAdd('import',
   ...) for file upload errors but SOURCE_COLORS had no entry, falling
   back to default gray. Added colors for both import and export sources.

1. Move embed-mode skip from DebugPill (conditional return before hooks,
   violates React rules) to main.jsx (don't mount component at all).
2. Remove unused status param from diagRow style function (dead code).

   id) to PWA diagnostics tab per DEBUG_SYSTEM.md spec.
4. Add visible textarea fallback with auto-select when all clipboard
   methods fail, per spec's manual copy requirement.
5. Route Projects.jsx console.error through debugAdd for structured
   logging instead of relying on console interception.
6. Verify no duplicate entries from console interception + explicit
   debugAdd — all callers use one or the other, never both.

commits.subject: fix(build): exclude data dirs from Tailwind scanning, add test scenario
commits.body:
Tailwind v4 auto-scans all subdirectories of the CSS file. JSON data
files in public/data-commits/ and public/repos/ contain commit history
with Tailwind-like class names (e.g. "z-[9999] → z-[80]" from other
projects' changelogs). This caused phantom z-index CSS utilities in
the build output — unused but violating the "no ad-hoc values" rule.

Fix: @source not directives in styles.css exclude both directories.
Build output now contains only scale-mapped z-index values.

Also:
- Added z-index visual stacking test scenario (TESTING_GUIDE.md)
- Documented hamburger menu stacking context limitation (TODO.md):
  backdrop/dropdown trapped inside header z-21, drawers (z-30) render
  above the backdrop. Fix would require portaling — low priority.

- Sub-layer rationale for --z-sticky-header (21), --z-drawer-backdrop

  base scale's "backdrop always 40" rule

commits.subject: fix(debug): HMR double-wrap, re-entrancy guard, race condition, tick()
commits.body:
Three bug fixes and one code quality improvement:

- debugLog.ts: Store true original console.error/warn on window so HMR
  module re-execution doesn't nest wrappers (each cycle was adding a
  layer that duplicated log entries). Add re-entrancy guard flag on
  console interception — prevents infinite recursion if any subscriber
  or library calls console.error during a debugAdd callback chain.

- +layout.svelte: Add `destroyed` flag to dynamic import callback.
  Without it, if the layout unmounts before the import resolves, the
  cleanup function runs with pill=null, then the import creates an
  orphaned pill that's never destroyed.

- DebugPill.svelte: Replace setTimeout(0) with Svelte's tick() for
  auto-scroll — tick() waits for pending DOM updates to flush, which
  is the correct Svelte primitive for "act after DOM update". Remove
  redundant `entries = debugGetEntries()` before subscribe — the
  subscriber's immediate delivery already handles initialisation.

Tags: debug, hmr, race-condition, svelte
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: debug-system
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(debug): harden debug system — SSR safety, cleanup, type exports
commits.body:
Audit and fix issues from initial implementation:

- debugLog.ts: Wrap all browser side-effects (console interception,
  global error listeners, pre-framework bridge) in typeof window guard
  to prevent Node.js console patching during SSR build. Export
  DebugSource, DebugSeverity, MAX_ENTRIES for consumers. Extract shared
  formatDebugTimestamp() helper (eliminates duplication between report
  generator and pill display). After bridge imports pre-framework
  errors, remove inline listeners and clear buffer to prevent
  double-capture. Add boot log entry on module init.

- app.html: Store inline error/rejection listeners as named window
  references (__debugInlineErrorHandler, __debugInlineRejectionHandler)
  so debugLog.ts can remove them after takeover.

- DebugPill.svelte: Use shared formatDebugTimestamp(). Show structured
  entry.details below event text when present. Remove unnecessary
  typeof window guard inside onMount. Type tab array with as-const for
  type safety. Add stale-run guards after each async probe in PWA
  diagnostics. Log boot confirmation on mount.

- +layout.svelte: Dynamic import of DebugPill (confirmed debugLog.ts
  absent from SSR bundle). Store pill reference, return cleanup
  function for proper $destroy() on unmount.

Tags: debug, diagnostics, ssr, cleanup

commits.subject: fix(ui): remove click-to-close from modal backdrops
commits.body:
The previous commit added on:click handlers to the backdrop divs,
introducing click-outside-to-close behavior that didn't exist before.
This is a UX change, not a z-index structural change — the Save Modal's
cancelSave clears user input, so an accidental outside click would
discard typed text. Removed the handlers to keep this commit purely
structural (z-index normalization only, no behavior changes).

Backdrops remain as separate z-40 divs with default pointer-events,
which correctly blocks clicks from reaching page content underneath.

commits.subject: fix(ui): split modal backdrop (z-40) and modal (z-60) into adjacent siblings
commits.body:
The previous commit placed the combined backdrop+modal div at z-60,
violating the glow-props Z_INDEX_SCALE rule: "Backdrop is always z-40
regardless of what it's behind." This matters for predictability — any
future component relying on "backdrop is at 40" would be wrong.

Split both modals (Save Modal, Comparison View) into:
- Backdrop div: fixed inset-0, bg-background/*, z-40, click-to-close
- Modal container div: fixed inset-0, z-60, pointer-events-none
- Modal card: pointer-events-auto (receives clicks through the container)

commits.subject: fix(print): overhaul print CSS for dark theme — fix invisible text, align with glow-props pattern
commits.body:
The existing print CSS only set `body { background: white; color: black }`
but Tailwind utility classes (text-foreground, bg-card, bg-secondary-light)
still resolved to dark theme values — producing white text on white paper
and dark gray card backgrounds. This was a critical readability bug for
anyone using the "Save as PDF" or "Print / PDF" features.

Fix: Override :root CSS variables with light-theme values in @media print
so all Tailwind classes automatically resolve to print-friendly colors.
Also override hardcoded dark hex colors (secondary-light, secondary-dark),
add link styling, .print-avoid-break utility class, sticky→static override,
section/card/result-panel break-inside rules, and !important on
print-color-adjust. Full alignment with glow-props DOWNLOAD_PDF pattern.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): update banner not showing + button not working
commits.body:
Two bugs:

1. checkVersionUpdate() on initial load detected new versions but the
   return value was discarded — hasUpdate never became true, so the
   banner only appeared after backgrounding/foregrounding the tab.

2. When version.json detected the change (not SW lifecycle), there was
   no waiting service worker. updateServiceWorker(true) sent SKIP_WAITING
   to nothing — no reload happened. Added 2s fallback to location.reload().

commits.subject: refactor(debug): migrate DebugPill from inline styles to DaisyUI tokens
commits.body:
The DebugPill was the last component with hardcoded hex colors. The
assumption that it needed inline styles to survive CSS failure was
wrong — the Vue DebugPill only mounts after Vue is running, which
guarantees CSS is loaded. The pre-framework inline pill in index.html
(which stays hardcoded) handles the CSS-failure case.

Changes:
- All inline :style objects replaced with Tailwind/DaisyUI classes
  (bg-neutral, text-neutral-content, border-neutral-content/10, etc.)
- Source/severity/status colors now use resolveThemeColor() to read
  DaisyUI tokens (--color-primary, --color-info, --color-error, etc.)
  so the debug panel adapts to any DaisyUI theme
- Colors recompute reactively when isDark changes (theme switch)
- Removed ~40 inline style objects from template

commits.subject: feat(theme): use DaisyUI tokens for all colors, remove @theme block
commits.body:
All colors now come from DaisyUI semantic tokens — zero custom color
definitions remain.

- Removed @theme block entirely (overspend/underspend/data-neutral were
  unused; brand palette already removed)
- Created useThemeColor.ts: resolveThemeColor() reads DaisyUI's oklch
  CSS variables from <html> at runtime and converts to hex via a 1x1
  canvas pixel — no manual color math, browser handles gamut mapping
- CashflowGraph: replaced 8 hardcoded hex values with DaisyUI token
  reads (info=actuals, warning=forecast, success=balance, base-content
  =labels, base-300=grid). Chart colors now adapt to any DaisyUI theme.
  Hex fallbacks only used if CSS variable read fails (SSR, tests).
- Print CSS: kept hardcoded hex — print output is always forced white/black
  regardless of theme

commits.body:
The <meta name="theme-color"> tags were still hardcoded to the old
brand color #10b981 from before the DaisyUI migration. The default
combo is "classic" which uses #808080 (light) and #000000 (dark).

This caused the PWA status bar to flash the wrong color between HTML
parse and Vue mount, since only the composable updated it dynamically.

Changes:
- index.html meta tags: #10b981 → #808080 (light) / #000000 (dark)
- Flash prevention script: now also sets meta theme-color before
  first paint (reads combo meta colors from hardcoded combos object)
- vite.config.ts manifest theme_color: #10b981 → #808080

commits.subject: fix(tooling): add ESLint as devDependency and resolve all 45 lint errors
commits.body:
The project had eslint.config.js and a lint script but ESLint was never
installed — npm run lint would fail. Installed eslint@9.x, @eslint/js,

commits.subject: feat(theme): migrate from custom CSS variables to DaisyUI v5
commits.body:
Replace the custom :root/.dark CSS variable token system with DaisyUI's
semantic theme architecture. This gives users theme switching for free
and eliminates ~200 manual dark: class pairs across 30+ components.

6-phase migration following glow-props THEME_DARK_MODE.md:
- Phase 0: Install daisyui@5, configure @plugin with 4 themes
- Phase 1-2: Audit + remove custom --color-* variables
- Phase 3: Replace all component classes with DaisyUI semantics
- Phase 4: Z-index already aligned (no changes needed)
- Phase 5: TypeScript + Vite build verification
- Phase 6: Documentation updates

Key changes:
- Dual-layer theming: data-theme (DaisyUI) + .dark class (Tailwind)
- Named combos (Approach B): "Classic" (lofi/black), "Nature" (emerald/forest)
- Flash prevention script sets both layers before first paint
- Print handler saves/restores data-theme alongside .dark class
- HelpDrawer prose: oklch(var(--bc)) replaces var(--color-text-default)
- Brand colors retained in @theme for chart data visualization

commits.subject: feat(pwa): version.json supplementary update detection
commits.body:
Adds the last remaining item from the glow-props PWA_SYSTEM pattern:
build-time version.json for detecting app changes that don't modify
sw.js (edge case where precache manifest stays identical).

- versionJsonPlugin() in vite.config.ts emits version.json with an
  ISO buildTime timestamp on every production build
- checkVersionUpdate() in usePWAUpdate.ts fetches /version.json with
  cache: 'no-store', compares against localStorage
- Runs on initial load (stores current version) and on visibility
  change (throttled to once per minute to avoid spamming the server)
- If buildTime differs from stored value, sets hasUpdate = true
  (suppression watcher still applies within 30s of an update)

commits.subject: fix(pwa): Chromium fallback instructions were unreachable
commits.body:
The Chromium fallback instructions added to the install modal could
never be displayed because needsManualInstructions returned false for
Chromium browsers without a native prompt. The modal only opens when
showInstallPrompt is true, which requires either isNativeInstallAvailable
or needsManualInstructions.

Fix: After the 5-second diagnostic timeout fires on a Chromium browser
without a beforeinstallprompt event, set chromiumFallback ref to true.
needsManualInstructions now includes this flag, making the install
banner show "How to Install" with manual steps (address bar icon,
browser menu). This covers the 90-day suppression window after a user
dismisses Chrome's native prompt.

Also added clarifying comment for iOS Firefox → firefox-desktop mapping.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): full install detection sync + ChunkLoadError prevention
commits.body:
Closes all remaining gaps between budgy-ting and the glow-props
PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

Install detection (usePWAInstall.ts):
- Browser detection expanded from 3 to 7 Chromium browsers
  (+ opera, samsung, vivaldi, arc)
- Brave detection uses navigator.brave existence check instead of
  UA string (Brave Mobile strips "Brave" from UA)
- CHROMIUM_BROWSERS exported constant as single source of truth
- iOS non-Safari detection: CriOS/FxiOS/EdgiOS caught before the
  Safari check to avoid misclassifying iOS Chrome as Safari
- display-mode: standalone change listener detects install via
  browser menu (not just the native beforeinstallprompt flow)
- installed-via-browser analytics event type added

Install instructions (InstallInstructionsModal.vue):
- iOS non-Safari redirect tells users to open Safari with
  explanation of why other browsers can't install
- Chromium fallback instructions for when native prompt fails
  (Chrome suppresses for 90 days after dismissal)
- "Why install?" benefits section (offline, home screen, fullscreen)
- Per-browser warning notes (Brave Shields, Firefox desktop)

Deployment resilience (router/index.ts):
- lazyRetry() wrapper on all 5 lazy route imports — reloads once
  on ChunkLoadError (stale chunk 404 during deploy window)
- sessionStorage flag prevents infinite reload loops

commits.subject: fix(mobile): fix TDZ crash in BottomSheet — reorder useCallback declarations
commits.body:
BottomSheet.jsx had a temporal dead zone (TDZ) bug: handleTouchMove's
useCallback dependency array referenced snapToNearest before its const
declaration. const/let are not hoisted like function declarations, so
accessing them before their declaration line causes a ReferenceError:
"Cannot access 'm' before initialization" (minified variable name).

Fix: Move snapToNearest declaration before handleTouchMove and finishTouch
which both reference it in dependency arrays and closure bodies.

Also captures stack traces in debug system error handlers (console.error
interception + global error listener) to make future minified crash
debugging easier.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): settle delay, cleanupOutdatedCaches, install prompt diagnostic
commits.body:
Three improvements found during pattern audit:

1. checkForUpdate() now has a 1500ms settle delay after
   registration.update() — SW lifecycle events fire asynchronously
   after update() resolves, so without the delay the "Checking..."
   state flashes invisibly fast and events may not have propagated.
   Also added error handling with debugLog.

2. cleanupOutdatedCaches: true in workbox config — removes stale
   caches from incompatible older Workbox major versions that
   accumulate across deployments.

3. Fixed DebugPill install prompt diagnostic: usePWAInstall deletes
   window.__pwaInstallPromptEvent after consuming it, so the
   diagnostic always showed "Not received." Now sets a
   __pwaInstallPromptReceived flag before deleting, and DebugPill
   checks both sources.

Tags: pwa, debug, service-worker, cleanup

Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-system
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JjbdKNBLFZ6CmJkVqngkqm
commits.title: settle delay, cleanupOutdatedCaches, install prompt diagnostic

commits.subject: feat(pwa): visibility-based update checks + 30s post-update suppression
commits.body:
Two improvements to service worker update detection from glow-props
PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

1. visibilitychange listener triggers registration.update() when the
   tab regains focus — catches deploys that happened while the tab was
   backgrounded, without waiting for the next 60-minute interval.

2. wasJustUpdated() checks a sessionStorage timestamp to suppress false
   update re-detection within 30 seconds of applying an update. The
   browser's SW lifecycle may fire statechange events during activation
   that look like a new update is available.

PWA diagnostics tab (task 3) was already implemented in the prior
DEBUG_SYSTEM sync — no changes needed.

commits.subject: fix(pdf): comprehensive print output cleanup — pagination, layout, dark mode, interactive elements
commits.body:
The initial Save as PDF button worked but produced poor print output:
paginated to 25 rows, showed mobile cards instead of table, leaked dark
mode styles, and left interactive elements (chart toggles, form inputs,
banners) visible on paper.

This commit addresses all of those issues:

- beforeprint/afterprint in TransactionTable bypasses pagination to
  show all transactions (not just the current 25-row page)
- print-show CSS utility forces desktop table layout in print (A4
  width < sm breakpoint was showing mobile cards instead)
- beforeprint/afterprint in AppLayout temporarily removes .dark class
  for light-mode print (direct DOM toggle, no reactive side effects)
- ApexCharts @media print CSS hides toolbar, forces readable text
  colors on white paper regardless of dark mode
- no-print on chart toggle buttons, cash-on-hand input, AppLayout
  banners (update/offline/install prompt)
- Cash-on-hand shows as static text in print instead of <input>

commits.subject: fix(pwa): fix stale hasUpdate closure in checkForUpdate handlers
commits.body:
handleCheckForUpdate in both DesktopLayout and MobileLayout read
hasUpdate after the async checkForUpdate() settle delay. But hasUpdate
is a prop captured at render time — if onNeedRefresh fires during the
1500ms settle, the closure still holds the stale pre-check value. This
caused "You're on the latest version" toast even when an update was
just detected.

Fix: toast always says "Check complete" on success. If an update was
found during the settle, the update banner/menu item appears via normal
hasUpdate prop re-render — no need to duplicate that signal in the toast.

Also fix CLAUDE.md Quick Reference: TEST_RUNNER was "Manual" but the
project now has Jest with 133 automated unit tests.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): detect iOS browser variants (CriOS/FxiOS/EdgiOS) in detectBrowser
commits.body:
iOS Chrome uses CriOS, Firefox uses FxiOS, and Edge uses EdgiOS in their
UA strings instead of Chrome/Firefox/Edg. Without detecting these, all
iOS non-Safari browsers fall through to 'safari' (their UAs contain
"Safari" but not "Chrome"), making the iOS cross-redirect in
getInstallInstructions() dead code — it checks browser !== 'safari'
which was always false on iOS.

Now iOS Chrome → 'chrome', iOS Firefox → 'firefox', iOS Edge → 'edge'.
This enables the correct flow: supportsAutoInstall → 5s diagnostic
timeout (prompt never fires on iOS) → showManualInstructions → modal
shows "Open in Safari" cross-redirect with explanation.

Tags: pwa, browser-detection, ios

Impact: user-facing

Semver: patch

commits.body:
usePWAUpdate:
- Add _isChecking module-level guard to checkForUpdate() preventing
  concurrent calls from overlapping (double-tap protection).

usePWAInstall — full alignment with glow-props PWA_SYSTEM.md pattern:
- Expand browser detection from 5 to 9 types (add Opera, Samsung
  Internet, Vivaldi, Arc). Samsung/Opera ordered before Chrome in UA
  checks since their UA strings contain both identifiers.
- Export CHROMIUM_BROWSERS constant as single source of truth.
- Add BROWSER_DISPLAY_NAMES map for UI rendering.
- Brave detection uses 'brave' in navigator (UA stripped on mobile).
- Display-mode change listener catches browser-menu installs where
  appinstalled event doesn't fire.
- 5-second diagnostic timeout on Chromium: logs warning with manifest/SW
  status, falls back to manual instructions (Chrome suppresses prompt
  for 90 days after user dismissal).
- trackInstallEvent() localStorage analytics (prompted/installed/
  dismissed/installed-via-browser), capped at 50 entries.
- install() wrapped in try/catch — Chrome throws DOMException if
  prompt() is called twice on the same event.
- iOS non-Safari cross-redirect in getInstallInstructions() — tells
  users to open in Safari with explanation why.
- Install instructions for Samsung Internet and Opera.
- Vivaldi/Arc fall through to generic Chromium instructions.
- Effect deps corrected to include supportsAutoInstall and browser.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): catch unhandled rejections from registration.update()
commits.body:
Visibility handler and hourly interval call _registration.update()
fire-and-forget. If the registration becomes invalid (SW unregistered
externally, network error), the returned Promise rejects without a
handler — generating console errors and triggering debug console
interception, potentially spamming the log buffer.

checkForUpdate() already wraps this in try/catch. Added .catch(() => {})
to the two fire-and-forget callers for parity.

1. DebugPill copyReport setTimeout leaked on unmount — setCopyLabel could
   fire after component destroyed. Added copyTimerRef with cleanup effect
   and clearTimeout before each new timer.

2. debugLog deduplication mutated existing entry object in-place (count++,
   timestamp update). React subscribers do setEntries([...allEntries])
   which creates a new array but same object references inside. React
   compares by key+ref and skips re-render for the mutated entry — the
   x{count} badge never updated visually. Fixed by replacing the entry
   with a spread copy ({ ...last, count, timestamp }) so React sees a
   new reference and re-renders the row.

commits.subject: feat(robustness): safety snap timeout, debug log deduplication
commits.body:
Two hardening measures for production edge cases:

1. BottomSheet safety snap timeout: If touchend is lost due to OS gesture
   hijack (iOS swipe-back, Android nav) or browser bug, the sheet auto-
   snaps to nearest position after 500ms of no touch events. Prevents
   stuck-mid-drag state where sheet freezes at arbitrary height. Timer
   resets on every touchmove, cleared on touchend/touchcancel, cleaned
   up on unmount.

2. Debug log deduplication: Consecutive identical messages (same source +
   event + severity) are collapsed into a single entry with a count field
   and updated timestamp. Prevents third-party console.warn/error spam
   and React strict mode double-warnings from pushing real app entries
   out of the 200-entry circular buffer. Count displayed as yellow 'x{n}'
   badge in the Log tab and '(x{n})' suffix in text reports.

Tags: mobile, debug, robustness

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low

commits.subject: fix(debug): safe stringify, stable React keys, capture real stack traces
commits.body:
Three issues found during fresh-eyes full branch audit:

1. debugLog.js: JSON.stringify(e.details) in debugGenerateReport could
   throw on circular references, crashing report generation entirely.
   Added safeStringify wrapper with try/catch → '[unserializable]'
   fallback. Report generation must never crash.

2. DebugPill.jsx: PWA diagnostics results list used array index as React
   key. Results are cleared and rebuilt on each re-run — index keys cause
   incorrect DOM reuse if result order ever changes. Replaced with
   r.label which is unique and stable per diagnostic check.

3. index.html: Pre-React error handler passed filename:lineno as the
   'stack' parameter to __debugPushError. The ErrorEvent has e.error.stack
   with the full stack trace — now captured when available, with
   filename:lineno as fallback for cross-origin script errors where
   e.error is null.

commits.subject: fix: add oklchToHex decimal tests, fix BottomSheet focus-stealing
commits.body:
Two remaining items from full branch audit:

1. oklchToHex: Added 4 tests covering the decimal-without-percent path
   that was untested after the L normalization fix. Tests verify:
   - decimal 0.5 equals 50% (same mid-gray)
   - decimal 1 (no %) is white (L=1.0, not 1%)
   - percentage 1% is near-black (L=0.01)
   - decimal 0 is black
   Test count: 72 → 76.

2. BottomSheet: Focus-steal useEffect fired on every open, including when
   sheet was already open and user just switched tabs via MobileNav. This
   yanked focus from the just-tapped nav button after 350ms. Now tracks
   previous isOpen via wasOpenRef — only auto-focuses on closed→open
   transition. Sheet starts open (useState true) so initial mount also
   correctly skips the focus steal.

commits.subject: fix: full branch audit — 6 bugs and dead code across components, hooks, scripts
commits.body:
Full diff audit of all 63 files changed on this branch (not just debug
system commits). Found and fixed:

1. BurgerMenu.jsx: ArrowUp off-by-one when focus is outside button list.
   idx=-1 computed (length-2) instead of (length-1). Added idx===-1 guard
   to go to last item, matching ArrowDown which already goes to first.

2. BottomSheet.jsx: handleTouchEnd called snapToNearest on every touchend,
   including content taps that were never promoted to a sheet drag. Added
   isDragging guard to skip snap logic for non-drag touches.

3. useAdState.js: Dead getPageCount function — defined and exported but
   never destructured or called (App.jsx uses pages.length directly).
   Removed function and export.

4. useDarkMode.js: comboId in useEffect dep array was redundant (activeTheme
   is derived from comboId + isDark). Caused effect to fire twice per combo
   change, double-logging 'theme-applied'. Moved comboId to ref for logging,
   removed from deps.

5. generate-theme-meta.mjs: Dead comboMapStr variable — built with
   JSON.stringify + replace but never used. Line 159 uses
   JSON.stringify(comboMap) directly. Removed.

6. oklchToHex.mjs: L normalization heuristic (L > 1) couldn't distinguish
   oklch(1% ...) from oklch(1 ...). Regex now captures % sign in a group
   and uses it to decide normalization instead of the boundary-ambiguous
   heuristic.

commits.subject: refactor(hooks): simplify useFocusTrap to pure Tab-trapping
commits.body:
useFocusTrap previously did three things: focus first element on
activate, trap Tab/Shift+Tab at boundaries, and restore focus on
deactivate. The first and third are redundant with useDisclosureFocus
which already handles initial focus (via rAF) and return-to-trigger
(on close). No other consumers — modals use native <dialog> focus trap.

Stripped to just Tab boundary wrapping. Removes useRef import and
~15 lines of redundant focus management.

Also tracks App.jsx stale closure Escape handlers (lines 332-338)
in TODO.md — discovered during burger menu review.

commits.subject: feat(theme): install DaisyUI v5, delete all custom CSS variables
commits.body:
DaisyUI v5 with corporate (light) and business (dark) themes — both
have blue primaries, zero color overrides needed.

Deleted all 50+ custom CSS variables (:root and html.dark blocks).
Replaced 395+ var() references with DaisyUI theme tokens:
  --color-base-100/200/300 (backgrounds)
  --color-base-content (text)
  --color-primary/success/error/warning (semantic)

Where DaisyUI has no token (text opacity levels, primary alpha),
used color-mix() with theme variables. Hardcoded semantic colors
(tag bugfix/fix/enhancement) replaced with DaisyUI error/success/
warning tokens. Shadows/overlays stay hardcoded (theme-independent).

Added data-theme attribute and <meta name="theme-color"> to flash
prevention script, AppContext, and index.html.

Only remaining CSS variables: z-index scale (no DaisyUI equivalent),
chart-accent-rgb and tag-* (runtime dynamic set by JS).

commits.subject: feat(debug): add React DebugPill with pub/sub, console interception, clipboard fallbacks
commits.body:
Two-phase debug system: inline HTML pill (pre-React) + React DebugPill
(post-mount in separate #debug-root, survives App crashes).

- debugLog.js: typed pub/sub event store, 200-entry circular buffer,
  URL query param redaction in reports
- debugConsoleInterceptor.js: patches console.error/warn with reentrant
  guard to prevent infinite recursion
- DebugPill.jsx: 3 tabs (Log, Environment, PWA), collapsed pill badge,
  3-tier clipboard fallback (ClipboardItem > writeText > textarea)
- main.jsx: transfers inline errors, hides inline pill, mounts React pill,
  uses original console.error in ErrorBoundary to avoid double-capture

keep emit private).

commits.subject: feat(pwa): add version.json polling for non-SW deployment detection
commits.body:
When a deployment only changes config (e.g. vercel.json headers) but no
code, the SW precache manifest stays identical and no update is detected.
This is how the X-Frame-Options stale cache issue persisted.

Added version.json polling (synctone/few-lap pattern):
- scripts/write-build-version.mjs writes { buildTime } at build time
- npm build runs it as a prebuild step
- pwa.js fetches on startup (3s delay) and hourly, compares with
  localStorage, surfaces mismatch as pwa-update-available
- applyUpdate() stores the new buildTime to prevent re-detection
- vercel.json serves version.json with no-cache headers
- Not precached by SW (not in globPatterns)
- Gitignored (generated file)

commits.subject: fix(pwa): add settle delay and surface waiting workers on visibility
commits.body:
Two gaps found comparing with synctone/few-lap patterns:

1. checkForUpdate() checked reg.waiting immediately after reg.update()
   resolved, but the update process is async — the waiting worker may
   not be populated yet. Added 1.5s settle delay (synctone/few-lap
   pattern).

2. visibilitychange handler called reg.update() but never surfaced the
   result. The onNeedRefresh callback from registerSW only fires once
   during setup — separate reg.update() calls don't re-trigger it.
   Now checks for waiting workers after settle delay and dispatches
   pwa-update-available manually.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): align SW update flow with synctone/few-lap/canva-grid patterns
commits.body:
Cross-project review found 4 issues:

1. skipWaiting/prompt conflict: skipWaiting:true in workbox config
   auto-activated new SWs, causing controllerchange reload before the
   user ever saw the update prompt. Removed — now user-controlled via
   applyUpdate() which sends SKIP_WAITING message.

2. Surprise reloads: controllerchange listener reloaded on ANY SW
   takeover, including background lifecycle events. Added
   _userClickedUpdate guard so only user-initiated updates reload.

3. False re-detection: After reload from update, onNeedRefresh could
   fire again for the same SW version. Added 30s sessionStorage
   suppression window (pattern from synctone/few-lap).

4. No recovery: If SW serves corrupt assets, app hangs on spinner
   forever. Added 30s recovery script that clears caches and
   unregisters SW (max 2 attempts per session, pattern from
   synctone/few-lap).

commits.subject: fix(pwa): bypass SW navigation cache for embed URLs
commits.body:
The service worker precaches index.html with response headers from when
it was installed. If installed before the X-Frame-Options removal, the
cached response still included the old SAMEORIGIN header — blocking
cross-origin iframes. Since the iframe blocks before JS runs, the SW
update code never executes, creating a deadlock.

Added navigateFallbackDenylist for ?embed= URLs so they always bypass
the SW cache and go directly to the network (Vercel).

commits.subject: fix(theme): use named import for Uniwind — fixes theme switching
commits.body:
The `import Uniwind from 'uniwind'` was a default import, but the
package only exports `Uniwind` as a named export. This made `Uniwind`
undefined at runtime, so every `Uniwind.setTheme()` call silently threw
a TypeError. The CSS class on <html> never changed, keeping className-
based styles (backgrounds, text — "pretty much everything") stuck on
the initial system-scheme theme (synthwave via @variant dark).

Also added a try/catch fallback in `applyTheme` that manually updates
the CSS class on <html> if Uniwind.setTheme ever fails again, so theme
switching degrades gracefully instead of silently breaking.
