commits.subject: fix(theme): move theme state to Zustand store so all components re-render on switch
commits.body:
useTheme() previously used local useState — each of the ~25 components
calling it got independent copies of isDark and comboId. When SettingsModal
toggled dark mode, only SettingsModal re-rendered. All other components kept
stale colors for inline styles (className-based styling via Uniwind CSS
variables updated correctly, but inline style colors did not).

Fix: Replace useState with a shared Zustand store (useThemeStore). Now when
any component calls toggleMode/setCombo/setDark, the store update triggers
re-renders in ALL components consuming useTheme().colors.

The hook's public API is unchanged — no consumer modifications needed.

commits.subject: fix(ui): restore mobile column layout for toolbar, DaisyUI btn classes
commits.body:
Root cause of double-tap regression: commit 08a80bb migrated .app-toolbar
from custom CSS to Tailwind utilities but dropped flex-direction: column
on mobile. Original CSS:
  @media (max-width: 768px) { .app-toolbar { flex-direction: column; } }
Tailwind replacement was flex row on all sizes, causing ViewSwitcher
(shrink-0 max-md:w-full) to consume 100% row width and push QueryBuilder
to 0 width. The QueryBuilder's invisible overflow sat on top of the tabs,
consuming the first tap.

Fix: add max-md:flex-col max-md:gap-2 max-md:px-3 to toolbar container,
matching the original column layout on mobile.

Removed touch-manipulation band-aid from ViewSwitcher — it was masking
the layout bug, not fixing it.

Other DaisyUI compliance fixes:
- CreateNodeModal/ConnectNodeModal: custom cancel buttons → btn btn-ghost
- ConnectNodeModal: custom list item buttons → btn btn-ghost btn-sm
- ContextMenu: z-1000 → z-[1000] (valid Tailwind arbitrary value)

The refresh button only refetched chat data without checking for PWA
updates. Installed PWA users had no way to manually detect new deploys
except waiting for the hourly automatic check or closing/reopening the
app. Now the refresh button also calls checkForUpdates() which checks
both the service worker and version.json for new versions.

Also removed the standalone gate from the manual checkForUpdates() call
since the render condition (isInstalled && hasUpdate) already prevents
showing the update button to non-PWA browser users.

Tags: pwa, bugfix, update-detection

margins, and layout properties that can interfere with mobile touch responsiveness.

commits.subject: perf(ui): stabilize render-path object references in LocationSearch and StationList

Inline style objects in .map() and FlatList renderItem create new references every
render, defeating React's shallow comparison and causing unnecessary child re-renders.

LocationSearch:

  so FlatList renderItem and .map() items share one stable reference
- Extract recent search onPress handler to useCallback (handleSelectRecent)
  so .map() items don't create new closures per render

StationList:
- SkeletonCard: Replace inline { backgroundColor: colors.base100 } with className
  bg-base-100 — eliminates JS color resolution, uses CSS variable directly
- Remove colors prop from SkeletonCard and SkeletonList — no longer needed

commits.subject: refactor(styling): move font, color, and layout inline styles to className
commits.body:
Convert inline style objects to Tailwind className utilities where the
properties have direct equivalents. Eliminates redundant style props and
reduces the useTheme()/colors dependency footprint.

Key changes:
- Font styles: fontSize/fontWeight/letterSpacing → text-xl, font-bold,
  tracking-tight, text-[28px], etc.
- Theme colors: style={{ color: colors.primary }} → text-primary,
  style={{ backgroundColor: colors.base200 }} → bg-base-200
- Layout: flex/padding/margin/minHeight/width/overflow → flex-1, px-3,
  py-2.5, min-h-8, w-[280px], overflow-hidden
- Conditional colors: ternary style objects → conditional className strings
- Removed redundant style={{ color: colors.baseContent }} where className
  already had text-base-content (ChatHeaderMenu)

Import cleanup:
- ToneEditModal: removed useTheme + withAlpha (fully className-based now)
- MessageInfoModal: removed useTheme (fully className-based now)
- DesktopLayout.DesktopLayout: removed unused useTheme

commits.subject: feat(theme): add build script to auto-generate PWA meta theme-color hex
commits.body:
Add scripts/generate-theme-meta.mjs that reads DaisyUI oklch theme
values and converts them to hex for PWA meta theme-color tags. The
script updates daisyuiThemes.ts metaColor values, index.html meta
tags, and the flash prevention script's metaColors object.

Light themes use --color-primary, dark themes use --color-base-100.
Conversion: oklch → oklab → LMS → linear sRGB → gamma sRGB → hex.
Script is idempotent — no writes when values are already correct.

Fixed incorrect pastel meta-color: #fdeef4 (wrong) → #e9d4ff (correct,
derived from pastel's oklch primary value).

Run: npm run generate:theme-meta (after DaisyUI updates or combo changes)

commits.subject: refactor(markdown): replace regex parser with marked library
commits.body:
The hand-rolled regex markdown parser had fundamental structural bugs:
multi-line code blocks broken by the line-by-line paragraph regex,
double-escaping of already-escaped content, and raw < characters
creating broken HTML elements that truncated rendering.

Replace with marked (v17, ~43KB bundled/13KB gzipped) configured with
a custom renderer for DaisyUI styling:
- Code blocks: copy button wrapper, manual escapeHtml
- Inline code: custom badge-like classes
- Tables: scrollable overflow wrapper, parseInline for cell tokens
- Links: URL validation (allowlist http/https/relative), link-primary class
- Bold: font-semibold class
- Checkboxes: DaisyUI checkbox-xs styling
- Everything else: marked defaults (headings, paragraphs, blockquotes, lists)

Migration from classic scripts to ES modules:
- public/markdown.js (classic IIFE) -> src/markdown.js (ES module)
- Vite bundles marked as a dependency
- HTML script tags updated to type="module" (deferred, execute in order)
- Inline page scripts also type="module" so they run after markdown.js

Verified: 42 markdown files render correctly, 0 double-escaped entities,
all code block counts match, all files render to completion.

Tags: markdown, refactor, marked, dependencies
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: markdown-ux
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix: add /embed path route for cross-origin iframe support
commits.body:
The X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN header (audit fix H4, 19e1e21)
blocked cross-origin iframes, breaking embeds in see-veo and other
apps. Vercel matches headers by path only, not query params, so
the /?embed= route couldn't be exempted.

Solution: Add /embed path with X-Frame-Options exempted in vercel.json.
New URL format: /embed?charts=id1,id2 (cross-origin safe).
Legacy /?embed= still works for same-origin use.

Changes:
- vercel.json: restore SAMEORIGIN, exempt /embed and /embed.js paths
- App.jsx: detect embed mode from /embed path + ?charts= param
- index.html: skip debug pill on /embed path
- EMBED_REFERENCE.md: document new /embed URL format

Note: see-veo needs to update EMBED_BASE or iframe URLs to use
/embed?charts= instead of /?embed= for cross-origin to work.

Tags: security, embed, bugfix
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: embed-system
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(markdown): use placeholder approach to protect code blocks from paragraph regex
commits.body:
The previous fix (inline escaping) solved the raw < issue but missed a critical structural bug: multi-line code block content was being matched by the paragraph regex (/gm flag processes each line independently). The code block replacement puts escaped content between 
 and
, but middle lines of multi-line code don't start with <, so the paragraph regex wraps them in

tags. This: - Breaks 

 structure (browser auto-closes 
 at first 
)
- Double-escapes entities (paragraph handler calls inlineMarkdown again)
- Causes visible truncation as code block HTML structure collapses

Fix: Extract code blocks and tables into arrays with single-line
placeholders (, ) before running any
line-by-line regex. Restore them after all structural processing.
This ensures multi-line content is never exposed to line-by-line regexes.

Verified: 42 markdown files, 0 double-escaped entities, 0 unescaped <,
all code block counts match, all files render completely to last line.

Tags: markdown, bugfix, truncation, code-blocks
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: markdown-ux
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(markdown): prevent content truncation from unescaped HTML in rendered markdown
commits.body:
Root cause: The markdown parser had a two-phase design where structural
replacements (headings, lists, paragraphs) injected raw captured text
without escaping, then a catch-all pass (/>([^<]+)</g) tried to apply
inline formatting to all text between HTML tags. This caused three bugs:

1. TRUNCATION: Raw < in markdown (e.g. "use <details>") passed through
   unescaped into innerHTML, creating real HTML elements that swallowed
   all subsequent content. The catch-all regex treated < as a tag
   boundary rather than content to escape.

2. DOUBLE-ESCAPING: Code block content (already escaped by escapeHtml)
   was re-processed by the catch-all pass, turning < into &lt;
   and corrupting displayed code.

3. DOUBLE-PROCESSING: Table cells (already formatted by inlineMarkdown
   in the table handler) were re-processed, double-escaping entities.

Fix: Apply inlineMarkdown() directly in each structural replacement
(headings, lists, blockquotes, paragraphs, checkboxes). This escapes
HTML entities BEFORE injection into the HTML string, then applies
inline formatting (bold, italic, links, code). The catch-all phase 2
pass is removed entirely.

Additional fixes:
- Normalize \r\n and \r line endings to \n at parse start — Windows
  line endings broke the code block regex which requires \n after the
  opening fence
- Table headers now use inlineMarkdown (were using escapeHtml only,
  missing bold/italic/link/code formatting in headers)
- Paragraph regex updated to also skip lines starting with </ and <!
  (closing tags and HTML comments from code block wrappers)

Tags: markdown, bugfix, truncation, xss-prevention
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: markdown-ux
Semver: patch

commits.body:
1. Fix incorrect documentation about Uniwind status color limitation.

   The CLAUDE.md and global.css claimed status colors (error, success,
   warning, info) don't generate Tailwind utility classes in Uniwind.
   This is wrong — all --color-* CSS custom properties generate utilities
   identically. Verified by reading Uniwind's rule-visitor.ts source:
   processThemeRoot processes all variables uniformly with no filtering.

   Fixed: removed "inline only" annotations from CLAUDE.md color table,
   updated global.css header comment, updated Styling System description.

2. Migrate PWADebugBanner.tsx from hardcoded hex colors to useTheme().

   Replaced all #C9A57B (old rose gold) and #0A0A0A (old background)
   references with colors.primary, colors.primaryContent, colors.base100,
   colors.base200, colors.neutral, colors.baseContent from useTheme().
   Kept semantic log level colors (red/yellow/green) as fixed values
   since they represent severity, not theme.

commits.body:
Self-review found 4 issues in the PWA alignment commit:

1. Recovery script broken (high): SW no longer auto-skipWaits, so
   r.update() installed new SW but it never activated. Fixed: script
   now sends SKIP_WAITING to waiting/installing workers after update.

2. Chromium install gap (medium): "Install app" menu item did nothing
   for Chromium without native prompt (90-day cooldown). Fixed:
   needsManualInstructions now includes Chromium when hasNativePrompt
   is false.

3. Removed dead canInstall from usePWAInstall return (duplicated
   hasNativePrompt).

4. Removed dead applyUpdate() from usePWAUpdate (no longer imported).

commits.subject: refactor(pwa): comprehensive PWA implementation alignment
commits.body:
Compared few-lap PWA with synctone's implementation and strengthened
every PWA file to match best practices from both projects.

Key changes:
- usePWAUpdate: module-level singleton (persists across navigation),
  getRegistration() over register(), 30s false-positive suppression,
  user-controlled SKIP_WAITING instead of auto skipWaiting()
- usePWAInstall: triple-layer beforeinstallprompt capture, module-level
  event consumption, granular 8-type browser detection, richer API
- sw.js: user-controlled activation, 3-tier caching (static/dynamic/fonts),
  branded offline fallback page
- manifest.json: display_override, scope, lang, dir
- +html.tsx: __pwaPromptReceived flag in early capture
- _layout.tsx: early SW registration + backup prompt capture at module scope
- InstallInstructionsModal: Chromium desktop/Android/iOS, 90-day cooldown,
  Chrome-on-iOS redirect to Safari, analytics tracking
- vercel.json: explicit SW/manifest rewrites, Service-Worker-Allowed,
  Content-Type headers, must-revalidate cache control
- config.ts: JUST_UPDATED_SUPPRESS_MS constant

Tags: pwa, refactor, architecture, alignment

commits.subject: Merge pull request #45 from devmade-ai/claude/fix-post-deployment-app-state-GioLH
commits.body: Fix post-deployment broken app state with versioned SW and auto-reload
commits.title: Merge pull request #45 from devmade-ai/claude/fix-post-deployment-app-state-GioLH

commits.subject: fix(pwa): fix post-deployment broken app state
commits.body:
After every Vercel deployment, the PWA got stuck showing no header, no map,
no data, and an offline banner. The only recovery was clearing app data.

Root causes:
- sw.js was a static file that never changed between deployments, so the
  browser never detected a new SW and never showed an update banner
- staleWhileRevalidate for HTML served cached old HTML referencing new JS
  bundle hashes not yet in cache
- SW returned empty Response('', {status: 503}) for failed JS fetches,
  causing silent partial rendering (components fail without visible errors)
- No self-recovery mechanism — with no header visible, users were stuck

Fixes:
- SW: Build-time version injection (inject-sw-version.mjs) so browser
  detects new SW after each deployment
- SW: Auto skipWaiting() on install — broken UI can't show "Update" banner
- SW: Network-first for HTML navigation — always fresh HTML when online
- SW: Versioned cache names — old caches cleaned on activation
- SW: Removed silent 503 fallback — let JS errors propagate naturally
- SW: Proper offline fallback page for navigation requests
- usePWAUpdate: controllerchange listener auto-reloads when new SW activates
- +html.tsx: Inline recovery script forces immediate SW update check +
  fallback cache clear after 15s if app fails to mount
- _layout.tsx: Sets __appHealthy flag for recovery script coordination

Tags: pwa, service-worker, deployment, offline, recovery
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa
Semver: patch

commits.subject: Merge pull request #136 from devmade-ai/claude/fix-scroll-canvas-dimensions-dCaQE
commits.body: Fix canvas scaling to prevent overlap with bottom sheet on mobile
commits.title: Merge pull request #136 from devmade-ai/claude/fix-scroll-canvas-dimensions-dCaQE

commits.subject: fix(mobile): auto-scale canvas to fit above bottom sheet instead of scrolling
commits.body:
The previous scroll approach (overflow-y-auto + dynamic paddingBottom) only
worked on initial load because ResizeObserver picked up the padding change,
recalculated previewScale, and caused dimension flicker. The canvas also
changed dimensions at full snap, which would affect exports.

New approach: previewScale now subtracts the sheet's snap height (in px) from
the available container height on mobile, so the canvas auto-shrinks to fit
the visible area above the sheet. No scrolling needed, no dimension changes
that affect the export pipeline (exports already use platform.width/height
at scale(1), not previewScale).

Tags: mobile, canvas, layout, ux
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-ux
Semver: patch

commits.subject: feat(pwa): toast action buttons + one-tap update from check result
commits.body:
Add action button support to toasts so users can act directly from
the notification. Used for the "Check for updates" flow:

1. User taps "Check for updates" in burger menu
2. Toast: "Checking for updates…" (info)
3. If update found: toast "Update available!" with [Update now] button
4. Tapping [Update now] sends SKIP_WAITING to SW and reloads

New standalone applyUpdate() function in usePWAUpdate.ts — mirrors
updateApp() from the hook but works without the hook instance. Sends
SKIP_WAITING message to waiting SW and reloads on controllerchange.

Toast changes:
- showToast() accepts optional third arg: { label, onPress }
- ToastItem renders action button with semi-transparent background
- Action button dismisses the toast then runs the callback

commits.subject: fix(pwa): remove unconditional skipWaiting from SW install handler
commits.body:
The install event called self.skipWaiting() unconditionally, which
auto-activated every new service worker immediately on install. This
bypassed the prompt-based update flow — the user never saw the "New
version available" banner because the SW activated before the UI could
detect the waiting state.

Now the new SW stays in waiting state until the user clicks "Update"
in PWABanner, which sends the SKIP_WAITING message. This matches the
registerType: 'prompt' pattern from the glow-props PWA spec.

commits.subject: fix(audit): Phase 1 — quick wins from full audit sweep
commits.body:
Fix 6 issues found across rev, aud, and perf audits:

1. LocationSearch.tsx: Fix import typo — SEARCH_SEARCH_DEBOUNCE_MS → SEARCH_DEBOUNCE_MS
   (rev #1: would crash at runtime when user types in search)

2. useTheme.ts: Remove redundant isDark/colors deps from theme effect
   (rev #12, perf #1: effect fired twice per theme change)
   Compute meta color from resolved themeName instead of isDark/colors

3. FuelMapWeb.tsx: Narrow paint effect deps to colors.primary/primaryContent
   (perf #7: 6 GPU paint operations fired on any colors object change)

4. useFuelTypes.ts: Stabilize fuelTypes array reference via serialized ID key
   (perf #5: new array ref on every render caused FilterContext cascade
   re-renders across HeaderFilters, HomeScreen, StationList)

5. usePersistedState.ts: Remove non-null assertion on resolved value
   (aud #1: initialize to defaultValue instead of using resolved!)

6. useSearchModal.tsx: Add type annotation to useMemo for clarity
   (aud #17: memoization is correct — callbacks are stable useCallback refs)

commits.subject: fix(theme): make body background and focus rings reactive to theme changes
commits.body:
Replace hardcoded hex values in +html.tsx global styles with CSS
variables that Uniwind updates on theme change:

- body background: var(--color-base-200, <hex fallback>)
- input focus ring: var(--color-primary, <hex fallback>)
- button focus-visible ring: var(--color-primary, <hex fallback>)

Hex fallbacks still prevent white flash before Uniwind loads. Once
Uniwind sets the CSS variables (oklch values), the live theme colors
take over automatically — no JS needed for these updates.

Previously, body stayed dark when switching to light theme (visible
during overscroll), and focus rings used the wrong color on non-default
themes.

Tags: theme, pwa, css, accessibility

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-system
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(theme): make MapErrorBoundary reactive to theme changes
commits.body:
Extract fallback UI into a functional MapFallback component that uses
useTheme() hook. The class-based error boundary (required by React)
now renders this functional component, so fallback colors update
correctly when the user switches themes.

Previously, MapErrorBoundary used a static THEME_HEX lookup with
Appearance.getColorScheme() — it read colors once and never updated.

commits.subject: feat(theme): auto-generate hex colors from DaisyUI oklch definitions
commits.body:
Add build script (scripts/generate-theme-colors.mjs) that reads oklch
values from daisyui/theme/object.js and converts to hex via culori
(CSS Color Level 4 gamut mapping). Replaces manual oklch→hex conversion
that had incorrect values for out-of-sRGB-gamut themes (abyss was
significantly wrong — #6FE744 should have been #BEFF00).

Also adds runtime meta theme-color update in useTheme.ts so browser
chrome color changes when user switches themes.

- Build script reads from daisyui npm package (devDep)
- culori (devDep) for accurate CSS Color Level 4 gamut mapping
- npm run gen:theme-colors to regenerate after DaisyUI upgrades
- useTheme: updates both meta[name="theme-color"] tags on theme change
- Light themes use primary, dark themes use base200 for meta color

Tags: theme, pwa, build-script, colors

commits.subject: fix(pattern): fix pattern page stuck on Loading forever
commits.body:
Two bugs prevented pattern pages from loading:

1. escapeHtml/renderMarkdown variables were assigned at the bottom of the IIFE
   (lines 132-133) but render() was called at line 79. Due to var hoisting,
   escapeHtml was undefined when render() called it, throwing a TypeError that
   killed the entire script before the fetch even started.

2. The service worker's NavigationRoute served index.html for ALL navigations.
   Since precacheAndRoute doesn't match URLs with query params (?name=...),
   pattern.html and project.html navigations fell through to the SPA fallback,
   serving the wrong page on repeat visits.

Fix: Move variable assignments before first render() call, and disable
navigateFallback since this is a multi-page app (not an SPA).

Tags: bugfix, patterns, pwa, service-worker
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pattern-loading
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix: critical and high-priority audit fixes (C1-C5, H1-H10)
commits.body:
Critical fixes:
- C1: Remove unconditional dark class in main.jsx that overrode flash
  prevention script, causing light mode users to see dark flash
- C2: Validate ?data= URL param — restrict to http/https schemes only,
  preventing SSRF via file://, data://, or internal network URLs
- C3: Replace postMessage wildcard '*' with document.referrer origin;
  add height bounds check and source validation in embed.js
- C4: Route ErrorBoundary errors to debug pill via __debugPushError
  so section-level crashes are visible without DevTools
- C5: Individual try/catch per month file fetch with user-facing warning
  listing which months failed to load (was silent data loss)

High-priority fixes:
- H1: Add viewport-fit=cover and safe-area-inset padding for notch/home
  indicator on iOS devices
- H2: Increase filter mode toggle button padding from 2px to 6px with
  min-height 32px (was far below 44px touch target)
- H4: Add security headers to vercel.json (X-Content-Type-Options,
  X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy) with CORS exception for embed.js
- H5: Route SW registration errors to debug pill
- H6: Wrap Header, TabBar, FilterSidebar, DetailPane, SettingsPane in
  ErrorBoundary so crashes don't blank the entire app
- H7: Replace TESTING_GUIDE dark mode toggle + private mode sections
  (features that don't exist) with actual theme test scenarios
- H8: Update CLAUDE.md dark mode status from "Partial" to "Implemented"
- H9: Update USER_GUIDE.md theme section to reflect light/dark support
- H10: Add Projects tab to QuickGuide onboarding tour

commits.subject: feat(theme): add light theme, flash prevention, cross-tab sync, system preference
commits.body:
Items 17-20 from cross-project alignment:

Light theme CSS variables (item 17):
- Restructure :root as light theme default, html.dark as override
- Full light palette: backgrounds (#F8F9FA), text (#1a1a1a), borders (#e5e7eb),
  shadows, chart grid, and reduced glow intensities
- Backward compatible: html starts with class="dark" for existing users

Flash prevention (item 18):
- Inline classic <script> in <head> reads darkMode from localStorage
- Applies .dark class before any stylesheet or module loads
- Falls back to prefers-color-scheme on first visit
- try/catch for sandboxed environments

Cross-tab sync (item 19):
- Storage event listener in AppContext syncs darkMode across tabs
- Only fires in OTHER tabs (no infinite loop)
- Dispatches SET_DARK_MODE action to update React state

System preference fallback (item 20):
- matchMedia listener tracks OS dark mode changes
- Only applies when user hasn't manually set a preference
- Once user toggles, their choice persists (not overridden by OS)

1. **Extract shared markdown renderer** (high): Created public/markdown.js
   with renderMarkdown, inlineMarkdown, isSafeUrl, escapeHtml, copyMarkdown.
   Both project.html and pattern.html now use window.md.* instead of
   duplicating ~100 lines each. Saves ~10KB across both pages.

2. **Deduplicate theme arrays** (medium): head-common.html now declares
   lt/dt arrays once, reused for both random picker and validation.
   Previously identical arrays were declared twice 5 lines apart.

3. **Fix dev server path traversal** (medium): vite.config.js pattern
   middleware now uses path.basename() and validates .md extension
   to prevent /patterns/../../../etc/passwd escapes.

4. **Remove stale duplicate comment** (medium): project.html had two
   consecutive requirement comments for tab switching; removed the old one.

5. **Update .md-render CSS comment** (low): Now mentions both project.html
   and pattern.html, and references shared markdown.js.

6. **Explicit String(dark)** (low): theme.js applyTheme now stores
   String(dark) instead of relying on implicit boolean→string coercion.

Tags: refactor, cleanup, security, review
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(web): map rendering and bottom sheet scrolling on mobile web
commits.body:
Map: Created mapbox.web.ts platform file that exports MAPBOX_TOKEN
without importing @rnmapbox/maps (native-only SDK). The unconditional
native SDK import in mapbox.ts was breaking the module resolution on
web, preventing map initialization entirely.

Bottom sheet: Split into BottomSheetWeb (flex layout with native CSS
scroll) and BottomSheetNative (PanResponder + translateY with snap
points). PanResponder on the content area was intercepting all touch
events on mobile web, making both the filters and station list
completely unscrollable.

commits.subject: refactor(layout): square map with overlay bottom sheet
commits.body:
Map is now a fixed square (screenWidth × screenWidth) at the top of the
screen. Bottom sheet is absolutely positioned and slides up from below
the map to overlay it when expanded.

Previous: flex column split where the sheet couldn't overlay the map,
just pushed it up — not a real bottom sheet experience.

BottomSheet changes:
- Back to position:absolute + translateY (supports useNativeDriver:true)
- Accepts mapHeight prop to know where to start
- snapHalf: translateY = mapHeight (sheet sits below the map)
- snapFull: translateY = 56px (sheet covers map, map peeks at top)
- zIndex: 30 (sheets/drawers level per glow-props z-index scale)

Home screen changes:
- Map section: fixed height = screenWidth (square)
- Container: position:relative + overflow:hidden as stacking context
- Passes mapHeight to BottomSheet

Tags: layout, bottom-sheet, map, mobile-web

commits.subject: refactor(layout): split layout replaces full-screen map overlay
commits.body:
Previous: Map filled entire screen with absolute-positioned bottom sheet
overlay. On mobile web the map overflowed the viewport, the bottom sheet
was invisible, and there was no indication it existed.

Current: Flex column layout — map on top (flex:1), station list panel
on bottom (animated height). Panel is always visible with a drag handle
labeled "Stations". Two snap points: half (55%, default) and full (90%).

BottomSheet rewritten:
- Animates height instead of translateY — no absolute positioning
- Panel grows upward (map shrinks), panel shrinks (map grows)
- Two snap points instead of three (removed peek)
- Content PanResponder: drag down at scroll top collapses, drag up
  when not full expands, content scrolling never blocked
- Keyboard handling preserved (expand to full, restore on dismiss)

Home screen:
- Map section with flex:1 + position:relative contains the map,
  floating search bar, and error banners
- BottomSheet is a sibling below the map, not an overlay
- Removed all absolute positioning between map and sheet

     text-base-content/70 (helper) to match every other tab

     category headers, and helper text (better dark theme readability)

   being closed.

   backdrop-blur-sm stacking context) to MobileLayout (outside header).

   the menu reliably closes it.

commits.subject: fix(mobile-web): map overflow, bottom sheet visibility, PWA banner above header
commits.body:
Three structural fixes for mobile web:

1. Map overflowing viewport: FuelMapWeb div used flex:1 which doesn't
   constrain height on a plain <div> without explicit parent height.
   Changed to position:absolute with inset:0 so the map fills exactly
   its container. Added position:relative to FuelMap container and
   overflow:hidden to the home screen container.

2. Bottom sheet invisible: Added explicit top:0 to the sheet's absolute
   positioning (was relying on RN Web default). Combined with the
   container overflow:hidden, ensures the sheet stays within bounds.

3. PWA actions above header: Created PWABanner component that combines
   install prompt, update notification, and offline-ready status into
   a single dismissable banner above the header. Prioritizes: update >
   install > offline-ready. Replaces the separate UpdatePrompt and
   removes InstallPrompt from the bottom sheet.

commits.subject: fix(mobile-web): bottom sheet touch, burger menu position, PWA install visibility
commits.body:
Three mobile web fixes:

1. Bottom sheet inaccessible: Added touch-action:none to drag handle so
   mobile browsers don't intercept swipes as page scroll. Fixed content
   PanResponder that captured ALL vertical drags when sheet wasn't full —
   now only captures drag-down at scroll top (collapse) or drag-up when
   not full (expand), allowing FlatList scrolling at all sheet positions.

2. Burger menu on left: RN Web Modal doesn't reliably support position:fixed
   backdrop + position:absolute child on mobile browsers. Switched to flexbox
   alignment (alignItems:flex-end + marginRight) so menu anchors to the right
   edge regardless of Modal container width.

3. PWA install banner invisible: Was floating at bottom:190 over the map where
   it's easily missed or hidden behind the bottom sheet. Moved inside the
   bottom sheet content so it's visible when users interact with the station
   list. Increased auto-hide from 10s to 30s for slower mobile interactions.

commits.subject: refactor(ui): audit sweep — fixes, extractions, accessibility, docs
commits.body:
Full audit sweep across review, quality, mobile UX, clean, perf,
security, debug, and improve triggers. All findings addressed:

High priority:
- Fix: Update and Refresh had identical icons — Update now uses
  cloud-download (ICON_UPDATE), Refresh keeps circular arrows
- Fix: Nested scroll containers in burger menu (nav overflow-y-auto
  + theme list overflow-y-auto) — removed inner scroll, parent nav
  handles it via max-h-[calc(100dvh-4rem)]
- Fix: MobileNav tab buttons lacked guaranteed 44px touch targets —
  added min-h-11

Medium priority:
- BurgerMenu: Added ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Home/End keyboard navigation,
  matching ThemeSelector's pattern for consistent disclosure UX
- Extracted menu icon SVG paths to src/config/menuIcons.js — single
  source of truth, cleaner items array in MobileLayout
- Extracted ThemeList.jsx — shared component used by both
  MenuThemeSection (mobile) and ThemeSelector (desktop), eliminating
  duplicated theme list rendering
- Extracted HeaderButton component in DesktopLayout — replaced 7
  repeated className strings with a single component
- Added debugLog calls for burger menu open/close events

Low priority:
- Extracted useDisclosureFocus hook — deduplicates identical focus
  management pattern (hasBeenOpenRef + rAF + return-to-trigger) from
  BurgerMenu and ThemeSelector
- CLAUDE.md: Clarified canvas design themes (19 in themes.js) vs
  app UI themes (16 DaisyUI in daisyuiThemes.js), added new files
  to architecture section

commits.subject: fix(pwa): align icon generation with glow-props suggestions
commits.body:
Three gaps from glow-props Suggested Implementations → App Icons from SVG Source:
1. Sharp now rasterizes SVG at 400 DPI (~5.5x default 72 DPI) before downscaling,
   producing crisper edges especially at 192px.
2. Added shape-rendering="geometricPrecision" to icon.svg root element for
   accurate geometry during rasterization.
3. Added prefer_related_applications: false to PWA manifest so Chrome doesn't
   skip beforeinstallprompt when it thinks a native app exists.

Also wrapped generate-icons.mjs in async function with error handling per spec.

commits.subject: fix(theme): resolve 9 audit findings from full codebase review
commits.body:
Critical fixes:
- C1+C2: FuelMapWeb map layers now update on theme change via
  setPaintProperty() effect. Map init guarded with themeLoaded to
  prevent wrong colors before AsyncStorage hydrates.
- C3: All broken semantic color classes (text-error, bg-success/10,
  border-warning, etc.) replaced with inline styles. Uniwind doesn't
  generate utility classes for @variant-only CSS variables. Added
  withAlpha() utility for consistent hex opacity.

High priority:
- H1: Toast now uses active theme colors instead of hardcoded
  DEFAULT_COMBO — toasts match selected combo.
- H2-H4: Updated CLAUDE.md architecture, USER_GUIDE.md, and
  TESTING_GUIDE.md with theme combo documentation.
- H5: Wrapped Uniwind.setTheme() in try-catch with debug logging.

Medium:
- M1: Created withAlpha(hex, opacity) utility in themeColors.ts,
  replaced 4 fragile hex concatenation sites.
- M2: Runtime validation of hydrated combo from AsyncStorage —
  falls back to DEFAULT_COMBO if corrupted.
- M7: Theme swatch buttons now have minHeight: 44 (WCAG AA).
- M8: Theme changes logged to debug system.
- M10: renderEmptyState wrapped in useCallback.

on every page load/refresh. Off by default — users who want variety can

commits.subject: fix(menu): remove backdrop overlay and clean up dead code
commits.body:
The navbar's backdrop-filter creates a containing block that traps
position:fixed children — the backdrop only covered the navbar area.
Moving it outside would cover the menu (z-index stacking conflict).
Removed the backdrop element since click-outside uses a document
handler and scroll uses body overflow lock. Removed dead z-backdrop
CSS utility.

commits.subject: fix(menu): fix tap-outside close and scroll-through on mobile
commits.body:
Two bugs caused by the navbar's backdrop-filter creating a containing
block for position:fixed — the backdrop only covered the navbar area,
not the full viewport.

1. Tap outside menu didn't close: replaced backdrop click handler with
   a document-level click handler that checks if the target is outside
   the menu and trigger. Works regardless of backdrop positioning.

2. Scroll bled through to page: added body overflow lock on menu open,
   restored on close. overscroll-contain alone only prevents chaining
   on scroll containers — taps on non-scrollable menu areas (nav links,
   toggle button) still chained to the body.

commits.subject: fix(theme): code quality fixes and documentation for theme combo picker
commits.body:
Fixes 7 issues found during CLAUDE.md compliance review:

1. Touch targets: combo picker buttons now have min-h-11 (44px) matching
   all other burger menu items — was py-1.5 (~32px), violating the 44px
   minimum for mobile touch targets.

2. Hero gradient: switched from fixed amber/rose to DaisyUI from-primary
   to-accent tokens so the gradient auto-matches the active theme combo.
   Monochrome themes (lofi, black) degrade gracefully to solid text.

3. Cross-tab sync: added skipPersist flag to applyTheme() so the storage
   event handler doesn't redundantly write values back to localStorage.

4. Dead data: removed unused label and desc properties from COMBOS array
   in theme.js — HTML is the source of truth for display text.

5. SESSION_NOTES.md: rewritten with this session's work (was stale from
   CSS migration session).

6. HISTORY.md: added entries for theme combo picker, icon redesign, and
   code quality fixes.

7. README.md + TODO.md: updated tech stack description, added notes about
   combo map duplication and bundle size trade-off.

commits.subject: fix(nav): fix header vertical alignment and use DaisyUI navbar slots
commits.body:
Asymmetric vertical padding — padding-top was env(safe-area-inset-top)
(0 on non-notch devices) while padding-bottom inherited navbar default
0.5rem, shifting content upward. Fixed by using max(0.5rem, env(...))
for padding-top so non-notch devices get symmetric padding.

Also switched from flex-1/flex-none to navbar-start/navbar-end for
consistent centering via DaisyUI's built-in slot alignment.

commits.subject: fix(theme): add missing .dark class removal in flash-prevention scripts
commits.body:
The flash-prevention inline scripts set .dark class when dark mode is active
but didn't remove it in the light mode branch. If a user switched from dark
to light mode and reloaded, the .dark class could persist on <html>, causing
Tailwind dark: utilities to activate while DaisyUI components were in light
mode — a visual mismatch.

commits.subject: feat(css): migrate from Pico CSS to Tailwind v4 + DaisyUI v5
commits.body:
Replace Pico CSS v2.1.1 with Tailwind CSS v4.2.2 + DaisyUI v5.5.19 for full
styling control, animations, and customization. Pico's opinionated defaults
caused persistent spacing/wrapping issues and limited visual polish.

- Add Tailwind CSS v4, @tailwindcss/vite plugin, DaisyUI v5
- Create main.css with Tailwind directives, DaisyUI theme config, custom
  animations (scroll-triggered fade-in-up, card hover effects)
- Rewrite index.html with Tailwind utilities + DaisyUI components (navbar,
  card, badge, link), gradient hero text, sticky glassmorphism nav
- Rewrite project.html — meta grid, tabs, doc viewer, markdown renderer
- Update theme.js to set both .dark class (Tailwind) and data-theme (DaisyUI)
- Update flash-prevention scripts for dual class/attribute approach
- Add Intersection Observer for scroll-triggered animations
- Delete pico-theme.css and styles.css (420 lines of Pico overrides)
- Remove @picocss/pico dependency
- Move Tailwind/DaisyUI to devDependencies (build-time only)
- Update README and docs to reflect new tech stack

All accessibility preserved: disclosure pattern burger menu, aria-expanded,
focus-visible outlines, 44px touch targets, safe area insets, print styles.

Tags: css, tailwind, daisyui, migration, ui, animations

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(spacing): resolve 5 spacing issues across both pages
commits.body:
1. Hero top padding (index.html): Reduced from 3rem to 2rem total gap
   between nav and hero h1. Previous 2rem hero padding + 1rem Pico main
   padding was excessive, especially on mobile.

2. Section separation (index.html): Increased section margin-bottom from
   1rem to 2.5rem. Pico's default 1rem created weaker inter-section gaps
   than the 1.5rem intra-section subtitle gap, inverting visual hierarchy.

3. Vertical rhythm (project.html): Unified hero, meta-grid, and doc-tabs
   bottom margins to 1.5rem. Previous values (1rem, 2rem, 1.5rem) created
   inconsistent spacing between content blocks at the same hierarchy level.

4. Footer double spacing (both pages): Added last-child margin reset.
   Pico's body > footer padding-block (1rem) combined with p margin-bottom
   (1rem) doubled the bottom space.

5. Back link (project.html): Added .back-link class with smaller font size
   to visually de-emphasize navigation and differentiate from hero content.

Tags: ui, spacing, css, layout

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low

commits.subject: fix(nav): align header content with container edges
commits.body:
The nav had no horizontal padding, so the logo and burger button sat
flush at the viewport edges while <main class="container"> content was
inset by 1rem (mobile) or centered with max-width (desktop). This
created visual misalignment between header and content.

Added horizontal padding matching Pico's container padding
(--pico-spacing: 1rem), using max() to respect safe-area-insets on
notch devices.

Tags: ui, spacing, css
